title: fix(store): include devDependencies in bundled manifest
body:
## Summary
- include `devDependencies` in the bundled manifest subset stored in the package index
- keep bundled manifest type definitions in sync
- update normalization and package-requester tests to assert the cached manifest now includes `devDependencies`

## Why
When a package is already cached in the store, `readPackage` hooks receive `bundledManifest`. If `devDependencies` are omitted there, hooks like `@pnpm/plugin-types-fixer` become non-deterministic and may no-op for cached packages.

Fixes #10698

## Testing
- `pnpm --filter @pnpm/store.cafs run _test -- normalizeBundledManifest`
- `pnpm --filter @pnpm/package-requester run _test -- test/index.ts --testNamePattern="should skip store integrity check and resolve manifest if fetchRawManifest is true"`
author: dhruv7539

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR updates pnpm’s “bundled manifest” (the subset of `package.json` cached in the store index and reused for cached packages) to include `devDependencies`, so hooks like `readPackage` receive consistent manifests regardless of store state. **Changes:** - Include `devDependencies` in `normalizeBundledManifest`’s cached field subset. - Update the `BundledManifest` type to stay in sync with the stored subset. - Adjust CAFS normalization + package-requester tests to assert `devDependencies` are present in cached/bundled manifests. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | store/cafs/src/normalizeBundledManifest.ts | Adds `devDependencies` to the list of fields persisted into the bundled manifest subset. | | store/cafs/test/normalizeBundledManifest.test.ts | Updates normalization tests to reflect that `devDependencies` are now included/preserved. | | pkg-manager/package-requester/test/index.ts | Updates requester tests to assert bundled manifests now include `devDependencies` when served from cache. | | packages/types/src/package.ts | Extends `BundledManifest` type definition to include `devDependencies`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix: skip re-importing packages when global virtual store is warm
body:
## Problem

When `node_modules` is deleted but the global virtual store (`{storeDir}/links/`) remains intact, pnpm re-imports every package from the content-addressable store — even though the materialized package directories already exist globally.

The root cause: the skip logic in `lockfileToDepGraph.ts` depends on `currentLockfile` (from `node_modules/.pnpm/lock.yaml`). When `node_modules` is deleted, `currentLockfile` is `null`, `currentPackages = {}`, `depIsPresent` is always `false`, and both skip paths are unreachable. Every package falls through to `fetchPackage()`.

```
rm -rf node_modules
  → readCurrentLockfile() returns null
  → currentPackages = {}
  → depIsPresent = false for ALL packages
  → existing skip paths unreachable
  → every package re-fetched + re-imported (expensive)
```

With the standard (local) virtual store this is unavoidable — `dir` points inside `node_modules` which was deleted. But with the global virtual store, `dir` points to `{storeDir}/links/{hash}/...` — a path **outside** `node_modules` that **survives deletion**. The `pathExists(dir)` check would succeed if it were reached, but it never is.

## Solution

Add a reattach fast-path after the existing skip blocks, before the `fetchPackage` fallback:

```typescript
if (!fetchResponse && opts.enableGlobalVirtualStore && !isDirectoryDep
    && !opts.force) {
  if (dirExists ?? await pathExists(dir)) {
    fetchResponse = {}
  }
}
```

### Why this is safe

The global virtual store directory path **is** the integrity check. The `depGraphHash` in the path encodes:

- **Engine**: Node.js version + platform
- **Full transitive dep graph**: recursive hash of every dependency's `pkgIdWithPatchHash + resolution.integrity`

If any of these change, the hash changes, the path changes, and `pathExists` fails. Directory existence is a **constructive proof** of validity.

### Guard conditions

| Guard | Reason |
|-------|--------|
| `!fetchResponse` | Don't override if an existing skip already set it |
| `opts.enableGlobalVirtualStore` | Only in GVS mode (dir points to global path) |
| `!isDirectoryDep` | Exclude workspace `file:` protocol packages |
| `!opts.force` | `--force` should bypass all skip logic |

### What `fetchResponse = {}` does downstream

This is the same pattern used by the existing secondary skip (line 253):
```
fetchResponse = {}
  → graph[dir].fetching = undefined
  → linkAllPkgs: if (!depNode.fetching) return
  → importPackage() is never called
```

## Benchmark Results

Measured with `benchmarks/bench.sh` (5 runs, 1 warmup, fixture package with ~200 deps):

| # | Scenario | main | branch | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Headless (warm store+cache) | 3.643s ± 0.062s | 3.705s ± 0.019s | no change |
| 2 | Re-resolution (add dep, warm) | 6.525s ± 4.527s | 6.585s ± 4.498s | no change |
| 3 | Full resolution (warm, no lockfile) | 6.366s ± 0.085s | 6.317s ± 0.050s | no change |
| 4 | Headless (cold store+cache) | 8.368s ± 0.058s | 8.275s ± 0.059s | no change |
| 5 | Cold install (nothing warm) | 9.231s ± 0.138s | 9.219s+ | no change |
| **6** | **GVS warm reinstall** | **5.136s ± 0.073s** | **0.695s ± 0.009s** | **7.4x faster** |

**No regressions on any existing scenario. GVS warm reinstall goes from 5.1s to 695ms — a 7.4x speedup.**

## Related issues

- [#9696](https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/9696) — GVS improvements tracker
- [#10681](https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/10681) — Allow GVS in CI (directly benefits "pre-warmed cache" CI workflows)
- [#8190](https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/8190) — Original GVS implementation

## Test plan

- [x] New test: install with GVS → delete only `node_modules` (keep global store warm) → reinstall with `frozenLockfile: true` → verify `node_modules` recreated correctly
- [x] Spy on `fetchPackage` confirms 0 calls during warm reinstall (fast-path actually fires)
- [x] All 4 `globalVirtualStore` tests pass
- [x] Lint passes
- [x] Benchmark shows 7.4x speedup with zero regressions
author: vsumner

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR optimizes installs when using pnpm’s **global virtual store** by avoiding unnecessary package re-imports after `node_modules` is deleted but the global virtual store directories remain present (“warm”), and adds a regression test plus a changeset entry. **Changes:** - Add a “reattach” fast-path in `lockfileToDepGraph` to skip fetching/importing when the computed global virtual store package directory already exists. - Add an install test that deletes only `node_modules` and reinstalls with `frozenLockfile`. - Add a changeset documenting the patch release. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `deps/graph-builder/src/lockfileToDepGraph.ts` | Adds a new warm-global-store existence check to set `fetchResponse = {}` and avoid re-import. | | `pkg-manager/core/test/install/globalVirtualStore.ts` | Adds a test case for reinstalling after deleting only `node_modules` while keeping the global virtual store. | | `.changeset/global-virtual-store-reattach.md` | Declares patch releases and describes the behavior change. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: skip re-importing packages when global virtual store is warm

When node_modules is deleted but the global virtual store directories
survive, pnpm previously re-fetched every package because the skip
logic required currentLockfile to be present. Add a fast-path that
checks pathExists(dir) for GVS directories even when currentLockfile
is missing, since the GVS directory hash encodes engine, integrity,
and full dependency subgraph.

fix: remove includeUnchangedDeps guard from GVS fast-path

The includeUnchangedDeps flag is true whenever currentHoistPattern
differs from the desired hoistPattern. After deleting node_modules,
currentHoistPattern is always undefined (read from .modules.yaml),
so the flag is always true when hoisting is configured — defeating
the optimization in the exact scenario it targets.

The guard is unnecessary because the fast-path only skips fetch/import
(fetchResponse = {}), not graph inclusion. The package is still added
to the graph with children populated, so hoisting recalculation works.

perf: add GVS warm reinstall benchmark scenario

Adds benchmark 6: frozen lockfile reinstall with a warm global virtual
store after deleting node_modules. This measures the reattach fast-path
where all packages are skipped (no fetch/import) because their GVS
hash directories already exist.
fix: use proper types in fetchPackage spy to pass tsgo strict checks

title: feat: add `pnpm clean` command for safe `node_modules`/lockfile removal
body:
Adds a new `pnpm clean` command that safely removes `node_modules` contents from all workspace projects. Uses Node.js `fs.rm()` which correctly handles NTFS junctions on Windows without following them into their targets, preventing catastrophic data loss. Preserves non-pnpm hidden files (e.g. .cache) and lockfiles by default; use `--lockfile/-l` to also remove `pnpm-lock.yaml` files. Also cleans custom `virtual-store-dir` when configured inside the project root.

close #10707
close #6816
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds a new `pnpm clean` command that safely removes `node_modules` contents from all workspace projects to address catastrophic data loss issues on Windows caused by NTFS junctions when using standard deletion commands like `rm -rf` or `Remove-Item -Recurse`. The command uses `@zkochan/rimraf` which leverages Node.js's `fs.rm()` to correctly handle junctions without following them into their targets.

**Changes:**
- Adds `pnpm clean` command with `--lockfile` option to optionally remove `pnpm-lock.yaml` files
- Implements safe removal that preserves non-pnpm hidden files (e.g., `.cache`) while removing pnpm-specific entries (`.pnpm`, `.bin`, `.modules.yaml`) and regular packages
- Handles custom virtual-store-dir configurations when located inside the project root

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| pnpm/src/cmd/help.ts | Adds clean command to the advanced commands help section |
| pnpm/test/clean.ts | Comprehensive test suite covering single project, workspace, lockfile handling, and virtual store scenarios |
| pnpm/package.json | Adds is-subdir dependency for path validation |
| pnpm-lock.yaml | Updates lockfile with is-subdir dependency |

</details>

</details>

title: fix(npm-resolver): respect version constraints when falling back to workspace packages
body:
Fix regression introduced in 81f441cd3.

When link-workspace-packages=true, the fallback resolution paths (registry 404
and no matching registry version) pass update: Boolean(opts.update) to
tryResolveFromWorkspacePackages. On fresh installs without a lockfile entry,
opts.update is 'compatible' (truthy), which overrides the version spec to '*'
and matches any workspace package regardless of version.

Change both fallback call sites to pass update: false so version constraints
are always respected for non-workspace-protocol dependencies. The workspace:
protocol path returns before these blocks and correctly continues to use
opts.update.

Closes https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/10173
author: roysandrew

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes a regression in `@pnpm/npm-resolver` where, with `link-workspace-packages=true`, fallback-to-workspace resolution could incorrectly ignore the requested version range during fresh installs (when `opts.update` is truthy), causing mismatched workspace packages to be linked. **Changes:** - Pass `update: false` to `tryResolveFromWorkspacePackages` in both non-workspace fallback paths so workspace fallback always respects the requested version constraint. - Add regression tests covering 404 and no-matching-registry-version scenarios to ensure mismatched workspace versions are not linked. - Add a changeset for the patch release. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `resolving/npm-resolver/src/index.ts` | Ensures fallback-to-workspace resolution does not override dependency version constraints via `update`. | | `resolving/npm-resolver/test/index.ts` | Adds tests that fail if a mismatching workspace version is incorrectly linked during fallback resolution. | | `.changeset/legal-ways-accept.md` | Declares a patch release and documents the fix for `link-workspace-packages=true`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix(npm-resolver): respect version constraints when falling back to workspace packages

When link-workspace-packages=true, the fallback resolution paths (registry 404
and no matching registry version) pass update: Boolean(opts.update) to
tryResolveFromWorkspacePackages. On fresh installs without a lockfile entry,
opts.update is 'compatible' (truthy), which overrides the version spec to '*'
and matches any workspace package regardless of version.

Change both fallback call sites to pass update: false so version constraints
are always respected for non-workspace-protocol dependencies. The workspace:
protocol path returns before these blocks and correctly continues to use
opts.update.

Closes #10173
test: clarify npm-resolver test names for workspace version mismatch scenarios

title: feat: isolated global packages
body:
**TLDR:** Global packages in pnpm v10 are annoying and slow because they all are installed to a single global package. Instead, we will now use a system that is similar to the one used by "pnpm dlx" (aka "pnpx").

Each globally installed package (or group of packages installed together) now gets its own isolated installation directory with its own `package.json`, `node_modules`, and lockfile. This prevents global packages from interfering with each other through peer dependency conflicts or version resolution shifts.

## Changes

- Add `@pnpm/global-packages` shared utilities package for scanning, hashing, and managing isolated global installs
- `pnpm add -g` creates isolated installs in `{pnpmHomeDir}/global/v11/{hash}/`
- `pnpm remove -g` removes the entire installation group containing the package
- `pnpm update -g` re-installs into new isolated directories and swaps symlinks
- `pnpm list -g` scans isolated directories to show installed global packages
- `pnpm outdated -g` checks each isolated installation for outdated dependencies
- `pnpm store prune` cleans up orphaned global installation directories

## Breaking changes

- `pnpm install -g` (no args) is no longer supported — use `pnpm add -g <pkg>`
- `pnpm link <pkg-name>` no longer resolves packages from the global store — only relative or absolute paths are accepted
- `pnpm link --global` is removed — use `pnpm add -g .` to register a local package's bins globally
author: zkochan

This PR introduces an *isolated* global package installation layout, where each global install group lives in its own directory (with its own `package.json`, lockfile, and `node_modules`) to prevent cross-package dependency/peer resolution interference.

- Adds new `@pnpm/global-packages` workspace package to manage scanning/cleanup, hashing, and install-dir handling for global installs.
- Updates global-related commands (`add -g`, `remove -g`, `update -g`, `list -g`, `outdated -g`, `store prune`) to work with isolated global install directories.
- Updates tests and changesets to reflect new global layout and breaking CLI behavior (notably `pnpm link` and `pnpm install -g`).

feat: isolated global packages

Each globally installed package (or group of packages installed together)
now gets its own isolated installation directory with its own package.json,
node_modules, and lockfile. This prevents global packages from interfering
with each other through peer dependency conflicts or version resolution shifts.

- Add @pnpm/global-packages shared utilities package
- Extract createCacheKey from dlx to shared package
- pnpm add -g creates isolated installs in {pnpmHomeDir}/global/{hash}/
- pnpm remove -g removes entire installation group containing the package

- pnpm list -g scans isolated directories

title: fix: run @pnpm/exe setup script in-process to fix version switching without Node.js
body:
When pnpm is installed as a standalone executable in environments without a system Node.js (e.g. Docker containers), the `@pnpm/exe` preinstall script (`node setup.js`) fails because `node` is not on PATH. This broke version switching via the `packageManager` field in package.json since v10.30.2, which changed `getCurrentPackageName()` to return `@pnpm/exe` instead of platform-specific package names like `@pnpm/linux-x64`.

Install with `--ignore-scripts` and run the setup script in-process using `createRequire`, which uses the Node.js runtime already bundled in the standalone binary.

Closes #10687
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes version switching via the `packageManager` field in package.json when pnpm is installed as a standalone executable in environments without a system Node.js installation (e.g., Docker containers). The issue (#10687) was introduced in v10.30.2 when `getCurrentPackageName()` started returning `@pnpm/exe` for standalone executables, which caused the package's preinstall script (`node setup.js`) to fail because `node` wasn't available on PATH. **Changes:** - Replace `--allow-build=@pnpm/exe` with `--ignore-scripts` to skip all install scripts - Add `--config.strict-dep-builds=false` to prevent errors when scripts are ignored - Implement `linkExePlatformBinary()` function to replicate the setup.js logic in-process without requiring external Node.js - Update changeset documenting the fix ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | tools/plugin-commands-self-updater/src/installPnpmToTools.ts | Adds in-process platform binary linking logic and modifies pnpm add command to use --ignore-scripts instead of allowing specific build scripts | | .changeset/fix-exe-version-switch-no-nodejs.md | Changeset documenting the bug fix for version switching without Node.js | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: run @pnpm/exe setup logic in-process to fix version switching without Node.js

When pnpm is installed as a standalone executable in environments without
a system Node.js (e.g. Docker containers), the `@pnpm/exe` preinstall
script (`node setup.js`) fails because `node` is not on PATH. This broke
version switching via the `packageManager` field in package.json since
v10.30.2, which changed `getCurrentPackageName()` to return `@pnpm/exe`
instead of platform-specific package names like `@pnpm/linux-x64`.

Install with `--ignore-scripts` and link the platform-specific binary
in-process instead. The setup logic is inlined because setup.js can't
be loaded at runtime: `require()` fails on ESM (pnpm v11+) and
`import()` is intercepted by pkg's virtual filesystem in standalone
executables.

Closes #10687

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: feat: use global virtual store for global packages and dlx
body:
## Summary

Global installs (`pnpm add -g`) and `pnpm dlx` now use the global virtual store by default. Instead of creating a `.pnpm` directory inside each install location, packages are stored in the content-addressable store and hard-linked from `{storeDir}/links`. This makes global installs and `dlx` faster when packages share common dependencies, as they are deduplicated through the store.

## Changes

- **config**: Enable `enableGlobalVirtualStore` by default for global installs. When disabled, the standard `node_modules/.pnpm` layout is used instead of hardcoding `.pnpm`.
- **dlx**: Pass `enableGlobalVirtualStore` through to the `add` handler so `dlx` also benefits from the virtual store.
- **tests**: Explicitly set `enableGlobalVirtualStore: false` in dlx tests that rely on the non-virtual-store layout.

## Opt out

Run `pnpm config set enableGlobalVirtualStore false` to use the previous behavior. This is also automatically disabled in CI environments, where there is no warm cache to benefit from.
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR introduces support for using a global virtual store for global package installations (`pnpm install -g`) and `pnpm dlx` commands. Previously, these operations created `.pnpm` directories in individual project locations. With this change, packages are now stored centrally at `{storeDir}/links` by default, improving performance and reducing disk space usage across multiple dlx invocations and global installs. **Changes:** - Global installs and dlx now default to using the global virtual store (stored at `{storeDir}/links`) - Added `enableGlobalVirtualStore` configuration option that can be set to false to revert to the old behavior - CI environments automatically disable the global virtual store since there's no benefit from a warm cache ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | config/config/src/index.ts | Implements logic to set `enableGlobalVirtualStore` to true by default for global installs, and conditionally sets `virtualStoreDir` to `.pnpm` when the global virtual store is disabled | | exec/plugin-commands-script-runners/src/dlx.ts | Updates dlx handler to default `enableGlobalVirtualStore` to true when not explicitly set | | exec/plugin-commands-script-runners/test/dlx.e2e.ts | Updates tests that verify build script behavior to explicitly disable the global virtual store, as they check for files in specific `.pnpm` paths | | .changeset/global-install-virtual-store.md | Documents the feature change for the changelog | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

commits.message: feat: use global virtual store for global packages and dlx
Merge branch 'main' into global-install

fix(config): remove unnecessary virtualStoreDir override for global installs

When the global virtual store is disabled, the default `node_modules/.pnpm`
path works fine — no need to explicitly override it to `.pnpm`.

Running `pnpm dlx` with no arguments would crash Node.js with a TypeError as it attempted to call `.indexOf()` on an undefined variable. This PR adds a guard clause and throws a descriptive DLX_NO_PKG error and exits gracefully.

Fixes #10633

This commit adds a guard clause and displays the help message instead
and exits gracefully.

Fixes #10633

title: fix(link-bins): hardlink node.exe on Windows instead of creating a cmd-shim
body:
Third-party cmd shims (e.g., npm's rimraf.cmd) call node.exe from within IF/ELSE blocks in batch files. When node resolves to node.cmd instead of node.exe, Windows batch file chaining breaks with "The system cannot find the path specified."

On Windows, hardlink node.exe directly into the bin directory. On non-Windows, symlink the node binary directly.
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR addresses a Windows-specific issue where third-party cmd shims fail when node is wrapped in a cmd-shim instead of being a direct executable. The fix changes the linking strategy for the node binary to use hardlinks on Windows and symlinks on non-Windows systems, bypassing the cmd-shim wrapper. **Changes:** - Adds special handling for node.exe on Windows to create a hardlink instead of a cmd-shim wrapper - Adds special handling for node on non-Windows systems to create a direct symlink instead of a shell shim --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix(link-bins): stop prepending redundant paths to NODE_PATH in command shims
body: Fixed "input line too long" error on Windows when running lifecycle scripts with the global virtual store enabled. The `NODE_PATH` in command shims no longer includes all paths from `Module._nodeModulePaths()`. Instead, it includes only the package's bundled dependencies directory (e.g., `.pnpm/pkg@version/node_modules/pkg/node_modules`), the package's sibling dependencies directory (e.g., `.pnpm/pkg@version/node_modules`), and the hoisted `node_modules` directory. These paths are needed so that tools like `import-local` (used by jest, eslint, etc.) which resolve from CWD can find the correct dependency versions.
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes the "input line too long" error on Windows when running lifecycle scripts with the global virtual store enabled. The issue occurs because cmd.exe has an 8191-character line limit, and the previous implementation populated NODE_PATH with redundant paths from `Module._nodeModulePaths()`. **Changes:** - Removed redundant NODE_PATH construction logic that included paths Node.js already searches during standard require() resolution - Simplified to only include `extraNodePaths` (the hoisted node_modules directory), which is the only path standard resolution cannot find - Removed the now-unused `getBinNodePaths` helper function and `Module` import ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg-manager/link-bins/src/index.ts | Removed redundant NODE_PATH construction logic, deleted `getBinNodePaths` function, and removed unused `Module` import | | .changeset/fix-windows-node-path-too-long.md | Added changeset documenting the fix for Windows command line length limit issue | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix(link-bins): stop prepending redundant paths to NODE_PATH in command shims

The NODE_PATH in cmd shims previously included all paths from
Module._nodeModulePaths(), which are already searched by Node.js
during standard require() resolution. With the global virtual store,
binaries resolve to deeply nested store paths, causing
Module._nodeModulePaths() to generate many intermediate paths that
exceed cmd.exe's 8191-character line limit on Windows.

Now only extraNodePaths (the hoisted node_modules directory) is
included — the one path that standard resolution can't find on its own.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(link-bins): include package's own node_modules in NODE_PATH for command shims

The previous commit removed all Module._nodeModulePaths() from NODE_PATH
to fix Windows "input line too long" errors. However, this broke tools like
import-local (used by jest, eslint, etc.) that resolve from CWD rather than
from the binary's location. When resolving transitive dependencies from CWD,
NODE_PATH was the only resolution path available.

Now the shim includes just the package's own dependency directories:
- .pnpm/pkg@version/node_modules/pkg/node_modules (bundled dependencies)
- .pnpm/pkg@version/node_modules (sibling dependencies)

This keeps NODE_PATH short (2-3 entries vs dozens) while ensuring correct
version resolution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(link-bins): extract getBinNodePaths, fix scoped packages and priority

Extract getBinNodePaths into its own module for testability. Fix the
bundled dependencies path for scoped packages (@scope/pkg) and correct
the NODE_PATH priority so package-specific paths come before hoisted
paths. Add unit tests covering virtual store layout, scoped packages,
symlink resolution, and ENOENT fallback.

fix(link-bins): address review feedback for getBinNodePaths

Use path.dirname() walk with path.relative() instead of split/join to
correctly handle Windows UNC and extended-length paths. Add 'junction'
type to symlink test for Windows compatibility. Update changeset to
accurately describe all three NODE_PATH entries.

title: refactor(env): `pnpm env` use now delegates to `pnpm add --global`
body:
This PR overhauls `pnpm env` use to route through pnpm's own install machinery instead of maintaining a parallel code path with manual symlink/shim/hardlink logic.

```
pnpm env use -g <version>
```

now runs:

```
pnpm add --global node@runtime:<version>
```

via `@pnpm/exec.pnpm-cli-runner`. All manual symlink, hardlink, and cmd-shim code in `envUse.ts` is gone (~1000 lines removed across the package).

### Changes

**npm and npx shims on all platforms**

Added `getNodeBinsForCurrentOS(platform)` to `@pnpm/constants`, returning a `Record<string, string>` with the correct relative paths for `node`, `npm`, and `npx` inside a Node.js distribution. `BinaryResolution.bin` is widened from `string` to `string | Record<string, string>` in `@pnpm/resolver-base` and `@pnpm/lockfile.types`, so the node resolver can set all three entries and pnpm's bin-linker creates shims for each automatically.

**Windows npm/npx fix**

`addFilesFromDir` was skipping root-level `node_modules/` (to avoid storing a package's own dependencies), which stripped the bundled `npm` from Node.js Windows zip archives. Added an `includeNodeModules` option and enabled it from the binary fetcher so Windows distributions keep their full contents.

**Removed subcommands**

`pnpm env add` and `pnpm env remove` are removed. `pnpm env use` handles both installing and activating a version. `pnpm env list` now always lists remote versions (the `--remote` flag is no longer required, though it is kept for backwards compatibility).

**musl support**

On Alpine Linux and other musl-based systems, the musl variant of Node.js is automatically downloaded from [unofficial-builds.nodejs.org](https://unofficial-builds.nodejs.org).
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request refactors the `pnpm env use` command to install Node.js through pnpm's own package installation machinery instead of using a custom download and extraction mechanism. This change unifies the implementation and enables automatic cleanup of unused Node.js versions through `pnpm store prune`.

**Changes:**
- Added `includeNodeModules` option to `addFilesFromDir` to preserve bundled node_modules in Node.js Windows distributions
- Refactored `pnpm env use` to install Node.js as a package in a synthetic project at `{pnpmHomeDir}/env/`
- Removed `pnpm env add` and `pnpm env remove` commands
- Changed `pnpm env list` to always list remote versions only

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 20 out of 21 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| worker/src/types.ts | Added includeNodeModules field to AddDirToStoreMessage interface |
| worker/src/start.ts | Threaded includeNodeModules parameter through addFilesFromDir function |
| worker/src/index.ts | Updated AddFilesFromDirOptions type to include includeNodeModules |
| store/cafs/src/index.ts | Added includeNodeModules parameter to CafsFunctions.addFilesFromDir signature |
| store/cafs/src/addFilesFromDir.ts | Implemented includeNodeModules logic to optionally preserve root-level node_modules |
| fetching/binary-fetcher/src/index.ts | Set includeNodeModules: true for zip archives to preserve Node.js bundled npm |
| env/plugin-commands-env/src/node.ts | Removed old Node.js download functions, extended type to support add.handler fields |
| env/plugin-commands-env/src/envUse.ts | Completely rewritten to use pnpm's add.handler for Node.js installation |
| env/plugin-commands-env/src/envList.ts | Simplified to only list remote versions |
| env/plugin-commands-env/src/envRemove.ts | File deleted - env remove functionality removed |
| env/plugin-commands

commits.message:
refactor(env): route env use through pnpm install machinery

- `pnpm env use` now installs Node.js via `add.handler` into a
  synthetic project at `{pnpmHomeDir}/env/`, storing Node in the
  global virtual store so `pnpm store prune` handles cleanup
- Version specifiers (lts, codenames, ranges) are pre-resolved to an
  exact version before passing to `add.handler` as `node@runtime:{v}`
- Remove `env add` and `env remove` subcommands
- `env list` is now remote-only (no `--remote` flag required)
- Remove `--platform`, `--arch`, `--libc` CLI flags; musl is
  auto-detected internally via `detect-libc`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(env): prevent workspace virtual store from leaking into env installs

When a user runs `pnpm env use --global` from inside a pnpm workspace
that has `enableGlobalVirtualStore: true` (e.g. the pnpm monorepo
itself), the workspace config leaked into the `add.handler` call.
With `enableGlobalVirtualStore: true`, the `globalVirtualStoreDir`
stays as the relative `.pnpm` string, which then gets resolved against
`process.cwd()` during package import operations — causing Node.js to
be written into the workspace's `.pnpm/` directory instead of the
pnpm store.

Fix by explicitly overriding `global: false` and
`enableGlobalVirtualStore: false` after the `...opts` spread so the
env synthetic project always uses an isolated, store-absolute virtual
store path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
chore: update tsconfig.json

chore(env): remove dead code left over from use-node-version removal

`getNodeBinDir`, `getNodeDir`, `getNodeVersionsBaseDir`, and
`readNodeVersionsManifest` became unreachable after
`prepareExecutionEnv` was deleted in the v11 use-node-version removal
(71de2b3f2). Remove them along with the test file and the deps they
pulled in: @pnpm/node.fetcher, @pnpm/store-path, @pnpm/remove-bins,
@pnpm/env.system-node-version, @pnpm/types, load-json-file, semver.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: provide cacheDir in tests via tempy; add cacheDir to NvmNodeCommandOptions

add.handler (called by envUse) requires cacheDir. In production opts always
contain cacheDir from the full Config; tests now supply it explicitly via
temporaryDirectory() from tempy. Also adds cacheDir as an optional pick in
NvmNodeCommandOptions so it flows through the ...opts spread when present.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: preserve bundled node_modules from Node.js Windows zip for npm/npx shims

On Windows, the Node.js distribution places npm inside a root-level
node_modules/ directory of the zip archive. addFilesFromDir was skipping
root-level node_modules (to avoid storing a package's own npm dependencies),
which caused the bundled npm to be absent from the installed node package.
This prevented envUse from creating npm and npx shims on Windows.

Add an includeNodeModules option to addFilesFromDir and pass it as true
from the binary fetcher so binary zip archives preserve their full content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(env): hide internal dirs in pnpm home; remove dead nodejs_current symlink

- Rename env/ to .env/ and nodejs_current to .nodejs_current so internal
  directories don't appear alongside user binaries in pnpm home
- Remove the nodejs_current symlink entirely: getNodeExecPathAndTargetDir
  was the only consumer and was never called, making both dead code
- Remove --remote from env list usage examples and help (the command always
  lists remote versions; keep the flag in cliOptionsTypes for compatibility)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(env): delegate env use to pnpm add -g via CLI runner; auto-shim npm/npx

- `pnpm env use -g <spec>` now runs `pnpm add --global node@runtime:<spec>`
  via @pnpm/exec.pnpm-cli-runner instead of calling add.handler directly.
  All manual symlink/hardlink/cmd-shim code is gone.

- Added `getNodeBinsForCurrentOS(platform)` to @pnpm/constants returning
  `{ node, npm, npx }` with the correct relative paths inside the Node.js
  package for each OS.

- `BinaryResolution.bin` widened from `string` to `string | Record<string, string>`
  in @pnpm/resolver-base and @pnpm/lockfile.types.

- The node resolver now uses `getNodeBinsForCurrentOS(platform)` for
  `resolution.bin` on each platform variant and for `manifest.bin` on the
  resolved package, so pnpm's bin-linker creates node/npm/npx shims
  automatically without any post-install steps in envUse.

- Removed downloadNodeVersion.ts (dead code; resolution is handled entirely
  by the resolver) and all deps that envUse no longer needs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
chore: update tsconfig.json

fix(env): mock runPnpmCli in tests; fix global bin dir PATH check

Replace integration tests (that spawned real pnpm subprocesses and
downloaded Node.js) with unit tests that mock @pnpm/exec.pnpm-cli-runner.
Tests now verify that envUse constructs the correct pnpm add arguments
without any network access or subprocess execution.

Also reverts the PATH-injection workaround from envUse/runPnpmCli that
was added to satisfy pnpm's "global bin dir not in PATH" check in CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
test: fix

fix: update env/plugin-commands-env/src/envUse.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
refactor: env use
docs: update changesets
fix: compile
fix: compile
test: fix

## Pull request overview

This PR adds cross-platform Node.js download support to `pnpm env` commands and enables automatic detection of musl-based Linux systems (like Alpine Linux). The implementation allows users to download Node.js binaries for different platforms, architectures, and C library variants, storing them in platform-specific subdirectories to avoid collisions. Additionally, a `--json` flag is added to `pnpm env add` for programmatic consumption of installation results.

**Changes:**
- Added `--platform`, `--arch`, and `--libc` CLI options for cross-platform Node.js downloads
- Implemented automatic musl detection and switching to unofficial-builds mirror for musl systems

title: feat: replace pkg with Node.js SEA for standalone executables
body:
## Summary

- Replace `@yao-pkg/pkg` with Node.js native Single Executable Applications
  (`node --build-sea`, Node.js 25.5+) for building standalone pnpm binaries
- The SEA binary embeds only the CJS bootstrap (`pnpm.cjs`); the main bundle
  and all assets live in a `dist/` directory shipped alongside the binary
- `dist/` is shipped once in `@pnpm/exe` rather than duplicated across each
  platform package (`@pnpm/linux-x64`, `@pnpm/macos-arm64`, etc.)
- `@reflink/reflink` is externalized in esbuild and resolved at runtime from
  `dist/node_modules/` (JS pnpm) or via npm-installed optional deps (`@pnpm/exe`)
- SEA detection uses `require('node:sea').isSea()` instead of `process.pkg`
- Remove pkg config files, pkg catalog entry, and pkg patch

## Test plan

- [ ] CI builds SEA binaries for all 8 targets (linux-x64, linux-arm64,
      linuxstatic-x64, linuxstatic-arm64, macos-x64, macos-arm64, win-x64,
      win-arm64)
- [ ] `pnpm --version` works from the built binary without SEA warnings
- [ ] `pnpm install` works (verifies worker threads, node-gyp, reflink)
- [ ] `npm install -g @pnpm/exe` installs correctly and resolves reflink
      via optional dependencies
- [ ] GitHub release tarballs contain binary + dist/ with only the target
      platform's reflink package
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request replaces the `@yao-pkg/pkg` build tool with Node.js native Single Executable Applications (SEA) for creating standalone pnpm binaries. The change modernizes the build pipeline by leveraging Node.js 25.6+'s built-in `--build-sea` functionality, eliminating the need for the third-party pkg tool.

**Changes:**
- Replaced pkg-based build system with Node.js SEA using `node --build-sea` command
- Externalized `@reflink/reflink` dependency to be resolved at runtime instead of being bundled
- Centralized dist/ assets in `@pnpm/exe` package instead of duplicating across platform-specific packages
- Updated SEA detection logic to use `require('node:sea').isSea()` instead of `process.pkg`

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 37 out of 38 changed files in this pull request and generated 10 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| pnpm/artifacts/exe/scripts/build-artifacts.ts | New SEA build script that downloads Node.js binaries and creates platform-specific executables |
| pnpm/artifacts/exe/scripts/reflink-utils.ts | New utility functions for managing reflink platform packages |
| pnpm/artifacts/exe/scripts/reflink-utils.test.ts | Test coverage for reflink utilities |
| __utils__/scripts/src/copy-artifacts.ts | Updated to create tarballs with binary + dist/ directory for each platform |
| cli/cli-meta/src/index.ts | Updated SEA detection logic and getCurrentPackageName to use node:sea module |
| cli/cli-meta/test/getCurrentPackageName.test.ts | Updated tests to cover new SEA detection approach |
| pnpm/bundle.ts | Externalized `@reflink/reflink` from esbuild bundles |
| pnpm/bundle-deps.ts | Added --force flag to install all reflink platform packages |
| pnpm/package.json | Added `@reflink/reflink` as a dependency |

Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **tools/plugin-commands-self-updater/src/installPnpmToTools.ts:24** * `installPnpmToTools()` now always installs `${getCurrentPackageName()}@${pnpmVersion}`. When running from a SEA binary, `getCurrentPackageName()` resolves to `@pnpm/exe`, which will break `pnpm self-update ` (e.g. `pnpm self-update 9`) because `@pnpm/exe@9.x` won’t exist. The package to install should be derived from the *target* `pnpmVersion` (e.g. legacy platform package vs `@pnpm/exe` vs `pnpm`) rather than from the currently running binary; reintroduce version-based selection logic (and dependencies like `semver`) to preserve downgrade/old-version support.

commits.message:
feat: switch from pkg to Node.js SEA for creating standalone executables

Replace @yao-pkg/pkg with Node.js native Single Executable Applications
(--build-sea, Node.js 25.5+). The SEA binary embeds only pnpm.cjs (CJS
bootstrap), while pnpm.mjs and all assets live in a dist/ directory
shipped alongside the binary in platform-specific tarballs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: move dist/ from platform packages to @pnpm/exe

The dist/ directory (pnpm.mjs, worker.js, templates, etc.) is identical
across all platforms, so ship it once in @pnpm/exe instead of duplicating
it in each platform package. Platform packages now only contain the
binary. The self-updater installs @pnpm/exe (not the platform package)
so it gets both dist/ and the binary via optionalDependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: externalize @reflink/reflink in esbuild bundle

Make @reflink/reflink external in both the main and worker esbuild
bundles so the require() calls resolve at runtime from dist/node_modules
instead of being inlined. Add @reflink/reflink as a production dependency
of both pnpm (bundled into dist/node_modules by bundle-deps.ts) and
@pnpm/exe (installed by npm alongside the binary).

For GitHub release tarballs, only the target platform's reflink package
is kept. For @pnpm/exe npm publishing, all reflink platform packages
are stripped from dist/ since npm installs the right one automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
chore: update cspell list

test: update system-node-version tests for SEA detection

Mock @pnpm/cli-meta's detectIfCurrentPkgIsExecutable instead of
setting process.pkg, which is no longer used for SEA detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: improve cli-meta test coverage for SEA migration

Add tests for detectIfCurrentPkgIsExecutable() (non-SEA path) and
isExecutedByCorepack() which were previously untested. The SEA=true
path of detectIfCurrentPkgIsExecutable() cannot be unit tested since
node:sea is unavailable in an ESM test environment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: move GitHub tarball assembly to copy-artifacts.ts

build-artifacts.ts (prepublishOnly of @pnpm/exe) now only builds the
SEA executables and prepares the exe npm dist/. The per-target dist/
assembly for GitHub release tarballs moves to copy-artifacts.ts, which
is the natural owner of that concern.

Other changes:
- Extract getReflinkKeepPackages/stripReflinkPackages to reflink-utils.ts
  with tests using node:test
- Move --force from top-level pnpm install in release.yml to the pnpm
  deploy in bundle-deps.ts, where it is actually needed to install all
  @reflink/reflink-* platform packages into dist/node_modules
- Change @pnpm/exe prepublishOnly to run pnpm's full prepublishOnly
  (compile + bundle-deps) so dist/node_modules is populated before
  build-artifacts.ts and copy-artifacts.ts read from pnpm/dist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: copy dist/ alongside binary when running pnpm setup for SEA

When the pnpm CLI is a Node.js SEA binary, it requires a dist/ directory
adjacent to the executable at runtime (containing pnpm.mjs and bundled
node_modules). The copyCli function in plugin-commands-setup now copies
dist/ from alongside the current binary into the tools directory so that
the installed pnpm works correctly after `pnpm setup`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: avoid argument list too long when creating Windows zip archives

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: propagate errors in copy-artifacts script

Previously errors in createArtifactTarball were swallowed, causing the
script to exit 0 even when artifact creation failed. Now errors are
re-thrown with a descriptive message, and the top-level IIFE has a
.catch() handler that sets a non-zero exit code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: remove reflink-utils.ts from @pnpm/exe

The stripReflinkPackages call in build-artifacts.ts stripped all platform
packages while keeping @reflink/reflink. Instead, just remove the entire
@reflink directory from dist/ — @pnpm/exe already declares @reflink/reflink
as a runtime dependency, so npm installs it (along with the right platform
package via optionalDependencies) automatically.

This eliminates reflink-utils.ts, its tests, and the code duplication with
copy-artifacts.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: explain why require() works in ESM for node:sea detection

title: ci: run all tests if pnpm-workspace.yaml changes

title: fix: respect peer dep range in hoistPeers when preferred versions exist
body:
Previously, hoistPeers used semver.maxSatisfying(versions, '*') which picked the highest preferred version from the lockfile regardless of the peer dep range. This caused overrides that narrow a peer dep range to be ignored when a stale version existed in the lockfile.

Now hoistPeers first tries semver.maxSatisfying(versions, range) to find a preferred version that satisfies the actual peer dep range. If none satisfies it and autoInstallPeers is enabled, it falls back to the range itself so pnpm resolves a matching version from the registry.
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes `hoistPeers` to ensure auto-installed peer dependencies respect the *actual* peer dependency range (including narrowed ranges coming from overrides), rather than always selecting the highest preferred version from the lockfile. **Changes:** - Update `hoistPeers` to prefer the highest preferred version that satisfies the peer range, falling back to the range itself when auto-installing peers. - Add unit tests covering preferred-version selection vs. overridden/narrowed peer ranges. - Add an integration test to ensure overrides narrow auto-installed peer versions across subsequent installs, plus a changeset entry. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg-manager/resolve-dependencies/src/hoistPeers.ts | Changes preferred-version selection to respect the peer dependency range and adds an auto-install fallback. | | pkg-manager/resolve-dependencies/test/hoistPeers.test.ts | Adds unit tests for the new range-aware preferred-version behavior. | | pkg-manager/core/test/install/autoInstallPeers.ts | Adds an integration test ensuring overrides are respected even when a stale preferred version exists in the lockfile. | | .changeset/fix-hoist-peers-override.md | Records the patch-level change for `@pnpm/resolve-dependencies`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: respect peer dep range in hoistPeers when preferred versions exist

Previously, hoistPeers used semver.maxSatisfying(versions, '*') which
picked the highest preferred version from the lockfile regardless of the
peer dep range. This caused overrides that narrow a peer dep range to be
ignored when a stale version existed in the lockfile.

Now hoistPeers first tries semver.maxSatisfying(versions, range) to find
a preferred version that satisfies the actual peer dep range. If none
satisfies it and autoInstallPeers is enabled, it falls back to the range
itself so pnpm resolves a matching version from the registry.

fix: only fall back to exact-version range for overrides, handle workspace: protocol

- When no preferred version satisfies the peer dep range, only use the
  range directly if it is an exact version (e.g. "4.3.0" from an override).
  For semver ranges (e.g. "1", "^2.0.0"), fall back to the old behavior
  of picking the highest preferred version for deduplication.
- Guard against workspace: protocol ranges that would cause
  semver.maxSatisfying to throw.
- Add unit tests for hoisting deduplication and workspace: ranges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: only apply range-constrained peer selection for exact versions

The previous approach used semver.maxSatisfying(versions, range) for all
peer dep ranges, which broke aliased-dependency deduplication — e.g. when
three aliases of @pnpm.e2e/peer-c existed at 1.0.0, 1.0.1, and 2.0.0,
range ^1.0.0 would pick 1.0.1 instead of 2.0.0.

Now the range-aware logic only activates when the range is an exact
version (semver.valid), which is the override case (e.g. "4.3.0").
Regular semver ranges fall back to picking the highest preferred version.

- retry pnpm audit via /-/npm/v1/security/audits/quick when /-/npm/v1/security/audits returns non-200
- preserve existing behavior when the primary endpoint succeeds
- include both endpoint responses in the error when both fail
- add regression tests for quick fallback and dual-404 behavior

title: test: fix several flaky tests in pkg-manager
body:
Fix several flaky tests I observed while working on #10341.

Related flaky test fix: https://github.com/pnpm/registry-mock/pull/30
author: jasonpaulos

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR reduces CI test flakiness across the pnpm codebase by hardening a few assertions/mocks in tests and enabling Jest retries specifically in CI via the shared `@pnpm/jest-config`.

**Changes:**
- Add `jest.retryTimes(3)` (CI-only) in the shared Jest setup to automatically retry failed tests during CI runs.
- Stabilize tests by removing ANSI-sensitive string matching and by mocking GitHub requests used during resolution (git resolver + Deno runtime resolver).
- Ensure required dist-tags are present for a catalogs update test to avoid depending on pre-existing registry-mock state.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `__utils__/jest-config/setupFilesAfterEnv.js` | Adds CI-only Jest retries using `ci-info`’s `isCI`. |
| `__utils__/jest-config/package.json` | Adds `ci-info` dependency required by the setup file. |
| `pnpm-lock.yaml` | Records the new `ci-info` dependency for `__utils__/jest-config`. |
| `pkg-manager/plugin-commands-installation/test/update/getUpdateChoices.test.ts` | Makes assertion robust by stripping ANSI/VT control characters before substring check. |
| `pkg-manager/core/test/lockfile.ts` | Cleans up nock after each test and mocks GitHub HEAD check to prevent resolver behavior flaking due to network issues. |
| `pkg-manager/core/test/install/denoRuntime.ts` | Cleans up nock after each test and mocks GitHub API + checksum downloads to reduce network flakiness. |
| `pkg-manager/core/test/catalogs.ts` | Adds missing dist-tag setup so the “matching filter” update test doesn’t depend on external state. |
| `.changeset/giant-shoes-smell.md` | Announces `@pnpm/jest-config` change (retries in CI). |
</details>

title: fix: retry existence check in global virtual store race condition handler
body: When 3+ threads/processes concurrently import the same package to the global virtual store, a third party can rimraf the target between another thread's failed rename and its existence check. Retry the check up to 4 times with 50ms delays to let the competing operation complete.
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Addresses a race condition during concurrent imports into the global virtual store by making the post-rename “target already has expected content” check resilient to a brief window where another worker temporarily deletes the target before completing its own rename. **Changes:** - Add a bounded retry loop (4 attempts, 50ms delay between retries) around the target existence check after `renameOverwrite.sync` fails with `ENOTEMPTY`/`EEXIST`. - Add `rimrafed` to `cspell.json` to avoid spellcheck failures from the new comment. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | fs/indexed-pkg-importer/src/importIndexedDir.ts | Retries the “target file exists” check to handle a transient delete/recreate window during concurrent imports. | | cspell.json | Adds a new allowed word used in the updated inline comment. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: retry existence check in global virtual store race condition handler

When 3+ threads/processes concurrently import the same package to the
global virtual store, a third party can rimraf the target between another
thread's failed rename and its existence check. Retry the check up to 4
times with 50ms delays to let the competing operation complete.

title: fix: remove misleading `maxAge` argument to `pMemoize`
body:
## Changes

The `pMemoize` function has never accepted a `maxAge` argument. The docs previously mentioned it as a copy + paste mistake from another package.

- https://github.com/sindresorhus/p-memoize/issues/40
- https://github.com/sindresorhus/p-memoize/pull/44

Here's the implementation of the function, which I double checked to see doesn't read `options.maxAge` or pass down the `options` argument to anything else.

https://github.com/sindresorhus/p-memoize/blob/a006ac3224bbeb344cb62976e7f18bbc5ad61d14/index.ts#L125

Let's remove this to prevent future confusion. This PR should be a no-op.

## Context

I was debugging a test that should have loaded new package metadata but wasn't. It turns out this cached `pMemoize` function never expired.

title: refactor: simplify dependenciesHierarchyForPackage by delegating to getTree
body:
Instead of manually iterating over top-level dependencies, calling getPkgInfo/getTreeNodeChildId/getTree per dependency, and handling dedup/search logic in parallel with materializeChildren, delegate entirely to a single getTree call with the importer as root.

The returned PackageNode[] are then post-categorized into their dependency fields (dependencies, devDependencies, optionalDependencies) using a fieldMap built from the lockfile importer snapshot.

This eliminates the duplicated dedup/search handling between dependenciesHierarchyForPackage and materializeChildren, and removes the GetTreeResult wrapper type from getTree (now returns PackageNode[] directly). The materializeChildren cache is now the sole mechanism for cross-importer deduplication.
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR refactors `dependenciesHierarchyForPackage` to delegate dependency tree building to a single `getTree` call using the importer as the root, rather than making separate calls for each top-level dependency. The returned nodes are then post-categorized into their dependency fields. This eliminates duplicated deduplication and search handling logic, simplifies the codebase, and removes the `GetTreeResult` wrapper type in favor of returning `PackageNode[]` directly. **Changes:** - Simplified `getTree` to return `PackageNode[]` directly instead of a `GetTreeResult` wrapper - Refactored `dependenciesHierarchyForPackage` to make a single `getTree` call with the importer as root - Unified deduplication logic in `materializeChildren` as the sole cross-importer dedup mechanism ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | reviewing/dependencies-hierarchy/src/getTree.ts | Removed `GetTreeResult` interface and simplified `getTree` to return `PackageNode[]` directly, moving all dedup handling to `materializeChildren` | | reviewing/dependencies-hierarchy/src/buildDependenciesHierarchy.ts | Refactored to call `getTree` once per importer with `maxDepth: opts.depth + 1`, then categorize nodes into dependency fields using a fieldMap; removed unused imports | | reviewing/dependencies-hierarchy/test/getTree.test.ts | Updated tests to expect `PackageNode[]` return type and verify dedup behavior at node level instead of result wrapper level | | cspell.json | Added "dedup" to dictionary | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

commits.message:
refactor: simplify dependenciesHierarchyForPackage by delegating to getTree

Instead of manually iterating over top-level dependencies, calling
getPkgInfo/getTreeNodeChildId/getTree per dependency, and handling
dedup/search logic in parallel with materializeChildren, delegate
entirely to a single getTree call with the importer as root.

The returned PackageNode[] are then post-categorized into their
dependency fields (dependencies, devDependencies, optionalDependencies)
using a fieldMap built from the lockfile importer snapshot.

This eliminates the duplicated dedup/search handling between
dependenciesHierarchyForPackage and materializeChildren, and removes
the GetTreeResult wrapper type from getTree (now returns PackageNode[]
directly). The materializeChildren cache is now the sole mechanism for
cross-importer deduplication.

title: feat: reverse `pnpm why` tree and improve `list`/`why` output
body:
## Summary

- **`pnpm why` now shows a reverse dependency tree.** The searched package appears at the root with its dependants as branches, walking back to workspace roots. This replaces the previous forward-tree output which was noisy and hard to read for deeply nested dependencies.
- **Replaced `archy` with a new `@pnpm/text.tree-renderer` package** that renders trees using box-drawing characters (├──, └──, │) and supports grouped sections, dim connectors, and deduplication markers.
- **Show peer dependency hash suffixes** in `pnpm list` and `pnpm why` output to distinguish between different peer-dep variants of the same package.
- **Improved `pnpm list` visual output:** bold importer nodes, dimmed workspace paths, dependency grouping, package count summary, and deterministic sort order.
- **Added `--long` support to `pnpm why`** and the ability to read package manifests from the CAS store.
- **Deduplicated shared code** between `list` and `why` commands into a common module, and reused `getPkgInfo` in the why tree builder.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `@pnpm/text.tree-renderer` (new package)
- [x] Unit tests for `renderWhyTree`
- [x] Updated integration tests for `pnpm list` and `pnpm why`
- [x] Updated recursive list tests
author: zkochan

reviews.body:

This PR significantly refactors the `pnpm list` and `pnpm why` commands to improve their output and usability.

**Purpose:** 
Replace the `archy` package with a custom tree renderer (`@pnpm/text.tree-renderer`) and reverse the `pnpm why` output to show a bottom-up dependency tree (from the searched package back to workspace roots), making it easier to understand why a package is installed.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `@pnpm/text.tree-renderer` package with box-drawing characters and grouped sections
- Reverses `pnpm why` tree to show dependants walking back to importers  
- Shows peer dependency hash suffixes to distinguish peer-dep variants
- Adds visual improvements: bold importers, dimmed paths, dependency grouping, package count summary

title: perf: share dependency graph and materialization cache across importers in list/why
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR optimizes the performance of `pnpm why` and `pnpm list` commands in workspaces with many importers by sharing the dependency graph and materialization cache across all importers instead of rebuilding them independently for each one.

**Changes:**
- Refactored dependency graph building to accept multiple root nodes and build a single shared graph
- Modified tree materialization to return structured results with deduplication metadata
- Implemented cross-importer caching to avoid redundant tree materialization

title: fix(core): decouple shouldRefreshResolution from canResolve in custom resolvers
body:
## Summary

- Decoupled `shouldRefreshResolution` (renamed from `shouldForceResolve`) from `canResolve` — it now runs independently for **every** lockfile package, letting resolvers handle their own filtering via `depPath` and `pkgSnapshot`
- Renamed `shouldForceResolve` → `shouldRefreshResolution` to better reflect its purpose
- Optimized `checkCustomResolverForceResolve` to handle sync returns without creating promises, and short-circuit on the first `true` result

## Problem

The previous implementation gated `shouldForceResolve` behind `canResolve`, but `canResolve` received a `bareSpecifier` derived from the depPath key (e.g. `1.0.0`) rather than the original specifier from `package.json` (e.g. `custom:foo`). This meant custom resolvers that match on protocol prefixes in `canResolve` could never match, so `shouldForceResolve` was silently skipped for all custom-resolved packages.

## Fix

Rather than trying to recover the original specifier (which is unavailable for transitive deps and fragile for direct deps across workspaces), the hook is decoupled from `canResolve` entirely. Each resolver's `shouldRefreshResolution` is now responsible for its own filtering logic — typically by inspecting `depPath` or `pkgSnapshot.resolution`.

This is a cleaner API boundary: `canResolve` operates during resolution with full specifier info, while `shouldRefreshResolution` operates pre-resolution with only lockfile data.

## Changes

- **`hooks/types/src/index.ts`** — Renamed `shouldForceResolve` → `shouldRefreshResolution`, updated JSDoc to document the decoupled behavior
- **`pkg-manager/core/src/install/checkCustomResolverForceResolve.ts`** — Removed `canResolve` gate and `parseDepPath` dependency; handles sync/async returns efficiently with a custom `anyTrue` helper
- **`pkg-manager/core/test/install/checkCustomResolverForceResolve.ts`** — Rewrote tests for the new semantics, including error propagation tests
- **`pkg-manager/core/README.md`** — Updated docs to reflect the rename

## Test plan

- [x] `shouldRefreshResolution` is called independently of `canResolve` (even when `canResolve` returns `false`)
- [x] Resolvers can filter by `depPath` or `pkgSnapshot.resolution` type
- [x] Sync and async hooks both work correctly
- [x] Checks run in parallel for async hooks
- [x] Errors from hooks are re-thrown (not swallowed)
- [x] All 14 unit tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: vsumner

commits.message:
fix(core): decouple shouldForceResolve from canResolve in custom resolvers

shouldForceResolve is now called for every package in the lockfile
without gating on canResolve, since it runs before resolution where
the original specifier is not available. Resolvers should handle their
own filtering within shouldForceResolve (e.g. by inspecting depPath
or pkgSnapshot.resolution).
refactor: shouldForceResolve=>shouldRefreshResolution

docs: remove changeset

We don't need a new changeset, we just updated the existing changeset

refactor(core): use Promise.any for early exit in checkCustomResolverForceResolve

Replace Promise.all + .some(Boolean) with Promise.any so that the check
short-circuits as soon as any shouldRefreshResolution hook returns true,
instead of waiting for every hook to complete. Real errors thrown by hooks
are re-thrown instead of being silently swallowed.

refactor(core): replace Promise.any with custom anyTrue helper

Handle sync boolean returns from shouldRefreshResolution without
creating unnecessary promises. Only async results go through the
anyTrue helper, which short-circuits on the first true value.

title: fix: virtual store race condition
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR addresses a race condition during concurrent imports into the global virtual store by making `importIndexedDir` tolerate `ENOTEMPTY`/`EEXIST` failures from the final directory rename when another worker has already completed the import. **Changes:** - Update `importIndexedDir` to handle rename races by treating some `ENOTEMPTY`/`EEXIST` errors as success when the target appears populated. - Add a Jest test that simulates the rename race by mocking `rename-overwrite`. - Bump `path-temp` to `^2.1.1` and update workspace/lock/config files accordingly. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

title: fix: list command should not fail with an OOM error
body:
Each package's full subtree is expanded exactly once. Subsequent occurrences show `deduped (N deps hidden)` so the user knows the subtree exists elsewhere in the output. In JSON output, the same information appears as `"deduped": true` and `"dedupedDependenciesCount": N`.

Example output after the fix: https://gist.github.com/zkochan/ec071623b49c16e50f2ad11d16509c8f

Before the fix: https://gist.github.com/zkochan/49a6b4886a815350e458edbd4711772d

close #8731
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR addresses out-of-memory failures in `pnpm list` / `pnpm why` on large dependency graphs by ensuring each package subtree is expanded at most once and subsequent occurrences are rendered as deduped nodes (with a hidden-deps count), including in JSON output.

**Changes:**
- Replaces recursive tree building with a two-phase approach: build a BFS dependency graph, then materialize a deduplicated tree using a shared cache.
- Adds dedupe indicators to tree and JSON renderers (`deduped`, `dedupedDependenciesCount`) so users can see when subtrees are elided.
- Adds/updates tests and fixtures (including a large lockfile fixture) to prevent regressions and validate circular/search/dedupe behavior.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 15 out of 16 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| reviewing/list/test/manyDeps.ts | Adds a regression test to ensure `list` can handle a large dependency graph without OOM. |
| reviewing/list/test/__fixtures__/many-deps/package.json | Fixture project manifest for the large dependency graph test. |
| reviewing/list/test/__fixtures__/many-deps/.npmrc | Fixture registry config for the large dependency graph test. |
| reviewing/list/test/__fixtures__/many-deps/pnpm-lock.yaml | Large lockfile fixture used to reproduce the prior OOM scenario. |
| reviewing/list/src/renderTree.ts | Adds `deduped (N deps hidden)` rendering in tree output. |
| reviewing/list/src/renderJson.ts | Adds `deduped` / `dedupedDependenciesCount` to JSON output schema. |
| reviewing/dependencies-hierarchy/test/getTree.test.ts | Updates tests to use the new graph+cache approach and adds coverage for dedupe/search/circular interactions. |
| reviewing/dependencies-hierarchy/test/circularTree.json | Updates expected circular fixture output to match dedupe behavior. |
| reviewing/dependencies-hierarchy/src/getTree.

title: build: replace ts-jest with simple transformer
body:
## Changes

This replaces `ts-jest` with Node.js's builtin type-stripping function.

## Motivation

The `ts-jest` transformer ships a full `typescript` dependency. Now that we're using TypeScript's `erasableSyntaxOnly` feature, we don't need a fully featured TypeScript compiler when running tests.

- https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10365

Node.js's `stripTypeScriptTypes` function should be a lot simpler, faster, and results in fewer dependencies.

## Benchmarks

For testing the performance of this change, I edited a test suite in `pkg-manager/core` to no-op and disabled Verdaccio.

<details>
<summary>Click to see diff</summary>

```diff
diff --git a/pkg-manager/core/package.json b/pkg-manager/core/package.json
index 9c0f24d46..e5d391c0d 100644
--- a/pkg-manager/core/package.json
+++ b/pkg-manager/core/package.json
@@ -165,6 +165,6 @@
     "node": ">=22.12"
   },
   "jest": {
-    "preset": "@pnpm/jest-config/with-registry"
+    "preset": "@pnpm/jest-config"
   }
 }
diff --git a/pkg-manager/core/test/install/local.ts b/pkg-manager/core/test/install/local.ts
index abe5dfb6a..56c477044 100644
--- a/pkg-manager/core/test/install/local.ts
+++ b/pkg-manager/core/test/install/local.ts
@@ -22,6 +22,9 @@ import { testDefaults } from '../utils/index.js'
 
 const f = fixtures(import.meta.dirname)
 
+// eslint-disable-next-line jest/no-focused-tests
+it.only('disable tests for performance benchmarking', () => {})
+
 test('scoped modules from a directory', async () => {
   const project = prepareEmpty()
   await addDependenciesToPackage({}, [`file:${f.find('local-scoped-pkg')}`], testDefaults())
```

</details>

For warmup, I ran:

```
❯ pnpm run test test/install/local.ts
```

And then performed benchmarks using:

```
❯ hyperfine 'pnpm run _test test/install/local.ts --no-cache'
```

### Before (1b4df57a0)

```
Benchmark 1: pnpm run _test test/install/local.ts --no-cache
  Time (mean ± σ):      3.318 s ±  0.072 s    [User: 4.051 s, System: 0.869 s]
  Range (min … max):    3.212 s …  3.441 s    10 runs
```

### After

```
Benchmark 1: pnpm run _test test/install/local.ts --no-cache
  Time (mean ± σ):      3.144 s ±  0.094 s    [User: 3.844 s, System: 0.849 s]
  Range (min … max):    3.051 s …  3.315 s    10 runs
```

So it's a tiny bit faster. I think this is still worth merging since it reduces the complexity of running tests significantly.
author: gluxon

title: fix: disable global virtual store during `pnpm deploy`
body:
## Problem

The deploy directory from `pnpm deploy` should ideally be self-contained. This isn't true when `enableGlobalVirtualStore` is set. Symlinks are created in the deploy directory that reference the global store.

## Changes

Let's override `enableGlobalVirtualStore` to always be `false`.

## Testing

I tested this manually and verified the output.

I can write a new test for this specific scenario if desired, but I think the existing unit tests cover this well. We have tests for `pnpm deploy` and `enableGlobalVirtualStore` already. I personally don't think we need tests to cover every command and option combination.
author: gluxon

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR makes `pnpm deploy` produce a self-contained deploy directory even when `enableGlobalVirtualStore` is enabled, by forcing that setting off for deploy-related installs. **Changes:** - Force `enableGlobalVirtualStore: false` in `pnpm deploy` execution paths to avoid symlinks outside the deploy directory - Expose `enableGlobalVirtualStore` in installation command option types - Add a changeset documenting the behavior change ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | releasing/plugin-commands-deploy/src/deploy.ts | Overrides `enableGlobalVirtualStore` to ensure deploy output is self-contained | | pkg-manager/plugin-commands-installation/src/installDeps.ts | Adds `enableGlobalVirtualStore` to the picked config options for install deps | | pkg-manager/plugin-commands-installation/src/install.ts | Adds `enableGlobalVirtualStore` to the picked config options for install command options | | .changeset/bold-words-give.md | Documents deploy behavior change and bumps affected packages | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: feat(workspace filtering): add support for filtering packages since last commit in repos under git worktree
body: Add support when managing repo in git worktree for filtering for packages changed or affected since last commit. Change made to better support concurrent-development workflows using LLM/agents across multiple git worktrees. Prior to this change, filtering for changed packages under git worktrees always returned 0 changed packages, even when commits since main had been made
author: kwkaiser

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview Adds git-worktree compatibility to `@pnpm/filter-workspace-packages` when filtering packages changed since a commit, addressing cases where the `.git` entry is a file (worktree) rather than a directory. **Changes:** - Switch repo-root detection to `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` to support git worktrees. - Add a regression test covering “changed packages under a git worktree”. - Remove `find-up` dependency and update lockfile/spellchecker/changeset accordingly. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | workspace/filter-workspace-packages/test/index.ts | Adds a new integration test that creates a repo + worktree and verifies diff-based selection works. | | workspace/filter-workspace-packages/src/getChangedPackages.ts | Changes repo root discovery from `find-up` to `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`. | | workspace/filter-workspace-packages/package.json | Removes `find-up` dependency. | | pnpm-lock.yaml | Removes `find-up` from the importer lock entry. | | cspell.json | Adds dictionary entries for new git/worktree-related terms. | | .changeset/every-impalas-change.md | Declares a minor bump and documents the worktree-related change. |
Files not reviewed (1) * **pnpm-lock.yaml**: Language not supported
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
feat(workspace filtering): add support for filtering packages since last commit under git worktree

 support managing repo in git worktree for filtering for packages changed since last commit

title: fix: allow pnpm `run -r` to work with empty pnpm-workspace.yaml
body:
close #10497 

Fix `pnpm run -r` failing with "No projects matched the filters" when an empty `pnpm-workspace.yaml` exists.
author: ryo-manba

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes `pnpm run -r` incorrectly failing with “No projects matched the filters” when a `pnpm-workspace.yaml` exists but is empty (or otherwise provides no workspace package patterns), ensuring recursive script execution works for non-monorepo projects. **Changes:** - Add a regression test covering `pnpm run -r` with an empty `pnpm-workspace.yaml`. - Adjust recursive filter initialization to only auto-exclude the workspace root when workspace package patterns are actually configured. - Add a changeset documenting the patch fix. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `pnpm/test/monorepo/index.ts` | Adds regression test verifying `pnpm run -r` succeeds when `pnpm-workspace.yaml` is empty. | | `pnpm/src/main.ts` | Prevents default root-exclusion behavior for recursive commands when no workspace package patterns are set, avoiding empty selection in non-monorepos. | | `.changeset/fifty-mangos-arrive.md` | Records the user-facing patch note for the fix. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

Updates `actions/upload-artifact` from 4.6.2 to 6.0.0
Release notes

Sourced from actions/upload-artifact's releases.

v6.0.0

v6 - What's new

[!IMPORTANT] actions/upload-artifact@v6 now runs on Node.js 24 (runs.using: node24) and requires a minimum Actions Runner version of 2.327.1. If you are using self-hosted runners, ensure they are updated before upgrading.

Node.js 24

This release updates the runtime to Node.js 24. v5 had preliminary support for Node.js 24, however this action was by default still running on Node.js 20. Now this action by default will run on Node.js 24.

What's Changed

  • Upload Artifact Node 24 support by @​salmanmkc in actions/upload-artifact#719
  • fix: update @​actions/artifact for Node.js 24 punycode deprecation by @​salmanmkc in actions/upload-artifact#744

Updates `github/codeql-action` from 4.31.9 to 4.31.11
Release notes

Sourced from github/codeql-action's releases.

v4.31.11

  • When running a Default Setup workflow with Actions debugging enabled, the CodeQL Action will now use more unique names when uploading logs from the Dependabot authentication proxy as workflow artifacts. This ensures that the artifact names do not clash between multiple jobs in a build matrix. #3409
  • Improved error handling throughout the CodeQL Action. #3415
  • Added experimental support for automatically excluding generated files from the analysis. This feature is not currently enabled for any analysis. In the future, it may be enabled by default for some GitHub-managed analyses. #3318
  • The changelog extracts that are included with releases of the CodeQL Action are now shorter to avoid duplicated information from appearing in Dependabot PRs. #3403

v4.31.10

CodeQL Action Changelog

See the releases page for the relevant changes to the CodeQL CLI and language packs.

4.31.10 - 12 Jan 2026

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.23.9. #3393

See the full CHANGELOG.md for more information.
