title: fix: skip local file: protocol dependencies during pnpm fetch
body:
This fixes an issue where `pnpm fetch` would fail in Docker builds when local directory dependencies (file: protocol) were not available.

## Problem

When using `pnpm fetch` in a Dockerfile for caching dependencies, it fails with:

```
ENOENT: no such file or directory, scandir '/app/some/path'
```

This happens when you have a dependency like:

```json
{
  "devDependencies": {
    "@org/pkg": "file:./some/path"
  }
}
```

## Solution

The `pnpm fetch` command is specifically designed to pre-cache dependencies from the registry in Docker builds. Local `file:` protocol dependencies should be skipped because:

1. They cannot be fetched from a registry
2. They are typically not available in the Docker build context (only the lockfile and package.json are copied)
3. Including them defeats the purpose of pre-caching deps

This PR adds an explicit `ignoreLocalPackages` option that is passed from the fetch command. When a local dependency is skipped, an info message is logged for transparency.

## Changes

- Added `ignoreLocalPackages` option to `StrictInstallOptions` (core)
- Added `ignoreLocalPackages` option to `HeadlessOptions` (headless)
- Added `ignoreLocalPackages` option to `LockfileToDepGraphOptions` (deps.graph-builder)
- Added `ignoreLocalPackages` option to `LockfileToHoistedDepGraphOptions` (headless)
- Skip directory deps in `buildGraphFromPackages` when `ignoreLocalPackages` is true
- Added the same logic to `lockfileToHoistedDepGraph` for consistency
- Pass `ignoreLocalPackages: true` from the fetch command explicitly
- Log an info message when skipping local dependencies for transparency
- Added regression test

Closes #10460
author: kairosci

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR fixes an issue where `pnpm fetch` fails in Docker builds when local directory dependencies (file: protocol) are not available. The fix adds an `ignoreLocalPackages` option that skips directory-type resolutions during `pnpm fetch`, which only downloads packages from the registry. **Changes:** - Added `ignoreLocalPackages` option to skip local directory dependencies during `pnpm fetch` - Applied the fix to both `lockfileToDepGraph` and `lockfileToHoistedDepGraph` for consistency - Added a regression test that simulates the Docker build scenario ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg-manager/plugin-commands-installation/test/fetch.ts | Added regression test for skipping file: protocol dependencies | | pkg-manager/headless/src/lockfileToHoistedDepGraph.ts | Added logic to skip directory dependencies when `ignoreLocalPackages` is true | | pkg-manager/headless/src/index.ts | Passes `ignoreLocalPackages` based on `ignorePackageManifest` flag | | deps/graph-builder/src/lockfileToDepGraph.ts | Added logic to skip directory dependencies when `ignoreLocalPackages` is true | | .changeset/fix-pnpm-fetch-file-protocol.md | Changeset documenting the fix |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

fix: skip local file: protocol dependencies during pnpm fetch

This fixes an issue where pnpm fetch would fail in Docker builds when
local directory dependencies (file: protocol) were not available.

The fix adds an ignoreLocalPackages option that is passed from the fetch
command to skip local dependencies during graph building, since pnpm
fetch only downloads packages from the registry and doesn't need local
packages that won't be available in Docker builds.

Closes #10460

title: build: rework bundled `dist/node_modules`
body:
## Background The `pnpm` package contains a bundled `node_modules` directory to include dependencies like `node-gyp` and `v8-compile-cache` out of the box. ## Changes This PR reworks the build process for this directory. The benefits of the new approach are: - **Reproducible** — The bundled `dist/node_modules` are now reproducible and reuse the `pnpm-lock.yaml` in this repo. Previously the bundled `dist/node_modules` contained latest semver compatible version of these dependencies. - **Unblocks a deprecation in v11** — pnpm v11 is expected to remove support for pnpm settings declared in `package.json`. The new approach no longer requires declaring `pnpm.overrides` in the `pnpm/package.json` file. - **Removes a warning during local dev** — A warning when running `pnpm install` in this repo is now removed. ## Considerations The currently published pnpm dist bundles the latest version of node-gyp 11.x (i.e. `11.5.0`) and its dependencies. This version isn't present in the `pnpm-lock.yaml` file in this repo at the moment. We should upgrade `node-gyp` in this repo separately now that this PR beings bundling the same dependency graph used for development. - https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10509 ## Testing - Here's a link to the latest version of pnpm: https://registry.npmjs.org/pnpm/-/pnpm-10.28.1.tgz - Here's a locally packed tgz created from running `pnpm pack` on this PR: [pnpm-11.0.0-alpha.3.tgz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24848671/pnpm-11.0.0-alpha.3.tgz) Comparing the two, the sizes are approximately the same. The differences in size are due to different versions of `node-gyp` being bundled.
BeforeAfter
author: gluxon

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR reworks the build process for the bundled `dist/node_modules` directory in the pnpm package. The new approach makes the bundled dependencies reproducible by reusing the workspace's `pnpm-lock.yaml`, removes the need for `pnpm.overrides` in `package.json` (unblocking a v11 deprecation), and eliminates a local development warning.

**Changes:**
- Replaced `publish-packed` with a custom TypeScript build script (`bundle-deps.ts`) that creates a temporary workspace to install and bundle dependencies
- Removed `pnpm.overrides` configuration from `pnpm/package.json` and related meta-updater logic that propagated these overrides
- Added `nm-prune` as a new dependency to optimize the bundled `node_modules` size

### Reviewed changes

| .meta-updater/src/index.ts | Removed logic for propagating workspace overrides and validating catalog references in pnpm package |
| .meta-updater/package.json | Removed `@pnpm/parse-overrides` dependency no longer needed |

</details>

</details>

<summary>Co

What publish-packed also does and your changes didn't implement, it removes dependencies and optionalDependencies from package.json of the published package. If you don't removed them, they will be still installed when pnpm CLI is installed.

build: move `node-gyp` from `optionalDependencies` to `dependencies`

The `node-gyp` dependency is bundled into the `pnpm` package before it's
published. The dependency declaration itself is then removed from the
published package manifest.

This means there's not a point to declaring `node-gyp` as an optional
dependency. It'll always be bundled and the published manifest doesn't
contain the dependency declaration.

title: perf: optimize how the integrities of files in the CAFS are stored
body:
We currently store the base64 integrity checksum of every file of the package in the index file. We also store the algo in each even though the algo is the same for ever file.

In this PR I do 2 optimizations:

1. the algo is only stored once per index file
2. the integrity checksum is stored in HEX format, which makes the index file larger but removes the need to convert the hash in most cases. The file paths are also in hex format, so we only needed base64 for verifying the content of the file. However, in most cases we skip verification of the file if we see that the file was not modified since creation (we check file attributes)

before:

```json
{
  "files": {
    "index.js": {
      "integrity": "sha512-V9oc/lDp3+F2qOJ+Xp2kZu2Tzl8asNXpQVy+QZV9Mw11+YGad0XBuqwEsPsLMZt40jOibpiTtR/rjNU4C3wEtw==",
      "checkedAt": 1769155915890,
      "mode": 420,
      "size": 3214
    }
  }
}
``` 

after:

```jsonc
{
  "algo": "sha512",
  "files": {
    "index.js": {
      "digest": "f310afae50bb5b74e5c17c5eb6fe426538b9deccd88664fbb66a5717fb6d36d86d4d1f530bb63b58914f9894e81da490e2e39bb99c8e01174e258358b9349b5c",
      // The file's location in the store is:
      // f3/10afae50bb5b74e5c17c5eb6fe426538b9deccd88664fbb66a5717fb6d36d86d4d1f530bb63b58914f9894e81da490e2e39bb99c8e01174e258358b9349b5c
      "checkedAt": 1769155915890,
      "mode": 420,
      "size": 3214
    }
  }
}
```

***

I am not sure there is a difference in performance.

Before:

```
Benchmark 1: node ~/src/pnpm/pnpm2/pnpm/dist/pnpm.mjs i --ignore-scripts --offline
  Time (mean ± σ):     72.017 s ±  1.790 s    [User: 8.415 s, System: 41.856 s]
  Range (min … max):   69.791 s … 74.961 s    10 runs
```

After:

```
Benchmark 1: node ~/src/pnpm/pnpm3/pnpm/dist/pnpm.mjs i --ignore-scripts --offline
  Time (mean ± σ):     69.996 s ±  2.690 s    [User: 8.266 s, System: 41.843 s]
  Range (min … max):   65.967 s … 74.928 s    10 runs

```
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates pnpm’s CAFS package index format to avoid storing full SRI strings per file by storing the hash algorithm once per index and storing per-file hex digests instead of `<algo>-<base64>` integrities.

**Changes:**
- Replace per-file `integrity` strings with per-file `digest` (hex) and add an `algo` field to the package files index.
- Update CAFS path lookups, integrity verification, and tooling (`store status`, `find-hash`, prune) to work with hex digests.
- Update affected tests and dependent packages to the new index format.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 17 out of 17 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| worker/src/start.ts | Writes new index format (`algo` + hex digests) from worker when adding tarballs/dirs. |
| store/plugin-commands-store/src/storeStatus/index.ts | Reconstructs SRI strings for `dint` checks using `algo` + stored hex digest. |
| store/plugin-commands-store-inspecting/src/findHash.ts | Updates hash search to compare against stored hex digests. |
| store/package-store/src/storeController/prune.ts | Tracks removed CAFS file hashes as hex digests (matching new stored format). |
| store/cafs/test/index.ts | Adjusts CAFS tests to use hex digests for file path resolution. |
| store/cafs/src/writeBufferToCafs.ts | Updates integrity verification to use hex digest + algorithm. |
| store/cafs/src/readManifestFromStore.ts | Reads manifests via CAFS file paths derived from stored hex digests. |
| store/cafs/src/index.ts | Updates CAFS public API type for `getFilePathByModeInCafs` (digest-based). |
| store/cafs/src/getFilePathInCafs.ts | Removes integrity parsing for file paths; uses hex directly. |
| store/cafs/src/checkPkgFilesIntegrity.ts | Verifies files via `ssri.fromHex(digest, algo)` and updates index schema. |
| store/cafs-types/src/index.ts | Renames `PackageFileInfo.

This path reads and re-writes that index without migrating it, which can leave the index in a legacy/mixed shape and break later readers expecting digest/algo. Consider normalizing the loaded index before further use (populate algo and convert file entries to { digest }). ``` let existingFilesIndex!: PackageFilesIndex try { existingFilesIndex = readMsgpackFileSync(filesIndexFile) } catch { ```

title: perf: migrate internal cache and index files to MessagePack serialization
body:

## Pull request overview

Migrates pnpm’s internal store index files and metadata cache files from JSON serialization to MessagePack (via `msgpackr`) to improve install performance.

**Changes:**
- Introduce `@pnpm/fs.msgpack-file` utilities for MessagePack read/write and adopt them across the repo.
- Switch store index files and metadata cache files to `.mpk`, and update related readers/writers and CLI commands.
- Replace object-based `PackageFiles*/SideEffects*` shapes with `Map`-based representations and update tests accordingly.

title: fix: prevent implicit root exclusion when user filters are provided
body:
Previously, `pnpm` would implicitly exclude the root package if `pnpm-workspace.yaml` was present, even if the user explicitly provided filters that should have matched the root (e.g. `--filter .` or `--filter root-pkg`).

This change ensures that the implicit exclusion is only applied when NO user filters are provided.

fixes issue: #10462 

Contribution by Gittensor, see my contribution statistics at https://gittensor.io/miners/details?githubId=178221229

title: fix: force re-fetch when resolution integrity changes
body:
This PR implements zkochan's suggestion from #10451.

## Problem

Custom resolvers needed a way to force re-fetching a package when its content changed (e.g., an updated local tarball). The original PR (#10451) proposed exposing a `forceFetch` flag in the `ResolveResult` interface.

## Solution

Instead of requiring resolvers to explicitly set `forceFetch`, the package requester now automatically detects when the integrity has changed between the current package and the newly resolved package. When the integrities differ, the package is automatically re-fetched.

This is cleaner because:
1. Custom resolvers can simply return the correct integrity in their resolution
2. The package requester handles the comparison automatically
3. No new API surface is needed

## Changes

- Added integrity comparison in `resolveAndFetch()` that compares `currentPkg.resolution.integrity` with `resolveResult.resolution.integrity`
- When integrities differ, the package is marked as `updated` and force-fetched
- Added a test to verify this behavior

Closes #10451
author: TrevorBurnham

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR implements automatic re-fetching of packages when their integrity changes, as an alternative to requiring custom resolvers to explicitly set a `forceFetch` flag. The solution detects integrity changes by comparing the integrity from the current package with the newly resolved package's integrity. **Changes:** - Added automatic integrity comparison in `resolveAndFetch()` to detect when a package's integrity has changed - When integrity differs, the package is marked as `updated` and force-fetched automatically - Added a test to verify the new behavior with a custom resolver ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg-manager/package-requester/src/packageRequester.ts | Implements integrity comparison logic and triggers force-fetch when integrity changes | | pkg-manager/package-requester/test/index.ts | Adds test case verifying force-fetch behavior when resolution integrity differs from current package | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: force re-fetch when resolution integrity changes

When a resolver returns a resolution with a different integrity than
the current package's resolution, automatically force re-fetching the
package. This allows custom resolvers to trigger re-fetches by simply
returning the updated integrity, without needing to explicitly set
a forceFetch flag.

Closes #10451

title: feat: pass pkgSnapshot to shouldForceResolve
body:
Follow-up for #10440

Currently, for every lockfile entry, pnpm calls all relevant custom resolvers' `shouldForceResolve` methods with these arguments:

```ts
shouldForceResolve(wantedDependency, wantedLockfile)
```

For the typical `shouldForceResolve` use case (as I imagine it), though, the most relevant information is the corresponding lockfile entry for that dependency. That could be extracted with `wantedLockfile.packages[`${wantedDependency.alias}@${wantedDependency.bareSpecifier}`]`, but that's cumbersome.

Also, I'd opted to use `wantedDependency` as the first argument here for consistency with other custom resolver methods (`canResolve`, `resolve`), but the `wantedDependency` object constructed for this call is significantly different from the one passed to those other methods, since it's derived from the lockfile rather than the package manifest. That inconsistency could be misleading.

So, this PR updates the method signature to:

```ts
shouldForceResolve(depPath, pkgSnapshot)
```

I've tested this locally to verify that it satisfies my use case perfectly:

* Having `depPath` (e.g. `some-dep@1.2.3`) as the first argument is clear and convenient for debugging.
* Having `pkgSnapshot` lets me easily check for the information that's most relevant to the force resolve decision (e.g. the existing resolution metadata).

Per discussion on the PR, `wantedLockfile` isn't really needed now that we have the relevant lockfile entry, `pkgSnapshot`.

This is of course a breaking change, but it's a breaking change to a recently-added feature that's only in an alpha release.
author: TrevorBurnham

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR updates the `shouldForceResolve` method signature in custom resolvers to provide more convenient and relevant information. The change simplifies the API by passing the dependency path and lockfile entry directly, rather than requiring users to parse and look up this information themselves. **Changes:** - Updated `shouldForceResolve` method signature from `(wantedDependency, wantedLockfile)` to `(depPath, pkgSnapshot, wantedLockfile)` - Removed dependency path parsing and `canResolve` checking logic from the implementation - Updated all tests to reflect the new signature and removed tests for the now-removed `canResolve` check ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `pkg-manager/core/src/install/checkCustomResolverForceResolve.ts` | Simplified implementation to directly pass depPath and pkgSnapshot to shouldForceResolve, removing dependency path parsing and canResolve check | | `pkg-manager/core/test/install/checkCustomResolverForceResolve.test.ts` | Updated all test cases to use new signature, removed obsolete tests, and added tests verifying correct parameters are passed | | `pkg-manager/core/README.md` | Updated API documentation and example code to demonstrate the new signature with a practical time-based cache invalidation use case | | `hooks/types/src/index.ts` | Updated TypeScript interface definition with new signature and improved JSDoc comments | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix(manifest-utils): normalize peer specs for protocol deps
body:
## Summary
Ensure `--save-peer` always writes a valid peer range: if the saved spec is not a valid peer range, derive the range from the resolved version; if no version is available, fall back to `*`. The original spec is preserved in the saved dependency field (e.g. devDependencies).

Useful for protocol-prefixed installs (e.g. jsr:) so `peerDependencies` records a valid semver range, while `devDependencies` preserves the full protocol spec.

Fixes #10417

## Repro
1. `pnpm init`
2. `pnpm add jsr:@std/html --save-peer`
3. `pnpm add sass`

## Expected vs actual (before)
- Expected: `peerDependencies` contains a valid peer range (semver / `workspace:` / `catalog:`)
- Actual: `peerDependencies` was saved as a protocol-prefixed spec (i.e. `jsr:^1.0.5` instead of `^1.0.5`), causing `ERR_PNPM_INVALID_PEER_DEPENDENCY_SPECIFICATION` and blocking subsequent installs

## What changed
- Pass resolved versions into the manifest update step and derive a peer range when the saved spec is not a valid peer range.
- Fall back to `*` when no resolved version is available.
- Preserve the original spec in the saved dependency field.
- Added regression test coverage for JSR + `--save-peer` & for git-hosted and tarball packages as well

## Tests
- `pnpm --filter @pnpm/manifest-utils test`
- `pnpm --filter @pnpm/resolve-dependencies test`
- `pnpm --filter @pnpm/plugin-commands-installation test`
- `pnpm run test-main`

## Manual verification
- Validated using @bcheidemann’s reproduction repo: https://github.com/bcheidemann/pnpm-reproduction-peer-dep-sources
- Confirmed the repro passes when run via the local pnpm dev build (`pd`) from this repository (not global pnpm)

## Notes on behavior
This keeps `peerDependencies` within pnpm’s allowed formats (semver / `workspace:` / `catalog:`) and avoids writing protocol-prefixed specs there. This change preserves that contract and only normalizes protocol-prefixed specs when it yields a valid peer range.
author: lindsaycode05

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR fixes `pnpm add --save-peer` for protocol-prefixed package installs (e.g., `jsr:`) so that `peerDependencies` receives a valid semver range while `devDependencies` preserves the full protocol specification. Previously, specs like `jsr:^1.0.5` were written directly to `peerDependencies`, causing `ERR_PNPM_INVALID_PEER_DEPENDENCY_SPECIFICATION` and blocking subsequent installs. **Changes:** - Added `normalizePeerSpecifier` function to strip protocol prefixes from peer dependency specs when they resolve to valid semver ranges - Added comprehensive test coverage for JSR protocol with `--save-peer` - Added dependency on `@pnpm/semver.peer-range` package for validation ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

title: feat: pass currentPkg to custom resolvers
body:
Previous discussion: #10342

This PR builds on the approach in #10439 of moving the skip resolution logic from the package requester into the resolvers themselves. The main difference is that `checkCustomResolverForceResolve` iterates over the lockfile, not the package manifests, so that the custom resolvers for subdependencies have the option to force full resolution.
author: TrevorBurnham

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refactors the resolution logic to give resolvers autonomy over whether to skip resolution, moving this decision from the package requester into the individual resolvers. The key architectural change is that resolvers are now always called and receive `currentPkg` (existing lockfile info) and `peekedManifest` (cached manifest from store) as context, allowing them to decide whether to perform full resolution or return cached results.

**Changes:**
- Moved skip-resolution logic from package-requester into individual resolvers (npm, local, git)
- Added `currentPkg` and `peekedManifest` options to resolver interfaces to provide resolution context
- Refactored `checkCustomResolverForceResolve` to iterate over lockfile packages instead of manifest dependencies, enabling custom resolvers for transitive dependencies

### Reviewed changes

| resolving/local-resolver/src/index.ts | Added skip-resolution logic for file tarballs (integrity check) and directories |

title: feat: improve filtered install performance with an optimistic lookup of package metadata from store
body: ## Performance This PR improves filtered install performance significantly. In tests of a real-world (proprietary) repo, **filtered install performance improved 7.78x**. These tests are off of the `main` branch, specifically commit 095f659720076ed8420d1c6403481170b745a403. |Test|Time (s)| |-|-| |Full Install|50.5| |Filtered Install|374.1| |Filtered Install (after this PR)|48.1| ## Explanation My colleagues have noticed `pnpm install --filter ` ends up finishing much slower than a normal full install (i.e. `pnpm install`). This ended up being because: 1. In a filtered install, pnpm computes the wanted lockfile with `opts.skipFetch` as `true`. 2. However, `opts.skipFetch` causes "_resolution_" to need to be performed to get every dependency's `package.json`. 3. The `package.json` of every dependency is then retrieved from the metadata cache. 4. Parsing metadata cache takes a long time since it contains the `package.json` of every version of the dependency. It's a bit surprising, but the end result is that `opts.skipFetch` causes pnpm installation to be significantly slower. ### CPU Profiles The above was discovered through analyzing CPU profiles. Here's an example of a full `pnpm install` on the repo mentioned. It generally finishes in **~50.5s** in this large proprietary repo. ``` Scope: all 1913 workspace projects Already up to date Progress: resolved 4978, reused 4936, downloaded 1, added 0, done Done in 50.5s using pnpm v0.0.0 ``` Here's a `pnpm install --filter` on a small package in the same repo. For the test, I added a new catalog dependency on `react`. This regularly takes more than **6 minutes**. ``` Progress: resolved 4978, reused 1, downloaded 0, added 0, done Done in 6m 14.1s using pnpm v0.0.0 ``` After the changes in this PR, `pnpm install --filter` is fast again. ``` Progress: resolved 4978, reused 1, downloaded 0, added 0, done Done in 48.1s using pnpm v0.0.0 ```
author: gluxon

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR optimizes filtered install performance by implementing an optimistic lookup of package metadata from the content-addressable file store (CAFS) before falling back to full resolution. The change addresses a significant performance regression where filtered installs were ~7.78x slower than full installs. **Key Changes:** - Adds optimistic package manifest retrieval from CAFS during `skipFetch` operations to avoid expensive metadata parsing - Refactors resolution logic to defer full resolution when manifest is available in store - Updates test fixtures to use temporary directories instead of hardcoded paths ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg-manager/package-requester/src/packageRequester.ts | Implements the core optimization by adding `getPackageManifestFromCafs` function and refactoring resolution logic to attempt CAFS lookup before performing full resolution | | pkg-manager/package-requester/test/index.ts | Adds test coverage for the new CAFS manifest lookup optimization and updates existing tests to use temporary directories | | .changeset/ninety-poets-shake.md | Documents the performance fix for filtered installs | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
The fetchingLockerForManifest is not set anywhere now.

title: feat: create new `@pnpm/yaml.document-sync` package
body:
## PR Stack

- https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10405
- https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10402

## Changes

Creating a new `@pnpm/yaml.document-sync` package that should be useful for updating `pnpm-workspace.yaml` and `package.yaml` files in subsequent PRs.

The next PR in the stack (https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10402) uses this library in `@pnpm/workspace.manifest-writer` to preserve comments in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`.

Here's an example of the library's API:

```ts
import { patchDocument } from '@pnpm/yaml.document-sync'
import yaml from 'yaml'

const source = `
# Comment
foo: 1
`

const target = {
  foo: 1,
  bar: 2,
}

const document = yaml.parseDocument(source)
patchDocument(document, target)
```

## Approach

There are 2 possible approaches to preserving YAML comments:

1. Parse the YAML string as a `yaml.Document` and pass it around the codebase. Whenever changes are required, directly modify the `yaml.Document`.
2. Parse the YAML string as JSON. Make changes to the JSON and then later diff the JSON contents when patching the YAML.

This library supports approach 2. I originally went with approach 1 before realizing some form of 2 would be necessary for the `writeSettings` function here.

https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/blob/095f659720076ed8420d1c6403481170b745a403/config/config-writer/src/index.ts#L13

Approach 1 would have required rewriting the pnpm codebase to keep track of all `pnpm-workspace.yaml` settings in a `yaml.Document` and requiring mutations to use the `yaml` library's API. I think this puts undue burden on future maintainers adding new `pnpm-workspace.yaml` settings, so I decided to write a more general purpose library instead.

As another benefit, this approach should be reusable for `package.yaml` later.
author: gluxon

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces a new `@pnpm/yaml-document-sync` package that enables updating YAML documents while preserving comments. The package provides a `patchDocument` function that reconciles differences between a YAML document and an in-memory JSON object, applying changes to the document while maintaining formatting and comments on a best-effort basis.

Key changes:
- New package implementing YAML document synchronization functionality
- Comprehensive test coverage for various YAML structures (maps, lists, scalars, aliases)
- Support for handling edge cases like list reconciliation and alias resolution

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>

title: feat: preserve comments when updating `pnpm-workspace.yaml`
body:
## PR Stack

- https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10405
- https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10402

## Changes

Partially fixes https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/2008.

 The `@pnpm/workspace.manifest-writer` now uses the newly created `@pnpm/yaml-document-sync` when updating `pnpm-workspace.yaml` to retain comments.

## Caveats

After this PR, pnpm will ship with both `yaml` and `js-yaml` as a dependency. While this is unfortunate, it's the best option for keeping lockfile reads and writes fast. For details, see: https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10392#issuecomment-3706491050

It works!

Key changes:
- Migrated workspace manifest writing from `write-yaml-file` to `yaml` library with document patching

title: fix(store): return only directory names when clean expired cache
body: I ran into this error when executing `pnpm store prune`. After looking into the relevant code, I found that the command does not filter out files when traversing directories, which causes macOS Finder cache files like `.DS_Store` to unexpectedly break the command. I understand that users normally wouldn't access this directory, so `.DS_Store` usually wouldn't be present. Still, I think it's important to add logic to ensure that filesystem traversal only considers directories, to improve the robustness of the code. This is a small and straightforward fix. Thanks in advance, and looking forward to your review.
author: nshcr

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes a bug where `pnpm store prune` would fail when encountering non-directory files (like macOS `.DS_Store` files) in the cache directory. The fix ensures that only directories are processed when cleaning the expired DLX cache. **Key Changes:** - Modified `readOptDir` to filter out files and return only directory names - Changed `readdirSync` to use `withFileTypes: true` option for more efficient type checking --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: chore: configure external source maps on final `pnpm.mjs` bundle
body: ## Changes Let's configure esbuild's `sourcemap` option to `external`: https://esbuild.github.io/api/#sourcemap > This mode means the source map is generated into a separate `.js.map` output file alongside the `.js` output file, but unlike linked mode the `.js` output file does not contain a `//# sourceMappingURL=` comment. The `.map` files are then excluded from the NPM package. ## Motivation This allows pnpm developers to more easily: 1. Unminify stack traces for production copies of pnpm 2. Debug pnpm using Chrome Developer Tools. ### Stack Traces Demo As a demonstration of (1), I added an exception to the `readPackage` hook: You can then use a tool such as https://sourcemap.tools to unminify the stack trace. 🎉 All you need to do is upload the `pnpm.mjs.map` file. ### Debugging Demo You can also add the source map when running Chrome's Node.js debugger. Doing so then lets you interactively debug the original TypeScript files. ## Future I think we should upload the `.map` files separately as a GitHub release artifacts as well. What do others think?
author: gluxon

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR configures esbuild to generate external source maps for the pnpm.mjs bundle. The source maps are created alongside the bundled files but are excluded from NPM distribution, allowing developers to debug production issues without increasing package size. **Key Changes:** - Enabled external source map generation in the esbuild configuration - Added exclusion patterns to prevent .map files from being published to NPM ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pnpm/bundle.ts | Added `sourcemap: 'external'` option to esbuild configuration with explanatory comments | | pnpm/package.json | Added `!dist/**/*.map` to exclude source map files from NPM package | | .meta-updater/src/index.ts | Added `!dist/**/*.map` exclusion pattern to manifest updater for consistency | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix: frozen lockfile installs in pnpm repo
body:
## Problem

I noticed frozen lockfile installs stopped working in this repo recently. The lockfile is always seen as out of date and `pnpm install` always performs an installation.

https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/blob/24da84c19d97e07863ef3b4345c4c59018a6d27f/lockfile/verification/src/linkedPackagesAreUpToDate.ts#L86-L87

The failing check is:

```ts
semver.satisfies(linkedPkg.version, availableRange, { loose: true })
```

In this case `linkedPkg.version` was `11.0.0-alpha.2` and `availableRange` is an empty string. The `availableRange` value was an empty string since there was nothing after the `workspace:` protocol here.

## Changes

Fixing this by modifying `workspace:` to `workspace:*` so it passes the `semver.satisfies` call above.

## Question

Is `workspace:` valid? Should we throw an error if we encounter this case?

I think the answer is yes.

I double checked what happens if you run `pnpm pack` on `__utils__/jest-config`, and the `pnpm` dependency becomes an empty string. It's not a problem in this specific case since `@pnpm/jest-config` is a private package, but I think we should consider throwing an error to prevent real users from hitting this problem.

```json
{
  "name": "@pnpm/jest-config",
  "version": "1000.0.5",
  "private": true,
  "main": "jest-preset.js",
  "type": "module",
  "dependencies": {
    "@pnpm/registry-mock": "5.2.0",
    "get-port": "^7.1.0",
    "tree-kill": "^1.2.2",
    "@pnpm/worker": "1000.3.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "pnpm": "",
    "ts-jest-resolver": "2.0.1",
    "@pnpm/jest-config": "1000.0.5"
  },
  "keywords": [
    "pnpm",
    "pnpm11"
  ],
  "repository": "https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/tree/main/__utils__/jest-config"
}
```
author: gluxon

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR fixes an issue where frozen lockfile installs were failing in the pnpm repository. The root cause was an invalid `workspace:` dependency specifier without a version range, which caused `semver.satisfies` validation to fail when checking if linked packages are up to date. **Key Changes:** - Updated the `pnpm` dependency specifier from `workspace:` to `workspace:*` in the jest-config package
Files not reviewed (1) * **pnpm-lock.yaml**: Language not supported

title: fix: `pnpm add` incorrectly modifies a catalog entry in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` to its exact version
body:
## PR Stack

- https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10369
- https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10370

## Problem

An earlier PR #9762 worked around issue #9759 by patching the output of dependency resolution.

The real issue is that we're mutating `wantedDependency.bareSpecifier` here and that change is getting saved to `pnpm-workspace.yaml` unintentionally.

https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/blob/9eddabb32b1d484cc0e0d9ae4c0b1ea9a2eb247a/pkg-manager/resolve-dependencies/src/resolveDependencies.ts#L1313-L1315

We should stop using mutation here, but the reason this replacement is done is explained in:

- https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/9590

## Changes

Instead of mutating `wantedDependency.bareSpecifier` to an exact version to make catalog lookups consistent, let's try a different approach where we pass through a more exact `options.preferredVersions` object.

This fixes https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/9759.
author: gluxon

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes a bug where `pnpm add` incorrectly mutated catalog entries in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` to their exact versions. The root cause was mutating `wantedDependency.bareSpecifier` during dependency resolution, which inadvertently affected the workspace configuration. Key changes: - Removed mutation of `wantedDependency.bareSpecifier` when a preferred version exists - Introduced `getExactSinglePreferredVersions()` to pass exact version preferences through `preferredVersions` instead - Added regression test to verify catalog entries remain unchanged when adding dependencies ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg-manager/resolve-dependencies/src/resolveDependencies.ts | Removed bareSpecifier mutation and uses preferredVersions parameter instead | | pkg-manager/resolve-dependencies/src/getExactSinglePreferredVersions.ts | New utility function to create PreferredVersions with exact version | | pkg-manager/core/test/catalogs.ts | Added regression test verifying catalog entries aren't mutated | | .changeset/thin-crabs-smoke.md | Documented the bug fix in changeset | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message: refactor: factor out a `getRealNameAndSpec` function
test: `pnpm add` does not modify existing catalog entries
fix: resolve preferred version without mutating bare specifier
Merge branch 'main' into gluxon/issue-9759

title: feat: enable injected local packages to work with global virtual store
body:
by leveraging `pkgLocationsByDepPath` for `file:` dependencies.

Related issue: https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/9696
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes injected local packages to work correctly with the global virtual store by introducing `injectionTargetsByDepPath` to track directory dependencies and refactoring how workspace package target directories are determined.

**Key Changes:**
- Introduced `injectionTargetsByDepPath` Map to specifically track directory-based dependencies (injected workspace packages) through the dependency graph building process
- Moved and refactored `extendProjectsWithTargetDirs` from `@pnpm/lockfile.utils` to `@pnpm/headless`, with a new implementation that parses dependency paths to extract workspace package IDs
- Added a helper function `getProjectsWithTargetDirs` in core that builds injection targets from the dependencies graph (which contains correct hash-based paths when global virtual store is enabled)

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `pkg-manager/headless/src/lockfileToHoistedDepGraph.ts` | Initializes and tracks `injectionTargetsByDepPath` Map for directory dependencies during hoisted graph building |
| `pkg-manager/headless/src/index.ts` | Updates imports and replaces `pkgLocationsByDepPath` with `injectionTargetsByDepPath` in function calls |
| `pkg-manager/headless/src/extendProjectsWithTargetDirs.ts` | New implementation that parses dependency paths to map injection targets to workspace projects |
| `pkg-manager/core/test/install/globalVirtualStore.ts` | Adds comprehensive test for injected local packages with global virtual store |
| `pkg-manager/core/src/install/index.ts` | Adds `getProjectsWithTargetDirs` helper and updates to use new `extendProjectsWithTargetDirs` export from headless |
| `lockfile/utils/src/index.ts` | Removes export of `extendProjectsWithTargetDirs` (breaking change) |
| `lockfile/utils/src/extendProjectsWithTargetDirs.ts`

title: feat: enhance `store prune` to clean global virtual store
body:
`pnpm store prune` will now clean the global virtual store via a new project registry and mark-and-sweep garbage collection.

Previous refactor PR, to make this easier to implement: https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10363
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR enhances `pnpm store prune` to clean the global virtual store by implementing mark-and-sweep garbage collection. Projects are now registered via symlinks in a `projects/` directory within the store, enabling the prune command to track active projects and safely remove unreferenced packages.

### Key Changes
- Added project registry system using symlinks in `{storeDir}/projects/` to track projects using the store
- Implemented mark-and-sweep garbage collection algorithm that walks symlinks to identify reachable packages
- Integrated global virtual store pruning into the existing prune workflow, executing before CAS pruning

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 19 out of 21 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `store/package-store/src/storeController/projectRegistry.ts` | New module for registering projects and retrieving registered project paths |
| `store/package-store/src/storeController/pruneGlobalVirtualStore.ts` | New module implementing mark-and-sweep garbage collection for the global virtual store |
| `store/package-store/src/storeController/prune.ts` | Integrated global virtual store pruning as first step before CAS pruning |
| `store/package-store/src/index.ts` | Exported project registry functions for use in other modules |
| `pkg-manager/get-context/src/index.ts` | Added project registration calls when getting context |
| `store/package-store/test/projectRegistry.ts` | Comprehensive unit tests for project registry functionality |
| `store/plugin-commands-store/test/storePrune.ts` | Integration tests for global virtual store pruning scenarios |
| `store/plugin-commands-store/tsconfig.json` | Added package-store project reference |
| `store/plugin-commands-store/package.json` | Added @pnpm/package-store dependency |
| `store/package-store/tsconfig.json` | Added crypto.hash and error proj

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 17 out of 18 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments.

title: feat: provide wantedLockfile to shouldForceResolve
body:
Follow-up for https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10246

I tried to use the new custom resolvers that I added in that PR and realized that my use case for `shouldForceResolve` required access to the lockfile. In fact, I think this is likely to be the common use case for this function: "I want to force full resolution if a newer version of a dependency `@my-company/critical` is available than the one that the lockfile says to install."

That was an oversight on my part! This PR adds a lockfile argument to `shouldForceResolve` to address this use case.
author: TrevorBurnham

Learn how to get started.

The issue occurred because packages resolved from the lockfile (which have `resolvedVia` set to `undefined`) were being incorrectly classified as exotic dependencies and blocked. ### Key Changes - Refactored the exotic dependency check to explicitly handle `null`/`undefined` `resolvedVia` values - Renamed `isNonExoticDep` function to `isExoticDep` for clearer logic flow - Added a null check before calling `isExoticDep` to skip validation for packages with undefined resolution methods ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pkg-manager/resolve-dependencies/src/resolveDependencies.ts | Fixed the logic to skip exotic dependency validation when `resolvedVia` is undefined, and refactored helper function for clarity | | __fixtures__/with-git-protocol-dep/cache/metadata-v1.3/localhost+7769/is-number.v8 | Added test fixture metadata cache file for validating the fix | | .changeset/dirty-cases-scream.md | Added changeset documenting the fix for issue #10324 | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix(cli/config): phantom keys
body: Fixes https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/10296
author: KSXGitHub

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a security/correctness issue where prototype chain properties (like `constructor`, `hasOwnProperty`, `toString`, etc.) were incorrectly identified as valid configuration keys. The fix replaces the `in` operator with `Object.hasOwn()` to check only own properties, not inherited ones.

**Key changes:**
- Replaced `in` operator with `Object.hasOwn()` for property existence checks across config-related files
- Added comprehensive test coverage for phantom keys (prototype chain properties)
- Updated 7 files to consistently use `Object.hasOwn()` for safer property checks

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| config/plugin-commands-config/src/configGet.ts | Fixed property existence check when retrieving config by replacing `in` with `Object.hasOwn()` |
| config/plugin-commands-config/src/configSet.ts | Fixed property validation checks for INI config keys and workspace keys |
| config/plugin-commands-config/src/parseConfigPropertyPath.ts | Fixed property check when normalizing top-level config names |
| config/plugin-commands-config/test/configGet.test.ts | Added comprehensive tests verifying that prototype properties return 'undefined' |
| config/config/src/index.ts | Fixed CLI options check to prevent prototype chain traversal |
| cli/default-reporter/src/reporterForClient/index.ts | Fixed command existence check in execution time reporting |
| workspace/find-packages/src/index.ts | Fixed manifest field existence check when validating non-root projects |
| text/comments-parser/src/insertComments.ts | Fixed duplicate key detection in comment insertion logic |
| .changeset/eighty-clowns-shave.md | Added changeset documenting the fix |
</details>

  • Bump minimum CodeQL bundle version to 2.17.6. #3223

title: fix: validate that Object methods are not used on Maps
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces a custom ESLint rule to prevent using Object methods (keys, values, entries) on Map and Set objects, and fixes existing violations in the codebase. Object methods on Maps/Sets always return empty arrays since these are not plain objects, which is a common mistake.

Key changes:
- Added custom ESLint rule `no-object-methods-on-map` to detect Object.keys/values/entries usage on Map/Set types
- Fixed two instances where `Object.keys()` was incorrectly used on Map objects
- Renamed a type interface from `Map` to `RecordByString` to avoid confusion with the built-in Map type

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

title: refactor: use Maps instead of Records
author: zkochan

## Pull request overview

This PR refactors the codebase to use `Map` instead of `Record` types for file indices and related key-value collections across the entire pnpm monorepo. This change improves type safety and provides better performance for operations like checking key existence and iteration.

**Key Changes:**
- Replaced `Record<string, T>` with `Map<string, T>` for file indices (`PackageFiles`, `FilesIndex`, `FilesMap`, `SideEffects`)
- Updated server-client communication from JSON to v8 serialization to support Map objects

title: fix(git-fetcher): ensure the specified commit is used after checkout
body:
This ensures that the current `HEAD` is the requested commit.

As partial commits are supported by the resolver the consistency check is performed using prefix matching.
https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/blob/0dfa8b862b739706f2a79f7d95afad6d676bd711/resolving/git-resolver/src/index.ts#L102-L104

For an example / test case see npm/pacote#439

It also seems to me shallow cloning for partial commits wouldn't work if the shorthash is not expanded first by the resolver. This also causes the shorthash to be written to `pnpm-lock.yaml` as-is. The resolution step `resolveRefFromRefs` needs the same check as the checkout / clone if that's actually a bug - in which case the check here can be made more strict.
author: klassiker

reviews.body: add a test and a changeset

## Pull request overview

This PR fixes an issue where git-hosted packages with partial commit hashes were not being properly validated after checkout. The fix ensures that partial commits are resolved to full commit hashes and validates that the checked-out HEAD matches the expected commit.

- Resolves partial commit hashes to full 40-character hashes in the git-resolver
- Adds validation after git checkout to ensure HEAD points to the expected commit
- Adds error handling for ambiguous commit references

title: fix(deploy): omit inject workspace packages setting in deploy lockfiles
body: When the deploy command creates a new lockfile, create the deployment lockfile without the setting to inject workspace packages, because it has already been applied when creating the lockfile and the deployment is not, itself, a workspace.
author: tilgovi

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes the deploy command to omit the `injectWorkspacePackages` setting from deployment lockfiles. Since the effects of injecting workspace packages are already applied when creating the lockfile and the deployment itself is not a workspace, this setting should not be preserved in the deployed lockfile. **Key changes:** - Modified lockfile creation in `createDeployFiles.ts` to explicitly set `injectWorkspacePackages` to `undefined` in the settings object - Set `injectWorkspacePackages` to `undefined` in the install options when deploying from a shared lockfile - Added comprehensive test to verify the setting is not preserved in deployed lockfiles ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `releasing/plugin-commands-deploy/src/createDeployFiles.ts` | Adds settings object override to set `injectWorkspacePackages` to undefined when creating deploy lockfile | | `releasing/plugin-commands-deploy/src/deploy.ts` | Sets `injectWorkspacePackages` to undefined in install handler options during deployment | | `releasing/plugin-commands-deploy/test/deploy.test.ts` | Adds test case validating that `injectWorkspacePackages` setting is not preserved in deployed lockfiles, including new import for install handler | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

has already been applied when creating the lockfile and the deployment
is not, itself, a workspace.

title: feat: add custom resolvers and fetchers
body:
This is a third attempt at adding a more flexible mechanism for hooking into pnpm's resolution and fetching systems. Previous attempts: #10153, #10201

## Design

This branch adds `resolvers` and `fetchers` as top-level pnpmfile exports. (The new `fetchers` is not to be confused with `hooks.fetchers`, which this PR deprecates.) `resolvers` is an array of custom resolvers, and `fetchers` is an array of custom fetchers.

A custom resolver is an object with these methods:

* `canResolve`: Tells pnpm whether this resolver can resolve a package. If so, `resolve` and `shouldForceResolve` are called.
* `resolve`: Provides a resolution for a package.
* `shouldForceResolve` (optional): During headless install, can tell pnpm that a package needs to be re-resolved.

A custom fetcher is an object with these methods:

* `canFetch`: Tells pnpm whether this fetcher can fetch a package, based on its resolution. If so, `fetch` is called.
* `fetch`: Tells pnpm where to load a package from.

I've written [an update to the pnpmfile docs](https://github.com/TrevorBurnham/pnpm.io/blob/custom-resolver-fetcher-docs/docs/pnpmfile.md) with more details and some examples. 

### Custom resolution types

A custom resolver can return a standard resolution, or it can return a resolution with a custom `type`, in which case it needs a corresponding custom fetcher.

To avoid ambiguity, this PR introduces the convention that custom resolution types should be `@`-scoped. If a resolution has `"type": "@my-company/internal-package"`, that means that you need a custom fetcher for that type.

### Relation to hooks

Custom resolvers and fetchers could, in principle, replace `hooks.readPackage`, `hooks.preResolution`, `hooks.afterAllResolved`, and `hooks.fetchers`. They're a complete and cohesive solution to the problems that those hooks solve.

Of course, they could also peacefully coexist with the existing hooks. This PR doesn't make any changes to the existing hooks, aside from adding an `@deprecated` annotation to `hooks.fetchers`.

## Testing

I've added unit tests for all of the new hooks. However, the integration tests I've tried to add emit the error "Jest environment has been torn down" followed by a timeout. This seems to be due to an issue with Jest's dependency resolution (a cycle somewhere, maybe?), but I haven't been able to track it down. This branch includes those integration tests with `test.skip` in case others want to try to get them running.
author: TrevorBurnham

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds a flexible mechanism for custom package resolution and fetching in pnpm through new top-level `resolvers` and `fetchers` exports in pnpmfiles. Custom resolvers intercept package resolution before built-in resolvers, while custom fetchers can completely handle fetching for custom resolution types. The implementation includes a caching layer for performance, proper type safety with @-scoped custom resolution types, and the ability for custom fetchers to delegate to pnpm's standard fetchers.

Key changes:
- Introduces `CustomResolver` and `CustomFetcher` interfaces with canResolve/resolve/shouldForceResolve and canFetch/fetch methods respectively
- Adds caching mechanism for canResolve checks to avoid redundant expensive operations
- Updates pickFetcher to support async operation and custom fetcher delegation

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 49 out of 50 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| hooks/types/src/index.ts | Defines CustomResolver and CustomFetcher interfaces with comprehensive documentation |
| hooks/types/src/customResolverCache.ts | Implements WeakMap-based caching for canResolve results |
| fetching/pick-fetcher/src/index.ts | Updates pickFetcher to be async and check custom fetchers first, with proper error handling for unhandled custom resolution types |
| resolving/default-resolver/src/index.ts | Integrates custom resolvers into resolution flow, checking them before built-in resolvers |

title: fix: add CommonJS shims for compatibility with older Corepack versions
body:
Fixes #10242

As described on that ticket, attempting to use pnpm v11 with corepack < 0.34.5 fails because earlier versions of corepack hard-coded the `pnpm.cjs` path, which v11 replaces with `pnpm.mjs`.

This PR fixes the issue by adding `pnpm.cjs` (and `pnpx.cjs`) modules that simply load the ESM modules. This should provided compatibility with older corepack versions.

## Testing

I've tested this change by copying these files into my corepack cache dir and running `pnpm` under corepack 0.33.0. It works smoothly.
author: TrevorBurnham

The fix generalizes the existence check to work with any file in the package, not just `package.json`. Key changes: - Introduced `pkgExistsAtTargetDir` and `pickFileFromFilesMap` helper functions to handle packages without `package.json` - Refactored `pkgLinkedToStore` to use the new helper for consistent file selection - Updated parameter order in `pkgLinkedToStore` for consistency ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | fs/indexed-pkg-importer/src/index.ts | Refactored package existence and linking checks to support packages without `package.json` by using any available file from the filesMap | | .changeset/stupid-grapes-press.md | Added changeset documenting the bug fix for packages without `package.json` |

## Pull request overview This PR adds a `--bare` flag to the `pnpm init` command, allowing users to create an empty package.json file without the default fields that are normally populated. This addresses feature request #10226. **Key Changes:** - Added `--bare` boolean option to the `init` command that creates an empty package.json (`{}`) instead of one with default fields - Updated field priority ordering to include `private` field positioning - Added test coverage for the new `--bare` functionality ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | packages/plugin-commands-init/src/init.ts | Implements `--bare` CLI option, updates type definitions, adds help text, and modifies handler to conditionally create empty manifest | | packages/plugin-commands-init/test/init.test.ts | Adds test case verifying `--bare` creates package.json without default fields | | .changeset/brave-ties-move.md | Documents the new `--bare` feature as a minor version change |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **packages/plugin-commands-init/src/init.ts:113** * The `type` field is missing from the priority list, but it can be set via `--init-type=module` (line 93-95). This means the `type` field will appear at an inconsistent position in the generated package.json. Consider adding `'type'` to the priority list, likely after `'version'` or `'private'`, to ensure consistent field ordering. ``` const priority = Object.fromEntries([ 'name', 'version', 'private', 'description', 'main', 'scripts', 'keywords', 'author', 'license', 'packageManager', ].map((key, index) => [key, index])) ```

title: fix: trustPolicy should ignore trust evidences of prerelease versions
body: Many packages only use provenance for prerelease versions.
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes the trustPolicy feature to correctly handle prerelease versions when checking for trust downgrades. The fix ensures that when installing a stable version, the trust check ignores trust evidence from prerelease versions, which is important because many packages only use provenance for prerelease versions. - Added logic to detect whether the version being installed is a prerelease and conditionally exclude prerelease versions from trust evidence checks - Added comprehensive test coverage for the scenario where only prerelease versions have provenance attestations - Updated changeset documentation to reflect the bug fix ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | resolving/npm-resolver/src/trustChecks.ts | Added prerelease detection logic using semver.prerelease() and modified detectStrongestTrustEvidenceBeforeDate to accept excludePrerelease option | | resolving/npm-resolver/test/trustChecks.test.ts | Added test case verifying that installing a stable version doesn't throw when only prerelease versions had provenance | | .changeset/many-showers-beg.md | Added changeset documenting the bug fix for trustPolicy prerelease handling | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix(self-update): respect custom registry when installing pnpm version
body:
Fixes an issue where pnpm ignores custom registry configuration when auto-installing a specified pnpm version via `managePackageManagerVersions`.

### Problem

When `managePackageManagerVersions: true` is set in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` and a custom registry is configured in `.npmrc` (e.g., `registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com/`), pnpm attempts to auto-install the specified version from `registry.npmjs.org` instead of respecting the user's custom registry configuration.

This occurs because `installPnpmToTools` runs in a temporary directory outside the project, which doesn't automatically inherit the project's `.npmrc` configuration.

### Solution

The fix explicitly passes the registry configuration from `opts.registries.default` or `opts.rawConfig.registry` to the `pnpm add` command via the `--config.registry` flag.

### Changes

- Modified `tools/plugin-commands-self-updater/src/installPnpmToTools.ts`
- Added registry configuration check: `const registry = opts.registries?.default || opts.rawConfig?.registry`
- Pass registry to `pnpm add command when available: `command.push(\`--config.registry=${registry}\`)

## Reproduction Steps

1. Create a `.npmrc` file with a custom registry:
   ```
   registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com/
   ```

2. Configure `pnpm-workspace.yaml`:
   ```yaml
   managePackageManagerVersions: true
   ```

3. Set `package.json`:
   ```json
   {
     "packageManager": "pnpm@10.22.0"
   }
   ```

4. Run `pnpm -v` without having pnpm@10.22.0 installed
5. **Before fix**: pnpm downloads from `registry.npmjs.org` instead of the custom registry
6. **After fix**: pnpm respects the custom registry configuration

## Testing

- ✅ Code compiles successfully
- ✅ Linting passes
- ✅ Manual testing confirms the fix works as expected

⚠️ **Note:** Test cases were not added due to module mocking complexity in ESM environment. If test coverage is required, I'm happy to add tests with guidance on the following:

1. **Test setup clarification**:
   - How to properly mock `runPnpmCli` in the existing test suite?
   - Best practices for simulating registry configurations in test environment

2. **Scope of test coverage**:
   - Should tests cover both `opts.registries.default` and `opts.rawConfig.registry` fallback paths?
   - Do you need tests for scenarios where no custom registry is configured?

I'm familiar with testing strategies but would benefit from understanding this project's conventions to ensure consistency with existing test patterns.

## Related Issues

<!-- If there's a related issue, add: Closes #<issue-number> -->

## Checklist

- [x] Code follows the project's style guidelines
- [x] Self-review completed
- [x] Code compiles without errors
- [x] Linting passes
- [x] Commit message follows the project's commit message conventions

title: fix: remove redundant mirror slash

the mirror base url already ends with a slash. if the internal mirror doesn't handle duplicate slashes, it will return a 404 error:


```
ERR_PNPM_FAILED_DOWNLOAD_SHASUM_FILE  Failed to fetch integrity file:
https://internal.test/mirrors/node//v24.4.1/SHASUMS256.txt (status: 404)
                                  ^^ Error
```


other impls:
https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/blob/60f3a05064736f805a92f38a978bf58bffddf20c/env/node.resolver/src/getNodeArtifactAddress.ts#L24-L27
author: tmkx

reviews.body: ## Pull Request Overview This PR fixes a bug where Node.js mirror URLs were constructed with duplicate slashes, causing 404 errors on mirror servers that don't handle double slashes gracefully. Since the `nodeMirrorBaseUrl` is guaranteed to end with a trailing slash (via the `normalizeNodeMirror` function), the extra slash when constructing the SHASUMS256.txt URL was redundant and problematic. - Removed redundant leading slash when constructing the Node.js mirror integrity file URL - Added changeset documenting the patch fix ### Reviewed Changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `env/node.resolver/src/index.ts` | Fixed URL construction in `readNodeAssets` to avoid double slash between base URL and version path | | `.changeset/silver-geckos-hunt.md` | Added changeset entry documenting the bug fix for the duplicate slash issue | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix: update import in `@pnpm/patching.apply-patch` for ESM compatibility
body:
The `@pnpm/patching.apply-patch` package is now an ESM package as of https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/9870, but it contains an import that's missing a file extension.

It seems like Jest doesn't catch this. They're tracking at https://github.com/jestjs/jest/issues/9885.

```
~/Developer/pnpm/patching/apply-patch
❯ node .
node:internal/modules/run_main:107
    triggerUncaughtException(
    ^

Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find module '/Users/gluxon/Developer/pnpm/patching/apply-patch/node_modules/@pnpm/patch-package/dist/applyPatches' imported from /Users/gluxon/Developer/pnpm/patching/apply-patch/lib/index.js
Did you mean to import "@pnpm/patch-package/dist/applyPatches.js"?
    at finalizeResolution (node:internal/modules/esm/resolve:274:11)
    at moduleResolve (node:internal/modules/esm/resolve:864:10)
    at defaultResolve (node:internal/modules/esm/resolve:990:11)
```

title: fix: self-update should not install @pnpm/exe >= 11
body:
We have [switched pnpm to esm](https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/9870) but unfortunately as a result we cannot bundle pnpm to an executable anymore. That's because of this issue in pkg: https://github.com/yao-pkg/pkg/issues/16

As a workaround, while pkg doesn't support esm, we will install pnpm and node.js ourselves, separately. This is possible because pnpm can now install the engine runtime of the installed package: https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10141
author: zkochan

title: fix: concurrently hard linking a directory
body: close #10160
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview This pull request addresses a race condition issue where two pnpm processes simultaneously hard-linking contents of a directory to the same destination could cause crashes. The fix uses a temporary directory pattern with atomic rename operations to prevent conflicts. ### Key Changes: - Modified `hardLinkDir` to write to temporary directories first, then atomically rename to final destinations - Added dependencies on `path-temp` (v2.1.0) and `rename-overwrite` (v6.0.2) packages - Updated pnpm lock file with new dependency resolutions ### Reviewed Changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | fs/hard-link-dir/src/index.ts | Refactored to use temp directories with atomic rename for race condition prevention | | fs/hard-link-dir/package.json | Added `path-temp` and `rename-overwrite` as dependencies | | pnpm-lock.yaml | Updated lock file with new dependency resolutions and version references | | .changeset/social-peas-juggle.md | Added changeset documenting the bug fix |
Files not reviewed (1) * **pnpm-lock.yaml**: Language not supported
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

title: fix(update): prevent package.json updates when updating indirect dependencies (#5118)
body: This PR fixes the bug described at #5118 where running `pnpm update <dep>` for an indirect dependency has the side effect of also updating direct dependencies. I've included a test case.
author: TrevorBurnham

reviews.body: ## Pull Request Overview This PR implements a feature to allow updating indirect (transitive) dependencies without modifying the package.json file. When a user runs `pnpm update` on a dependency that is not directly listed in package.json, the update should only affect the lockfile and not attempt to add the dependency to package.json. - Added logic to detect when only indirect dependencies are being updated - Prevents package.json modification for indirect dependency updates - Added comprehensive test coverage for the new behavior ### Reviewed Changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | pnpm/test/update.ts | Added test case to verify that updating an indirect dependency updates the lockfile without modifying package.json | | pkg-manager/plugin-commands-installation/src/installDeps.ts | Implemented logic to detect indirect dependency updates and pass appropriate flags to prevent package.json modification | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

My local machine was unable to run tests that touches `shouldFallbackToNpm` kind-of-settings (such as `registry`, `_authToken`, etc). It was because `npm` decided to write to `~/.npmrc` instead of the specified `opts.configDir`.

The test for this change would be pushed to https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/10145 later.

v4.31.2

CodeQL Action Changelog

See the releases page for the relevant changes to the CodeQL CLI and language packs.

4.31.2 - 30 Oct 2025

No user facing changes.

See the full CHANGELOG.md for more information.

v4.31.1

CodeQL Action Changelog

See the releases page for the relevant changes to the CodeQL CLI and language packs.

4.31.1 - 30 Oct 2025

  • The add-snippets input has been removed from the analyze action. This input has been deprecated since CodeQL Action 3.26.4 in August 2024 when this removal was announced.

See the full CHANGELOG.md for more information.

v4.31.0

CodeQL Action Changelog

See the releases page for the relevant changes to the CodeQL CLI and language packs.

4.31.0 - 24 Oct 2025

  • Bump minimum CodeQL bundle version to 2.17.6. #3223
  • When SARIF files are uploaded by the analyze or upload-sarif actions, the CodeQL Action automatically performs post-processing steps to prepare the data for the upload. Previously, these post-processing steps were only performed before an upload took place. We are now changing this so that the post-processing steps will always be performed, even when the SARIF files are not uploaded. This does not change anything for the upload-sarif action. For analyze, this may affect Advanced Setup for CodeQL users who specify a value other than always for the upload input. #3222

See the full CHANGELOG.md for more information.

Changelog

Sourced from github/codeql-action's changelog.

CodeQL Action Changelog

See the releases page for the relevant changes to the CodeQL CLI and language packs.

[UNRELEASED]

No user facing changes.

4.31.2 - 30 Oct 2025

No user facing changes.

4.31.1 - 30 Oct 2025

  • The add-snippets input has been removed from the analyze action. This input has been deprecated since CodeQL Action 3.26.4 in August 2024 when this removal was announced.

4.31.0 - 24 Oct 2025

  • Bump minimum CodeQL bundle version to 2.17.6. #3223
  • When SARIF files are uploaded by the analyze or upload-sarif actions, the CodeQL Action automatically performs post-processing steps to prepare the data for the upload. Previously, these post-processing steps were only performed before an upload took place. We are now changing this so that the post-processing steps will always be performed, even when the SARIF files are not uploaded. This does not change anything for the upload-sarif action. For analyze, this may affect Advanced Setup for CodeQL users who specify a value other than always for the upload input. #3222

4.30.9 - 17 Oct 2025

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.23.3. #3205
  • Experimental: A new setup-codeql action has been added which is similar to init, except it only installs the CodeQL CLI and does not initialize a database. Do not use this in production as it is part of an internal experiment and subject to change at any time. #3204

4.30.8 - 10 Oct 2025

No user facing changes.

4.30.7 - 06 Oct 2025

  • [v4+ only] The CodeQL Action now runs on Node.js v24. #3169

3.30.6 - 02 Oct 2025

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.23.2. #3168

3.30.5 - 26 Sep 2025

  • We fixed a bug that was introduced in 3.30.4 with upload-sarif which resulted in files without a .sarif extension not getting uploaded. #3160

3.30.4 - 25 Sep 2025

  • We have improved the CodeQL Action's ability to validate that the workflow it is used in does not use different versions of the CodeQL Action for different workflow steps. Mixing different versions of the CodeQL Action in the same workflow is unsupported and can lead to unpredictable results. A warning will now be emitted from the codeql-action/init step if different versions of the CodeQL Action are detected in the workflow file. Additionally, an error will now be thrown by the other CodeQL Action steps if they load a configuration file that was generated by a different version of the codeql-action/init step. #3099 and #3100
  • We added support for reducing the size of dependency caches for Java analyses, which will reduce cache usage and speed up workflows. This will be enabled automatically at a later time. #3107
  • You can now run the latest CodeQL nightly bundle by passing tools: nightly to the init action. In general, the nightly bundle is unstable and we only recommend running it when directed by GitHub staff. #3130
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.23.1. #3118

3.30.3 - 10 Sep 2025

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview This PR adds support for reading global configuration from a `rc.yaml` file located in the user's config directory (e.g., `~/.config/pnpm/rc.yaml`). This provides an alternative way to specify pnpm settings globally, similar to how workspace settings are configured in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`. Key changes: - Introduced `GLOBAL_CONFIG_YAML_FILENAME` constant for the new `rc.yaml` config file - Extended `readWorkspaceManifest` to accept a configurable filename parameter - Refactored workspace manifest settings application logic into a reusable function - Added test coverage for the global `rc.yaml` functionality ### Reviewed Changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | packages/constants/src/index.ts | Exports new `GLOBAL_CONFIG_YAML_FILENAME` constant for the global config file | | workspace/read-manifest/src/index.ts | Adds `ConfigFileName` type and updates `readWorkspaceManifest` to accept optional filename parameter | | config/config/src/index.ts | Reads global `rc.yaml` config, refactors settings application into reusable `addSettingsFromWorkspaceManifestToConfig` function | | config/config/src/getOptionsFromRootManifest.ts | Updates `getOptionsFromPnpmSettings` to handle `undefined` manifestDir for global config | | config/config/test/index.ts | Adds test case verifying global `rc.yaml` configuration is read correctly |

title: feat(cli/config)!: config command outputs changed from INI to JSON with camelCase keys
author: KSXGitHub

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview This PR changes the output format of `pnpm config list` from INI format to JSON format. The change removes the conditional formatting based on the `--json` flag for config list operations, making JSON the default and only output format. - Removes the `ini` dependency and all INI-related formatting logic - Updates `pnpm config list` to always output JSON format (camelCase keys) - Updates `pnpm config get` to output JSON for objects/arrays (without needing `--json` flag) - Updates documentation and help text to reflect the new behavior ### Reviewed Changes Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `config/plugin-commands-config/src/configList.ts` | Simplified to always return JSON output, removed INI encoding | | `config/plugin-commands-config/src/configGet.ts` | Changed to return JSON for objects instead of INI format | | `config/plugin-commands-config/src/processConfig.ts` | Removed conditional camelCase conversion, now always converts to camelCase | | `config/plugin-commands-config/src/config.ts` | Updated help text to reflect new JSON-only behavior | | `config/plugin-commands-config/test/configList.test.ts` | Updated tests to expect JSON output with camelCase keys instead of INI | | `config/plugin-commands-config/test/configGet.test.ts` | Updated tests to expect JSON output instead of INI | | `pnpm/test/config/list.ts` | Removed INI-related tests, consolidated JSON test cases | | `.changeset/sixty-results-end.md` | Changelog entry for breaking change in config list | | `.changeset/crazy-swans-retire.md` | Changelog entry for breaking change in config get |
