title: fix(workspace.manifest-writer): preserve formatting in pnpm-workspace.yaml when updating catalogs
body:
## Description

  Fixes an issue where running commands like `pnpm update` causes unintentional formatting changes in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`.

  ## Problem

  When updating dependencies, the `catalog` section lost its original quoting style even when values remained unchanged:

  **Before `pnpm update`:**
  ```yaml
  catalog:
    'zod': '3.24.3'
    "lodash": "4.17.21"
```

  **After `pnpm update`:**
```
  catalog:
    zod: 3.24.3
    lodash: 4.17.21
```

 ## Root Cause

  The issue was in the addCatalogs function (workspace/manifest-writer/src/index.ts):

  1. shouldBeUpdated was set to true unconditionally, even when no values changed
  2. This triggered unnecessary file rewrites
  3. Although patchDocument preserves formatting, values were overwritten in memory before comparison, causing new nodes to be created without original quote styles

##  Solution

  Modified addCatalogs to only flag updates when necessary:

  - Check if targetCatalog[dependencyName] differs from the new specifier before updating
  - Set shouldBeUpdated = true only when:
    - A value actually changes, OR
    - A new catalog is being added

  This prevents unnecessary in-memory modifications, allowing patchDocument to preserve original YAML formatting.

  Closes #10425
author: prgmr99

reviews.body: I checked locally and your change is not doing anything. The quotes are currently preserved without your fix. I even added a test.

title: fix(npm-resolver): request full metadata for optional dependencies
body:
close https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/9950

Biome correctly declares the `os`, `cpu`, and `libc` fields in each platform specific package[^1].

[^1]: https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/7442#issuecomment-3265414662

For context, the npm registry provides two types of metadata responses. Full metadata (`application/json`) includes the `libc` field, while Abbreviated metadata (`application/vnd.npm.install-v1+json`) does not.

Since pnpm uses abbreviated metadata by default, the `libc` field may be `undefined`. As a result, the check for `libc` in `checkPlatform()` can be skipped, making it impossible to distinguish between musl and glibc. To address this, adding a flag to request fullMetadata for each registry request allows pnpm to consistently obtain complete platform information and resolve `optionalDependencies` correctly. This change will prevent unsupported versions, such as `@biomejs/cli-linux-x64-musl`, from being erroneously downloaded on glibc systems.

You can verify the behavior by clearing pnpm's cache and running `pnpm install` in the directory containing the following `package.json`. The examples below were tested on Ubuntu 24.04.

```json
{
  "name": "sandbox-pnpm",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "",
  "main": "index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1"
  },
  "keywords": [],
  "author": "",
  "license": "ISC",
  "packageManager": "pnpm@11.0.0-alpha.2",
  "type": "module",
  "devDependencies": {
    "@biomejs/biome": "2.3.11"
  }
}
```

Before:

```plaintext
❯ pnpm install
Packages: +3
+++
Downloading @biomejs/cli-linux-x64@2.3.11: 16.70 MB/16.70 MB, completed
Downloading @biomejs/cli-linux-x64-musl@2.3.11: 16.71 MB/16.71 MB, completed
Progress: resolved 9, reused 0, downloaded 3, added 3, completed

devDependencies:
+ @biomejs/biome 2.3.11

Completed in 8.7s using pnpm v11.0.0-alpha.2
```

After:

```plaintext
❯ /path/to/pnpm/pnpm/bin/pnpm.mjs install
Packages: +2
++
Progress: resolved 9, reused 0, downloaded 2, added 2, completed

devDependencies:
+ @biomejs/biome 2.3.11

Downloading @biomejs/cli-linux-x64@2.3.11: 16.70 MB/16.70 MB, completed
Completed in 5.1s using pnpm v11.0.0-alpha.2
```
author: 3w36zj6

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes an issue where pnpm was incorrectly downloading both glibc and musl versions of optional dependencies on glibc systems due to missing `libc` field information in abbreviated metadata.

**Changes:**
- Added support for requesting full metadata for optional dependencies to obtain the `libc` field needed for proper platform filtering
- Modified cache key generation to include `metaDir` to prevent collisions between abbreviated and full metadata
- Propagated the `optional` flag from package requester through to the npm resolver

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>

| ---- | ----------- |

| resolving/npm-resolver/test/resolveFromNpm.test.ts | Added comprehensive integration tests for full metadata usage with optional dependencies |
| resolving/npm-resolver/test/pickPackage.test.ts | Added unit tests for cache key separation and fullMetadata flag propagation |
| resolving/npm-resolver/src/pickPackage.ts | Modified cache key to include metaDir and updated fetch call to pass fullMetadata flag |
| resolving/npm-resolver/src/index.ts | Added logic to use FULL_META_DIR for optional dependencies and pass optional flag through context |
| resolving/npm-resolver/src/fetch.ts | Added fullMetadata parameter to fetch function signature |
| pkg-manager/package-requester/test/index.ts | Added test to verify optional flag is passed to resolve function |
| pkg-manager/package-requester/src/packageRequester.ts | Modified to pass optional flag from WantedDependency to resolve options |
| network/fetching-types/src/index.ts | Added fullMetadata field to FetchFromRegistry options |

title: fix(run): fail when no packages have script in filtered recursive run
body:
Makes `pnpm run -r <script>` and `pnpm run --filter <filter> <script>` fail with exit code 1 when no packages have the specified script, aligning behavior with the documented expectation.

Previously, using a filter would cause the command to silently succeed (exit 0) even when no packages had the script. This was inconsistent with the documentation which states: "If none of the packages have the command, the command fails."

The `--if-present` flag can be used as an escape hatch when the error should be suppressed.

## Changes

- Modified `runRecursive.ts` to throw `ERR_PNPM_RECURSIVE_RUN_NO_SCRIPT` when no packages have the script (unless `--if-present` is set)
- Updated tests to verify the new error behavior
- Added changeset documenting the behavior change

## Verification

**Environment:** Darwin (arm64)  
**Runtime:** Node 24.11.1

### Test Steps
```bash
cd exec/plugin-commands-script-runners
pnpm run _test -- test/runRecursive.ts
```

### Output
```
Test Suites: 1 passed, 1 total
Tests:       18 passed, 18 total
```

Fixes #6844
author: OlegHQ

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes an inconsistency where `pnpm run -r <script>` and `pnpm run --filter <filter> <script>` would silently succeed (exit 0) when run with a filter and no packages had the specified script. The fix ensures these commands fail with exit code 1 when no packages have the script, aligning the behavior with the documented expectation and with the behavior when all packages are selected.

**Changes:**
- Modified error handling in `runRecursive.ts` to throw an error when no packages have the script regardless of filter scope
- Removed logger import and the info-level logging that was previously used for filtered runs with no matching scripts
- Updated test to verify both success with `--if-present` flag and failure without it

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |

| exec/plugin-commands-script-runners/test/runRecursive.ts | Updated test name and added assertions to verify the new error behavior with and without `--if-present` flag |
| .changeset/fix-recursive-run-no-script.md | Added changeset documenting the breaking behavior change |

```

```
</details>

commits.message:
fix(run): fail when no packages have script in filtered recursive run

Previously, `pnpm run -r <script>` and `pnpm run --filter <filter> <script>`
would silently succeed with exit code 0 when no packages had the specified
script, as long as a filter was used. This was inconsistent with the
documentation which states "If none of the packages have the command, the
command fails."

This change makes the command fail with ERR_PNPM_RECURSIVE_RUN_NO_SCRIPT in
all cases where no packages have the script, regardless of whether a filter
is used. The `--if-present` flag can be used to suppress this error.

title: refactor: move out skip resolution logic from package requester

This PR refactors the package resolution optimization logic by moving it from the package-requester into the individual resolvers. The key motivation is to simplify the package-requester and allow resolvers to handle their own caching/skip strategies.

**Changes:**
- Moved manifest peeking optimization from package-requester to npm-resolver, which now checks the store directly when storeDir is provided

title: fix: forward `$NODE_OPTIONS` when running jest for debug terminals
body:
## Motivation

I recently noticed test breakpoints no longer bind when running Jest tests in this repo.

## Changes

Many Node.js debugging tools (like the VS Code debug terminal) work by [setting `NODE_OPTIONS` in new terminal sessions](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/nodejs/nodejs-debugging#_how-can-i-set-nodeoptions).

Let's pass through existing `NODE_OPTIONS` in our `package.json` test scripts, which is what the Jest docs recommend in step 2 here.

https://jestjs.io/docs/ecmascript-modules

> Execute `node` with `--experimental-vm-modules`, e.g. `node --experimental-vm-modules node_modules/jest/bin/jest.js` or `NODE_OPTIONS="$NODE_OPTIONS --experimental-vm-modules" npx jest` etc.

This fixed the issue I was seeing and breakpoints now work again!
author: gluxon

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR enables debugger breakpoints in Jest tests by forwarding existing `NODE_OPTIONS` environment variables when running test scripts across the monorepo. - Updates test script configurations to preserve existing `NODE_OPTIONS` while adding `--experimental-vm-modules` - Modifies the meta-updater tool to generate the correct script format for future package updates - Follows Jest documentation recommendations for ESM support with debugging tools ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 127 out of 127 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `.meta-updater/src/index.ts` | Updated meta-updater to generate test scripts that forward `NODE_OPTIONS` | | Multiple `package.json` files (100+ packages) | Changed `_test` scripts to use `NODE_OPTIONS="$NODE_OPTIONS --experimental-vm-modules"` pattern | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: chore: enable TypeScript's `erasableSyntaxOnly` config
body:
## Motivation

Enabling TypeScript's `erasableSyntaxOnly` should allow us to:

- Remove `ts-node` in favor of running Node.js directly.
- Remove `ts-jest`.

Both of these will speed up the build processes in the pnpm repo and reduce the amount of custom setup required. I'll explore these in a future PR, but I think it's worth enabling `erasableSyntaxOnly` now as an incremental step.

## Changes

Configuring `erasableSyntaxOnly` required:

- Removing visibility modifiers like `public`/`private` from constructor parameters.

   ```ts
   ../reviewing/dependencies-hierarchy/src/getTree.ts:243:24 - error TS1294: This syntax is not allowed when 'erasableSyntaxOnly' is enabled.

   243   private constructor (private readonly keypath: readonly string[]) {}
                              ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
   ```

- Removing enums, which JavaScript doesn't natively have.

   ```ts
   ../pkg-manager/plugin-commands-installation/src/import/index.ts:66:6 - error TS1294: This syntax is not allowed when 'erasableSyntaxOnly' is enabled.
   
   66 enum YarnLockType {
           ~~~~~~~~~~~~
   ```
author: gluxon

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR enables TypeScript's `erasableSyntaxOnly` compiler option to simplify the build toolchain. This requires removing TypeScript-specific syntax that cannot be erased during compilation, specifically enum declarations and visibility modifiers on constructor parameters. **Key changes:** - Replaced enum declarations with const objects and type unions - Converted constructor parameter properties to explicit property declarations and assignments - Enabled `erasableSyntaxOnly` in the TypeScript configuration ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

title: fix: install absolute path pkg failed
body:
When a project has a `pnpm-workspace.yaml` file that contains the "packages" field, and I try to install dependencies locally on a disk that is located on another disk, the installation fails because the obtained path is incorrect.

For example, if I install `E:/test/b` in the `a` directory of the `D` drive, the final path is `D:\a\E:\test\b\`. (windows)

Display deprecation info for the latest version in table and list formats, consistent with JSON output.

```shell
# Before:
  │ @types/yup (dev) │ 0.29.14 │ 0.32.0 │

# After:
  │ @types/yup (dev) │ 0.29.14 │ 0.32.0 (deprecated) │
```
author: ryo-manba

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes issue #8658 by adding deprecation information to the table and list output formats when displaying outdated packages. Previously, deprecation status was only visible in JSON output or when packages had no semver diff. Now, when a package has a version update available and is deprecated, the Latest column will show the version number followed by a red "(deprecated)" label. - Adds deprecation text to version strings when latest version is deprecated - Includes unit test for the new renderLatest behavior with deprecated packages - Updates changeset documentation ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | reviewing/plugin-commands-outdated/src/outdated.ts | Adds conditional logic to append "(deprecated)" in red to the version text when latestManifest.deprecated is truthy | | reviewing/plugin-commands-outdated/test/renderLatest.test.ts | New test file verifying that renderLatest includes deprecation text and proper chalk styling | | .changeset/fix-outdated-deprecation-display.md | Changeset documenting the patch-level fix with reference to issue #8658 | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix: installation failed due to installation link redirection (v11)
body:
In version `11`, the `node-fetch` version was changed from `npm:@pnpm/node-fetch@1.0.0` to `^3.3.2`, causing the `location` header to return a relative path when installing dependencies via `pkg.pr.new`, thus preventing `new URL(location)` from parsing. 

Therefore, for compatibility, a special conversion for relative paths is implemented to ensure correct logic.

```
pnpm add https://pkg.pr.new/vue@14175
```
author: btea

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes a bug in the redirect handling logic within `fetchFromRegistry` where relative path URLs in the `location` header (starting with `/`) would fail to parse when constructing a new URL. The fix specifically handles the case where `pkg.pr.new` returns relative paths in redirect responses, which became an issue after upgrading from `npm:@pnpm/node-fetch@1.0.0` to `node-fetch ^3.3.2` in version 11. **Key Changes:** - Added relative path detection and conversion to absolute URLs before parsing redirect locations - Added a changeset documenting the fix ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | network/fetch/src/fetchFromRegistry.ts | Adds logic to detect relative path redirects (starting with `/`) and convert them to absolute URLs by prepending the protocol and host from the current URL | | .changeset/funny-melons-pay.md | Documents the fix as a major version change for `@pnpm/fetch` | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

author: VillainsRule

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes npm compatibility for handling redirecting tarballs by ensuring package resolution IDs use the original requested URL rather than the redirected URL. This aligns pnpm's behavior with npm and bun when dealing with tarball dependencies that redirect (e.g., from a registry URL to a CDN). **Key Changes:** - Modified the tarball resolver to use the original `bareSpecifier` URL for package IDs and normalized specifiers, while still using the redirected URL for the actual tarball download location - Updated test cases to use `https://` instead of `http://` for registry URLs, reflecting current registry behavior ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | resolving/tarball-resolver/src/index.ts | Changed to use `wantedDependency.bareSpecifier` for `id` and `normalizedBareSpecifier` fields instead of the redirected `resolvedUrl`, ensuring stable package identifiers | | resolving/tarball-resolver/test/index.ts | Updated test URLs from `http://` to `https://` for npm registry and JSR registry test cases | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. title: fix: normalize tarball URLs by removing default HTTP/HTTPS ports
body: Closes https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/6725
author: minijus

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR fixes an issue where tarball URLs with default ports (`:80` for HTTP and `:443` for HTTPS) were being saved to the lockfile. The solution normalizes these URLs by removing the default ports to ensure consistency across different registry configurations. **Key Changes:** - Introduces a `normalizeRegistryUrl` function that leverages the WHATWG URL API to automatically strip default ports - Applies URL normalization to tarball URLs in both NPM and JSR registry resolution flows - Adds comprehensive unit tests and an integration test to verify the behavior ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

title: fix(git-fetcher): block git dependencies from running prepare scripts unless allowed
body:
## Summary
- Git-hosted dependencies now respect the `onlyBuiltDependencies` allowlist before running prepare/prepublish/prepack scripts
- Fixes security bypass where git dependencies could execute scripts during fetch phase, circumventing v10's "scripts disabled by default" feature

## Test plan
- [x] Added tests for blocking git packages when not in allowlist
- [x] Added tests for allowing git packages when explicitly in allowlist
- [x] Existing git-fetcher tests pass
author: orenyomtov

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR implements a critical security fix by blocking git-hosted dependencies from executing prepare/prepublish/prepack scripts during the fetch phase unless they are explicitly allowed via the `onlyBuiltDependencies` configuration. This prevents a security bypass in pnpm v10's "scripts disabled by default" feature where git dependencies could circumvent the allowlist.

**Key Changes:**
- Introduced `allowBuild` parameter threading through the entire dependency resolution and fetching chain
- Added validation in `preparePackage` to enforce the allowlist before executing any build scripts
- Updated tests to verify both blocking and allowing behaviors for git dependencies with scripts

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 18 out of 19 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| `pnpm-lock.yaml` | Moved `@pnpm/types` from devDependencies to dependencies for tarball-fetcher |
| `pkg-manager/resolve-dependencies/src/resolveDependencyTree.ts` | Added `allowBuild` to `ResolveDependenciesOptions` and threaded it through the resolution context |
| `pkg-manager/resolve-dependencies/src/resolveDependencies.ts` | Added `allowBuild` to `ResolutionContext` and passed it when requesting packages |
| `pkg-manager/package-requester/src/packageRequester.ts` | Passed `allowBuild` to `fetchPackageToStore` and `fetchFromTarball` calls |
| `pkg-manager/headless/src/lockfileToHoistedDepGraph.ts` | Added `allowBuild` parameter support for headless installations |
| `pkg-manager/headless/src/index.ts` | Created `allowBuild` function earlier in the flow and passed it to dependency graph operations |
| `pkg-manager/core/src/install/index.ts` | Moved `al

title: fix(tarball-resolver): add integrity hash to HTTP tarball dependencies
body:
HTTP tarball dependencies are now resolved with an integrity hash computed from the tarball content, ensuring the lockfile can verify that the same content is downloaded on subsequent installs.
This prevents remote servers from serving different content to different users.
author: orenyomtov

reviews.body:
This is not a good solution. Now every tarball dependency will be downloaded twice.

The tarball dependencies are already fetched at resolution because pnpm needs to read their package.json file: https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/blob/05fb1aee5f6c40fb876564ece90b04174a327e12/pkg-manager/package-requester/src/packageRequester.ts#L319

So we can get the integrity from the fetcher and add it to the resolution object: https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/blob/05fb1aee5f6c40fb876564ece90b04174a327e12/pkg-manager/package-requester/src/packageRequester.ts#L329

commits.message: fix(tarball-resolver): add integrity hash to HTTP tarball dependencies
Refactor to download tarball just once
Refactor to download tarball just once
Fix tests
fix: only calc hash when it is not passed in to the fetcher
docs: update changesets

  • CodeQL Action v3 will be deprecated in December 2026. The Action now logs a warning for customers who are running v3 but could be running v4. For more information, see Upcoming deprecation of CodeQL Action v3.
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.23.5. #3288

No user facing changes.

4.31.1 - 30 Oct 2025

  • The add-snippets input has been removed from the analyze action. This input has been deprecated since CodeQL Action 3.26.4 in August 2024 when this removal was announced.

4.31.0 - 24 Oct 2025

  • Bump minimum CodeQL bundle version to 2.17.6. #3223
  • When SARIF files are uploaded by the analyze or upload-sarif actions, the CodeQL Action automatically performs post-processing steps to prepare the data for the upload. Previously, these post-processing steps were only performed before an upload took place. We are now changing this so that the post-processing steps will always be performed, even when the SARIF files are not uploaded. This does not change anything for the upload-sarif action. For analyze, this may affect Advanced Setup for CodeQL users who specify a value other than always for the upload input. #3222

4.30.9 - 17 Oct 2025

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.23.3. #3205
  • Experimental: A new setup-codeql action has been added which is similar to init, except it only installs the CodeQL CLI and does not initialize a database. Do not use this in production as it is part of an internal experiment and subject to change at any time. #3204

title: fix(publish): respect --force flag in recursive publish
body:
## Fix: Respect `--force` flag in recursive publish

Fixes #10272

### Problem

When using `pnpm -r publish --force`, the `--force` flag was being ignored. This caused packages to not be republished even when the flag was explicitly provided, breaking workflows that rely on force-publishing (e.g., when a `prepublishOnly` script bumps the version).

### Root Cause

In [recursivePublish.ts](cci:7://file:///c:/Users/aaron/Documents/pnpm/releasing/plugin-commands-publishing/src/recursivePublish.ts:0:0-0:0), the `--force` flag was checked at line 79 to determine which packages should be included in the publish queue (bypassing the "already published" check). However, when constructing the arguments to pass to individual [publish()](cci:1://file:///c:/Users/aaron/Documents/pnpm/releasing/plugin-commands-publishing/src/publish.ts:175:0-292:1) calls, the `--force` flag was not included in the `appendedArgs` array (lines 95-107).

This meant:
- ✅ Recursive publish correctly identified packages to publish when `force=true`
- ❌ Individual publish commands didn't receive the `--force` flag
- ❌ The actual npm publish could still fail or skip already-published packages

### Solution

Added the `--force` flag to the `appendedArgs` array (lines 102-104):

```typescript
if (opts.force) {
  appendedArgs.push('--force')
}
author: PR3C14D0

commits.message:
fix(publish): respect --force flag in recursive publish

When using 'pnpm -r publish --force', the --force flag was being
ignored. The flag was checked to determine which packages to publish,
but wasn't passed to individual publish commands.

This adds --force to the appendedArgs array so it gets passed through
to each publish call, following the same pattern as other CLI flags
like --access, --dry-run, and --otp.

body: close #10270
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a bug where optional dependencies were being silently skipped when they couldn't be resolved due to `minimumReleaseAge` settings, which is a security/policy configuration. The fix introduces a new error code `ERR_PNPM_NO_MATURE_MATCHING_VERSION` to distinguish maturity-related resolution failures from general resolution failures, ensuring users are properly notified when maturity policies prevent dependency resolution.

### Key Changes
- Introduced `ERR_PNPM_NO_MATURE_MATCHING_VERSION` error code to distinguish maturity constraint failures from general resolution failures
- Updated optional dependency error handling to exclude the new error code from silent skipping
- Maintained consistent error reporting for both old and new error codes

title: feat!: support lowercase options in pnpm add (-d, -p, -o, -e)
body:
Fixes #9197 

Add lowercase aliases for `pnpm add` options:
- `-d` as an alias for `-D` (--save-dev)
- `-p` as an alias for `-P` (--save-prod)
- `-o` as an alias for `-O` (--save-optional)
- `-e` as an alias for `-E` (--save-exact)

---

### Notes 

The `-p` and `-d` shorthands will override their global meanings when used with `pnpm add`:
- `pnpm add -p` → `--save-prod` (not `--parseable` anymore)
- `pnpm add -d` → `--save-dev` (not `--loglevel=info` anymore)

Is this acceptable? Thoughts?
author: ryo-manba

title: feat: add support for npm package trust evidence check via a new trustPolicy setting
body:
Fixes #8889 

Added support for npm package attestation check.
When `trustPolicy` is set to `no-downgrade`, installation will fail if provenance downgrade is detected.

Detect provenance downgrading:
- From trusted publisher to provenance only
- From trusted publisher to none
- From provenance only to none

### References

- https://docs.npmjs.com/trusted-publishers
- https://github.com/antfu-collective/taze/pull/198
- https://github.com/antfu/fast-npm-meta/pull/20
author: ryo-manba

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR adds support for npm package attestation checking in pnpm. The feature allows pnpm to verify that packages maintain or improve their attestation level (e.g., trusted publisher, provenance) over time and fail installations if a downgrade in attestation is detected.

Key changes:
- Introduces a new `attestationCheck` boolean configuration option across the dependency resolution pipeline
- Implements provenance downgrade detection logic that compares trust evidence between package versions
- Adds comprehensive test coverage for provenance checking functionality

body: close #9965
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview This PR migrates pnpm's global store and cache from JSON-based storage to V8 binary serialization format. The change introduces a new package `@pnpm/fs.v8-file` that provides utilities for reading/writing V8 serialized files, replacing the previous `load-json-file` dependency across the codebase. File extensions are changed from `.json` to `.v8` throughout the store and cache. - Introduces new `@pnpm/fs.v8-file` package with V8 serialization utilities - Replaces JSON file operations with V8 serialization across all store and cache operations - Updates file extensions from `.json` to `.v8` in store paths and test expectations ### Reviewed Changes Copilot reviewed 62 out of 63 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

author: KSXGitHub

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR implements breaking changes to how pnpm handles configuration settings, moving workspace-specific settings from `.npmrc` files to `pnpm-workspace.yaml` files, and standardizing the naming conventions between the two formats. The changes focus on separating concerns between npm-compatible settings (in `.npmrc`) and pnpm-specific workspace settings (in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`).

**Key changes:**
- Configuration settings now strictly enforce that workspace-specific settings must be in camelCase in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, while rc options remain in kebab-case
- Auth-related information is now censored in `pnpm config list` and `pnpm config get` commands
- Array values returned by `pnpm config get` are now properly formatted as JSON arrays instead of comma-separated strings

### Reviewed Changes

title: feat: add support for exact versions in `minimumReleaseAgeExclude`
body:
Fixes #9985

Allows specifying exact versions like `axios@1.12.2` in `minimumReleaseAgeExclude` to bypass `minimumReleaseAge` filtering for manually verified packages.

Semver ranges (`^`, `~`, `>=`) are intentionally rejected for security.
Users must verify each version individually to prevent unverified versions from being automatically included.
author: ryo-manba

reviews.body:

title: fix: only show save catalog skip warning when using `pnpm add --save-catalog`
body:
## Context

Fixes https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/9662.

This warning was originally added in:

- https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/9484

Looking through the original PR, I think the intention of this warning was to only print when running `pnpm add --save-catalog`.

## Changes

Let's gate this warning around `saveCatalogName != null`. I suspect this was the original intention in https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/9484. CC @KSXGitHub to confirm.

## Why did this false positive?

This warning prints whenever:

https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/blob/904ae1a87589e755a7a4c13f3b083ac244bb406e/pkg-manager/resolve-dependencies/src/resolveDependencyTree.ts#L255

I noticed a few cases when `normalizedBareSpecifier` will be different than the specifier defined in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`.

- When there's no `pnpm-lock.yaml` file, `normalizedBareSpecifier` will be the latest version of the package. (Originally noted by @zkochan here:
  - https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/9662#issuecomment-3364790040)
- The `normalizedBareSpecifier` value can also be `undefined`. For example, when resolution is skipped, this value will never be set.
  - https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/blob/bdbd31aa4fa6546d65b6eee50a79b51879340d40/pkg-manager/package-requester/src/packageRequester.ts#L191
author: gluxon

commits.message: fix: only show save catalog skip warning when using `pnpm add`

title: fix: remove rimrafSync in importIndexedDir fast-path error handler
body:
## Summary

- Remove `rimrafSync(newDir)` from the fast-path error handler in `importIndexedDir`
- When parallel processes import the same package into the global virtual store, a transient error in one process's fast path would trigger `rimrafSync` on the shared directory, deleting files that other processes already completed or are actively reading
- The staging path that follows already handles replacing the directory correctly via `renameOverwriteSync`, making the rimraf unnecessary

Fixes flaky "parallel dlx calls of the same package" test where concurrent installs to the shared GVS could leave partially-populated directories.

## Test plan

- [ ] Existing tests pass (especially `parallel dlx calls of the same package` in `test/dlx.ts`)
- [ ] The flaky test should no longer fail due to `ENOENT` / `Cannot find module` errors from the GVS
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR adjusts the `importIndexedDir()` fast-path failure handling to avoid destructively deleting the target directory during transient/concurrent import errors, which is particularly important when multiple processes import the same package into the shared global virtual store. **Changes:** - Removed `rimrafSync(newDir)` from the fast-path error handler so a failing importer doesn’t delete a directory another process may have completed or is reading. - Rely on the existing staging path (`renameOverwriteSync` / safe-to-skip logic) to converge the directory contents without the fast-path cleanup step. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: remove rimrafSync in importIndexedDir fast-path error handler

When parallel processes import the same package into the global virtual
store, a transient error in one process's fast path would trigger
rimrafSync on the shared directory, deleting files that other processes
already completed or are actively reading. The staging path that follows
already handles replacing the directory correctly via renameOverwriteSync.

test: add test for fast-path failure not deleting populated GVS directory

Simulates a transient I/O error during the fast-path import while another
process has already populated the target directory. Verifies that the
directory is preserved and the staging path recovers correctly.

title: perf: use abbreviated metadata for minimumReleaseAge
body:
## Summary

- Fetches abbreviated package metadata instead of full when `minimumReleaseAge` is set, checking the top-level `modified` field to determine if all versions are mature
- Only re-fetches full metadata for the rare case of recently-modified packages, avoiding the larger payload for the vast majority of packages
- Uses `fs.stat()` mtime for cache freshness checks instead of reading and parsing the JSON file

## How it works

1. **Cache check**: `stat()` the cache file — if `mtime >= now - minimumReleaseAge`, use the cached metadata without a network request
2. **Fetch abbreviated**: small payload, often served from CDN
3. **Check `modified` field**: if `modified < now - minimumReleaseAge`, all versions are old enough — done (common case)
4. **Re-fetch full** (rare): only when the package was recently modified and per-version timestamps are needed to filter

## Test plan

- [x] Existing `publishedBy` tests pass (full metadata path unchanged)
- [x] New test: abbreviated metadata with old `modified` → resolves without full fetch
- [x] New test: abbreviated metadata with recent `modified` → triggers full re-fetch
- [x] New test: cached file with recent mtime → uses cache without network
- [x] All 137 npm-resolver tests pass
- [x] pkg-metadata-filter tests pass
- [x] All affected packages compile cleanly
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR optimizes `minimumReleaseAge`/`publishedBy` resolution by defaulting to abbreviated registry metadata and only fetching full metadata when needed, plus switching cache freshness checks from reading JSON to using the cache file’s `mtime`. **Changes:** - Stop forcing `fullMetadata` solely due to `minimumReleaseAge` in store controller and `dlx`, enabling abbreviated fetches. - Extend `PackageMeta` with an optional top-level `modified` field and use it to decide whether a full metadata refetch is necessary. - Use `fs.stat()` `mtime` to decide when cached metadata is fresh enough to skip network I/O, and add tests for abbreviated/refetch/mtime behavior. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | store/connection-manager/src/createNewStoreController.ts | No longer forces full metadata when `minimumReleaseAge` is set. | | exec/commands/src/dlx.ts | Same `fullMetadata` selection change as store controller. | | resolving/registry/types/src/index.ts | Adds `modified?: string` to the `PackageMeta` type for abbreviated metadata support. | | resolving/npm-resolver/src/pickPackageFromMeta.ts | Uses `modified` to decide whether abbreviated metadata is sufficient for `publishedBy`. | | resolving/npm-resolver/src/pickPackage.ts | Adds mtime-based cache freshness and conditional full-metadata refetch after abbreviated fetch. | | resolving/npm-resolver/test/publishedBy.test.ts | Adds tests for abbreviated metadata path, full refetch path, and mtime cache shortcut. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

commits.message:
perf: use abbreviated metadata for minimumReleaseAge when possible

Instead of always fetching full package metadata when minimumReleaseAge
is set, fetch the smaller abbreviated document first and check the
top-level `modified` field. If the package was last modified before the
release age cutoff, all versions are mature and no per-version time
filtering is needed. Only re-fetch full metadata for the rare case of
recently-modified packages.

Also uses fs.stat() to check cache file mtime instead of reading and
parsing the JSON to check cachedAt, avoiding unnecessary I/O.

fix: validate modified date and handle abbreviated metadata edge cases

- Validate meta.modified date to prevent invalid dates from bypassing
  minimumReleaseAge filtering
- Skip full metadata refetch for packages excluded by publishedByExclude
- Allow ERR_PNPM_MISSING_TIME from cached abbreviated metadata to fall
  through to the network fetch path instead of throwing

fix: cache abbreviated metadata before re-fetching full metadata

Save the abbreviated metadata to disk before re-fetching full metadata
so subsequent runs benefit from the mtime cache fast-path.
fix: resolve type narrowing for conditional metadata fetch result

title: perf: use If-Modified-Since for conditional metadata fetches
body:
## Summary

- Sends `If-Modified-Since` header with the local cache file's mtime when fetching package metadata from the registry
- If the registry responds with **304 Not Modified**, reads the local cache file instead of downloading the response body
- Registries that don't support conditional requests simply return 200 as before — no behavior change

## How it works

1. `stat()` the local cache file to get its mtime
2. Send `GET` with `If-Modified-Since: <mtime>` header
3. **304** → read local cache, put in memory cache, done (no body transfer)
4. **200** → use response body as before

## Changes

- `fetching/types`: Added `ifModifiedSince` option to `FetchFromRegistry`
- `network/fetch`: Pass `If-Modified-Since` header when option is set
- `resolving/npm-resolver/fetch.ts`: Handle 304 status, return `notModified` discriminant
- `resolving/npm-resolver/pickPackage.ts`: Stat cache file, pass mtime, handle 304

## Test plan

- [x] New test: 304 response uses local cache
- [x] New test: 200 response fetches fresh metadata  
- [x] New test: no cache file skips If-Modified-Since
- [x] All 137 npm-resolver tests pass
- [x] All affected packages compile cleanly
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR adds conditional registry metadata fetching to reduce response body transfers by sending cache-derived validators (ETag / If-Modified-Since) and reusing on-disk metadata when the registry returns **304 Not Modified**. **Changes:** - Extend registry fetch types and implementation to support `If-None-Match` and `If-Modified-Since` request headers. - Update npm-resolver fetch/pick flow to handle 304 responses and persist ETag in the metadata cache. - Add npm-resolver tests covering the 304 path and basic ETag persistence. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 7 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 7 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | resolving/registry/types/src/index.ts | Extends `PackageMeta` with cache validator fields (`modified`, `etag`). | | fetching/types/src/index.ts | Adds optional conditional-request options to `FetchFromRegistry`. | | network/fetch/src/fetchFromRegistry.ts | Sends `if-none-match` / `if-modified-since` headers when provided. | | resolving/npm-resolver/src/fetch.ts | Plumbs conditional headers into requests and returns a discriminated 304 result; captures ETag. | | resolving/npm-resolver/src/pickPackage.ts | Loads cached meta for validators, handles 304 by returning disk cache, and persists ETag. | | resolving/npm-resolver/test/ifModifiedSince.test.ts | New tests for 304 behavior and cache persistence. | | deps/inspection/commands/src/view/index.ts | Updates caller to handle the new 304-capable return type. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

commits.message:
perf: use If-Modified-Since for conditional metadata fetches

Before fetching package metadata from the registry, stat the local cache
file and send its mtime as an If-Modified-Since header. If the registry
returns 304 Not Modified, read the local cache instead of downloading
the full response body. This saves bandwidth and latency for packages
whose metadata hasn't changed since the last fetch.

Registries that don't support If-Modified-Since simply return 200 as
before, so there is no behavior change for unsupported registries.
fix: handle notModified union type in view command

perf: use ETag and Last-Modified for conditional metadata fetches

Store ETag and Last-Modified response headers from the registry alongside
cached metadata. On subsequent fetches, send them back as If-None-Match
and If-Modified-Since headers. If the registry returns 304 Not Modified,
use the local cache without downloading the response body.

Both headers are sent for maximum compatibility — ETags are preferred by
CDNs (npm uses Cloudflare/Fastly) while Last-Modified is a fallback for
registries that don't support ETags.

Registries that don't support conditional requests simply return 200 as
before, so there is no behavior change for unsupported registries.

refactor: use existing modified field for If-Modified-Since

Instead of storing a separate lastModified field from the HTTP header,
derive If-Modified-Since from the modified field already present in the
package metadata (abbreviated doc top-level, full doc time.modified).
This avoids redundancy since they represent the same value.

fix: bump cachedAt on 304, assert headers in tests

- Update cachedAt on 304 so publishedBy cache checks see the entry as
  freshly validated
- Assert If-None-Match header is sent in 304 test
- Fix test name to match what it actually validates

title: perf: optimize undici connection settings and tarball buffering
body:
## Summary

- Enable Happy Eyeballs (`autoSelectFamily`) for faster dual-stack (IPv4/IPv6) connection establishment
- Increase keep-alive timeouts (30s idle, 10min max) to reduce connection churn during install
- Set optimized global dispatcher so requests without custom options still benefit
- Pre-allocate `SharedArrayBuffer` for tarball downloads when `Content-Length` is known, avoiding intermediate chunk array and double-copy

## Test plan

- [x] `@pnpm/network.fetch` tests pass (36/36)
- [x] `@pnpm/fetching.tarball-fetcher` tests pass (21/21)
- [x] Benchmark via CI
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Optimizes pnpm’s network and tarball download performance by tuning undici dispatcher defaults (Happy Eyeballs + longer keep-alive) and reducing tarball buffering overhead when `Content-Length` is available. **Changes:** - Sets an optimized undici global dispatcher and updates per-dispatcher keep-alive / dual-stack connection settings. - Improves tarball download buffering by pre-allocating the destination buffer when `Content-Length` is known. - Adds a changeset to release the performance improvements. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | network/fetch/src/dispatcher.ts | Configures global undici dispatcher and updates Agent settings (keep-alive + Happy Eyeballs) for non-proxy and SOCKS dispatchers. | | fetching/tarball-fetcher/src/remoteTarballFetcher.ts | Pre-allocates buffer on known `Content-Length` and refactors buffering/copying logic. | | .changeset/undici-perf-optimizations.md | Declares minor releases for the affected packages + pnpm with a summary of perf changes. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. commits.message:
perf: optimize undici HTTP performance

- Enable HTTP/2 multiplexing (allowH2) for HTTPS connections, with
  automatic fallback to HTTP/1.1 via ALPN negotiation
- Enable HTTP/1.1 pipelining (6 concurrent requests per connection)
  for servers that don't support HTTP/2
- Enable Happy Eyeballs (autoSelectFamily) for faster dual-stack
  connection establishment
- Increase keep-alive timeouts (30s idle, 10min max) to reduce
  connection churn during install
- Set optimized global dispatcher so all requests benefit even
  without custom dispatcher options
- Add ALPN protocol list for HTTP/2 negotiation through SOCKS proxies
- Pre-allocate SharedArrayBuffer for tarball downloads when
  Content-Length is known, avoiding intermediate chunk array and
  double-copy overhead

perf: optimize undici HTTP performance

- Enable Happy Eyeballs (autoSelectFamily) for faster dual-stack
  connection establishment
- Increase keep-alive timeouts (30s idle, 10min max) to reduce
  connection churn during install
- Set optimized global dispatcher so all requests benefit even
  without custom dispatcher options
- Pre-allocate SharedArrayBuffer for tarball downloads when
  Content-Length is known, avoiding intermediate chunk array and
  double-copy overhead

Removed HTTP/2 (allowH2) and pipelining after benchmarks showed
they hurt throughput: H2 multiplexing over fewer TCP connections
reduces parallelism vs 50 independent H1.1 connections.

title: fix: auto import mode falls through to hardlinks on ENOTSUP
body:
## Summary

- Fixed a performance regression on Linux where `auto` import mode would copy every file instead of hardlinking on filesystems without reflink support (e.g. ext4)
- The ENOTSUP fallback in `createClonePkg()` (added in #11117) silently converted clone failures to file copies, preventing the auto-importer from falling through to hardlinks
- On ext4 (GitHub Actions, most Linux CI), this caused a **2-9x regression** visible on https://benchmarks.vlt.sh — every file was being copied instead of hardlinked

## Root cause

`createAutoImporter()` probes whether the filesystem supports cloning by importing the first package with `createClonePkg()`. Before #11117, a clone failure (ENOTSUP) propagated up and the auto-importer fell through to hardlinks. After #11117, the `withFallback` wrapper caught ENOTSUP and silently fell back to `resilientCopyFileSync`, so the probe *succeeded* — and all subsequent packages used the "clone" path that actually copies every file.

## Fix

Use a raw clone function (without ENOTSUP fallback) for the auto-mode probe. If the filesystem doesn't support cloning, the error propagates and the auto-importer falls through to hardlinks. Once cloning is confirmed to work, subsequent packages use the full clone importer with ENOTSUP fallback for transient failures under heavy parallel I/O.

## Test plan

- [x] Updated existing test: `auto: ENOTSUP on clone falls through to hardlinks (not copy)` — verifies that on filesystems without reflink support, auto mode uses hardlinks instead of copies
- [ ] CI benchmark: trigger benchmark workflow to confirm the regression is resolved
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes `auto` package import mode on Linux so that a reflink-clone `ENOTSUP` during the *auto-mode probe* correctly falls through to hardlinks (instead of silently copying), restoring expected performance on non-reflink filesystems like ext4. **Changes:** - Updated auto-mode detection to probe cloning using a raw clone function without `ENOTSUP` fallback, so unsupported filesystems fall through to hardlinks. - Introduced `clonePkgRaw()` helper used by `auto` and `clone-or-copy` initial probes, while keeping `createClonePkg()`’s per-file `ENOTSUP` fallback for subsequent imports. - Updated/renamed the regression test to assert `auto` chooses hardlinks when clone throws `ENOTSUP`, and added a changeset entry. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | fs/indexed-pkg-importer/src/index.ts | Uses a raw clone probe to avoid masking unsupported reflinks and incorrectly locking `auto` into the clone path that copies. | | fs/indexed-pkg-importer/test/createImportPackage.test.ts | Regression test now asserts `auto` falls through to hardlinks on clone `ENOTSUP`. | | .changeset/fix-auto-clone-enotsup-fallback.md | Documents the Linux performance regression fix and publishes patch bumps. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

title: fix: recover from parallel dlx install failures using completed cache
body:
## Summary

- When parallel `pnpm dlx` processes install the same package, the shared global virtual store can cause spurious failures (e.g. ENOENT from concurrent directory swaps in `rename-overwrite`)
- Instead of crashing, the failing process now checks if another parallel process has completed and populated the dlx cache in the meantime, and uses that cached installation
- The cache age is validated using the configured `dlx-cache-max-age`

Fixes flaky "parallel dlx calls of the same package" test.

## Test plan

- [x] Existing `parallel dlx calls of the same package` test passes
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Improves `pnpm dlx` robustness when multiple `dlx` processes concurrently install the same package into a shared global virtual store, by falling back to a freshly completed cache entry instead of failing on transient concurrency errors. **Changes:** - Wrap dlx cache preparation + install + link creation in a single `try/catch` block. - On failure, check whether another parallel process has already populated the dlx cache and, if so, use that cached directory (validated via `dlx-cache-max-age`). --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: recover from parallel dlx install failures using completed cache

When parallel dlx processes install the same package, the shared global
virtual store can cause spurious failures (e.g. ENOENT from concurrent
directory swaps). Instead of crashing, check if another parallel process
has completed and populated the dlx cache in the meantime, and use that.

title: feat: add `pnpm pm` prefix to force built-in commands
body:
## Summary

- Added `pnpm pm <command>` syntax that always runs the built-in pnpm command, bypassing any same-named script in `package.json`
- When a project defines a script like `"clean": "rm -rf dist"`, `pnpm clean` runs that script, but `pnpm pm clean` runs the built-in clean command
- This applies to all overridable commands: `clean`, `purge`, `rebuild`, `deploy`, `setup`

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm pm clean` runs built-in even when a `clean` script exists
- [x] `pnpm pm clean` works in workspace subdirectory even when root has a `clean` script (no `SCRIPT_OVERRIDE_IN_WORKSPACE_ROOT` error)
- [x] All existing clean tests continue to pass
- [x] All parse-cli-args tests continue to pass
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Adds a `pnpm pm ` prefix to force running pnpm’s built-in commands (bypassing same-named `package.json` scripts), and wires this through CLI parsing and execution so script-override behavior can be selectively disabled. **Changes:** - Extend `@pnpm/cli.parse-cli-args` to recognize the `pm` prefix and expose a `builtInCommandForced` flag. - Update pnpm’s main command dispatch to skip script-override redirection when `builtInCommandForced` is set. - Add end-to-end tests ensuring `pnpm pm clean` runs the built-in clean in both single-project and workspace-subdir scenarios, plus a changeset. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `pnpm/test/clean.ts` | Adds coverage for `pnpm pm clean` behavior with script overrides and in workspace subdirs. | | `pnpm/src/main.ts` | Uses `builtInCommandForced` to bypass script-override redirection/error logic. | | `cli/parse-cli-args/src/index.ts` | Parses `pm` prefix and returns `builtInCommandForced` to callers. | | `.changeset/pnpm-pm-builtin-prefix.md` | Declares minor bumps and documents the new `pnpm pm ` syntax. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Otherwise the message implies there is no way to run the built-in command from a workspace subdir when the root has a same-named script. ``` if (cmd != null && !builtInCommandForced && overridableByScriptCommands.has(typedCommandName) && !cliOptions.global) { const currentDirManifest = config.dir === config.rootProjectManifestDir ? config.rootProjectManifest : await safeReadProjectManifestOnly(config.dir) if (currentDirManifest?.scripts?.[typedCommandName]) { // Redirect to "pnpm run " cmd = 'run' cliParams.unshift(typedCommandName) fallbackCommandUsed = true config.fallbackCommandUsed = true config.extraEnv = { ...config.extraEnv, npm_command: 'run-script', } } else if ( workspaceDir && config.dir !== config.rootProjectManifestDir && config.rootProjectManifest?.scripts?.[typedCommandName] ) { throw new PnpmError( 'SCRIPT_OVERRIDE_IN_WORKSPACE_ROOT', `The workspace root has a "${typedCommandName}" script, ` + `so the built-in "pnpm ${typedCommandName}" command cannot run from a subdirectory`, { ```

refactor(config): stop shelling out to npm for auth settings

Read and write auth-related settings (registry, tokens, credentials,
scoped registries) directly to INI config files instead of delegating
to `npm config`. Removes the @pnpm/exec.run-npm dependency from
@pnpm/config.commands.

fix(config): give pnpm global rc priority over ~/.npmrc for auth settings

Auth settings from the pnpm global rc file (e.g. ~/.config/pnpm/rc) now
override ~/.npmrc in rawConfig. This ensures tokens written by `pnpm login`
are correctly picked up by `pnpm publish`, since login writes to the pnpm
global rc but ~/.npmrc previously took priority in the npm-conf chain.

chore: remove @pnpm/exec.run-npm package

No longer used after removing npm config CLI delegation.
chore: remove accidentally committed __typecheck__/tsconfig.json

fix(config): narrow non-string rejection to credential keys, add priority test

Non-string value rejection now only applies to credential keys (_auth,
_authToken, _password, username), registry URLs, and scoped/registry-
prefixed keys — not to INI settings like strict-ssl, proxy, or ca that
can legitimately have boolean/null values.

Added a test verifying that auth tokens from the pnpm global rc take
priority over ~/.npmrc.

title: fix(exe): make pn a shell script in the tarball like pnpx/pnx
body:
## Summary

Follow-up to #11144. The previous fix created `pn` as a hardlink in `linkExePlatformBinary`, but that only works when the *installing* version has the fix. During store installs (auto version management), the bootstrap version's code runs — so an older bootstrap without the fix still leaves `pn` as a placeholder.

This makes `pn` a shell script (`exec pnpm "$@"`) written by `prepare.js`, the same approach that already works for `pnpx`/`pnx`. The tarball contains working scripts regardless of which version does the installing.

### Changes

- **`prepare.js`**: `pn` is now a shell script + `.cmd`/`.ps1`, only `pnpm` remains as a placeholder
- **`setup.js`**: only creates hardlink for `pnpm`, no longer touches `pn`
- **`linkExePlatformBinary`**: same — only `pnpm` hardlink, `pn`/`pnpx`/`pnx` come from the tarball

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `linkExePlatformBinary` pass
- [x] E2E test verifies `prepare.js` writes correct content for all bins
- [ ] CI passes
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview This PR aims to make the `pn` executable shipped by `@pnpm/exe` come from the published tarball (as a script wrapper), instead of being created via hardlinks during install, to avoid failures when an older “bootstrap” installer version performs the install (store installs / version management). **Changes:** - Update `prepare.js` to generate `pn` as a wrapper script (plus `pn.cmd`/`pn.ps1`), while keeping only `pnpm` as a placeholder. - Update `setup.js` and `linkExePlatformBinary()` to only hardlink the native `pnpm` binary (stop hardlinking `pn`). - Adjust tests and package file list for the new `pn` wrapper files. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

fix(exe): make pn a shell script in the tarball like pnpx/pnx

The store install path runs the bootstrap version's
linkExePlatformBinary, not the target version's. So the pn hardlink
fix only works when the bootstrap already has it. Making pn a shell
script in the tarball (via prepare.js) means it works regardless of
which version does the installing — same approach as pnpx/pnx.

title: fix(exe): move prepare script to prepublishOnly
body:
## Summary

- The `prepare` lifecycle hook in `@pnpm/exe` runs after install, overwriting the bin files (`pnpm`, `pn`, `pnpx`, `pnx`) that `preinstall` (`setup.js`) just created with placeholder text (`"This file intentionally left blank"`)
- This causes `pnx` to fail with `This: not found` in CI after upgrading to beta.4
- Moves `prepare.js` to run as part of `prepublishOnly` instead, so placeholders are only written during publish preparation

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes (the `compile-only` script can now run `pnx` successfully)

commits.message:
fix(exe): create pn/pnpx/pnx binaries in linkExePlatformBinary

When pnpm auto-manages its version via the `packageManager` field,
it installs @pnpm/exe to the store with scripts disabled. The
`linkExePlatformBinary` function replicates setup.js by linking the
platform binary, but it only created the `pnpm` binary.

The published @pnpm/exe tarball has placeholder files for pn, pnpx,
and pnx (written by prepare.js). Without setup.js running, these
remain as placeholders, causing "This: not found" when invoked.

Create pn (hardlink to native binary) and pnpx/pnx (shell scripts)
in linkExePlatformBinary, matching what setup.js does.
fix(exe): remove unnecessary placeholder writes on Windows
test(exe): verify pn/pnpx/pnx are created by linkExePlatformBinary

test(exe): e2e test that setup.js creates all binaries after prepare.js

Runs prepare.js (simulating publish) then setup.js (simulating install)
and verifies that pnpm and pn are hardlinks to the platform binary,
and pnpx and pnx are executable shell scripts.

Also fixes setup.js to unlink before writing shell scripts, so that
the 0o755 mode is applied even when prepare.js already created the
file with 0o644.
fix: use node: protocol for imports

fix(exe): use shell script aliases for pn instead of hardlinks

pn, like pnpx and pnx, is now a shell script (`exec pnpm "$@"`)
instead of a hardlink to the native binary. This avoids duplicating
the ~100MB binary.

Updated in both setup.js (registry installs) and
linkExePlatformBinary (store installs via version switching).

fix(exe): revert pn back to hardlink, keep pnpx/pnx as shell scripts

Hardlinks have zero overhead and no disk cost (shared inode).
Shell scripts are only needed for pnpx/pnx which inject the dlx arg.
fix(exe): only ignore ENOENT in createShellScript unlink

fix(exe): publish pnpx/pnx with real content instead of placeholders

prepare.js now writes the actual shell scripts for pnpx and pnx
(and their .cmd/.ps1 Windows wrappers) instead of placeholder text.
This means setup.js and linkExePlatformBinary only need to handle
the native binary hardlinks (pnpm, pn) and the Windows bin rewrite.

The published tarball contains the correct pnpx/pnx scripts for all
platforms, so they work even when lifecycle scripts don't run (e.g.
store installs during auto version management).

fix(exe): skip hardlink test when platform binary is unavailable

The platform-specific packages (@pnpm/linux-x64 etc.) are optional
dependencies only available in the @pnpm/exe package, not in CI
test environments. Split the test so prepare.js content verification
always runs, while the setup.js hardlink test skips gracefully.
style: use single quotes in test

title: perf: replace node-fetch with undici
body:
## Summary

Replace `node-fetch` and `@pnpm/network.agent` with undici native `fetch()` and dispatcher system.

### Key changes

- **HTTP client**: Replace `node-fetch` with `undici.fetch()` and `@zkochan/retry` for retries
- **Connection management**: Replace `@pnpm/network.agent` (agentkeepalive) with undici `Agent`/`ProxyAgent`, cached via LRU keyed by connection parameters
- **Proxy support**: HTTP/HTTPS proxies via undici `ProxyAgent`; SOCKS4/SOCKS5 via `socks` package with custom undici connector
- **Client certificates**: Per-registry SSL config matched using nerf-dart URL matching (same algorithm as `@pnpm/network.config`)
- **Test mocking**: Convert from `nock` to undici `MockAgent` across test suites; extract shared helpers into `@pnpm/testing.mock-agent`

### Breaking changes (internal packages only)

- `@pnpm/network.fetch`: `AgentOptions` renamed to `DispatcherOptions`; `fetchWithAgent` renamed to `fetchWithDispatcher`
- `@pnpm/fetching.types`: Removed `node-fetch` `Response` re-export (uses global `Response`)
- `@pnpm/network.agent` catalog dependency removed (replaced by built-in dispatcher module)

### Dependencies

- Added: `undici`, `socks`, `@pnpm/config.nerf-dart`, `lru-cache`
- Removed: `node-fetch`, `@pnpm/network.agent`
- Added override: `minimatch >=9 <10` to `>=10.2.4` (fixes ESM incompatibility with brace-expansion)
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR migrates pnpm’s internal HTTP stack from `node-fetch` + `agentkeepalive`-based agents to `undici`’s native `fetch()` and dispatcher model, updating proxy/cert handling and converting test HTTP mocking from `nock` to `undici`’s `MockAgent`.

**Changes:**
- Replace `node-fetch` usage with `undici.fetch()` and introduce dispatcher selection/caching (incl. proxy + client cert support).
- Update internal APIs/types (`fetchWithAgent` → `fetchWithDispatcher`, `AgentOptions` → `DispatcherOptions`, Response typing changes).
- Convert multiple test suites from `nock` to `MockAgent` and add shared helpers in `@pnpm/testing.mock-agent`.

### Reviewed changes

- Replace node-fetch with undici's fetch() and dispatcher system
- Replace @pnpm/network.agent with a new dispatcher module in @pnpm/network.fetch
- Cache dispatchers via LRU cache keyed by connection parameters
- Handle proxies via undici ProxyAgent instead of http/https-proxy-agent
- Convert test mocking from nock to undici MockAgent where applicable
- Add minimatch@9 override to fix ESM incompatibility with brace-expansion

and getMockAgent helpers. Replace inline copies across 15 test files.

network/fetch keeps its own local copy since it would create a circular
dependency through the shared package.

  instead of weak substring matching (pickSslConfigByUrl)

- Add explicit SOCKS proxy error (undici ProxyAgent doesn't support SOCKS)
- Add localAddress, maxSockets to proxy dispatcher cache key and config
- Add proxy authentication support (Basic auth from proxy URL credentials)
- Add proxyTls options for verifying proxy server certificates

- Implement SOCKS4/SOCKS5 proxy support using the socks package with
  undici Agent's custom connect function. TLS is handled by wrapping
  the SOCKS socket in a tls.connect() call for HTTPS targets.
- Add comprehensive dispatcher unit tests covering:
  - Basic dispatcher creation and caching

  - Real SOCKS5 integration test with a local server

fix: remove unused assert import from tarball fetcher
fix: replace crypto hash with simple fingerprint for proxy cache key (fixes CodeQL alert)

Remove all non-null assertions (!) on getMockAgent() calls by making it
throw if called before setupMockAgent(). This is cleaner than requiring
callers to assert non-null on every use.

Replaces the coverage lost when migrating from nock's badheaders to
MockAgent. Two new tests verify that:

- authorization header is NOT sent when getAuthHeader returns undefined

title: fix: respect frozen-lockfile flag when migrating config dependencies
body:
## What 

When using `--frozen-lockfile`, pnpm should not attempt to modify the `pnpm-workspace.yaml` file. However, when migrating config dependencies from the old inline integrity format to the new lockfile format, the code was always calling `writeSettings()` to update the workspace manifest, regardless of the frozen-lockfile flag.

This caused build failures in Docker and other read-only environments where the workspace manifest file cannot be modified.

## How

- Added `frozenLockfile?` flag to `MigrateOpts` interface
- Added `frozenLockfile?` flag to `InstallConfigDepsOpts` interface
- Updated `migrateConfigDepsToLockfile()` to skip `writeSettings()` when `frozenLockfile` is true
- Updated `resolveConfigDeps()` to skip `writeSettings()` when `frozenLockfile` is true
- Propagated `frozenLockfile` flag through the config loading chain
- Added test case to verify the fix

## Test

- New test `migration with frozen-lockfile: installs from old format without updating workspace manifest` passes
- All existing tests pass
- Build succeeds (pnpm compile)

Resolves #10829
author: kairosci

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR addresses `--frozen-lockfile` behavior during config-deps migration/resolution to avoid writing `pnpm-workspace.yaml` in read-only environments, and also introduces a new native `pnpm view`/`pnpm info` command in the inspection commands set.

**Changes:**
- Propagates a `frozenLockfile?` flag through config-deps install/resolve/migration flows and skips `writeSettings()` when frozen.
- Adds a new `pnpm view` command (with `info` alias) under `@pnpm/deps.inspection.commands`, wiring it into the CLI and adding e2e-style tests.
- Updates workspace references/dependencies (tsconfig refs + lockfile + package.json) to support the new command.

### Reviewed changes

| pnpm/src/cmd/index.ts | Registers the new `view` command in the main CLI command table. |

| installing/env-installer/test/installConfigDeps.ts | Adds a regression test for frozen-lockfile behavior during migration. |

| deps/inspection/commands/tsconfig.json | Adds project references needed by the new view command implementation. |
| deps
There no tests. Also, if we want it to work correctly, it should fail if there is no lockfile or the lockfile is not up to date. All scenarios should be covered with tests.

title: feat!: use cleaner output for script execution
body:
## Summary

**Breaking change:** Changes the output format when running scripts.

- Print `$ command` (dimmed) instead of `> pkg@version stage path` + `> command`
- Output the `$ command` line to **stderr** instead of stdout (matching bun's behavior), keeping stdout clean for piping
- Update `@pnpm/npm-lifecycle` to `1100.0.0-1` which removed the old header output

Before:
```
> test@1.0.0 foo /private/tmp/test
> echo "foo" && pnpm run bar

foo

> test@1.0.0 bar /private/tmp/test
> echo "bar"

bar
```

After:
```
$ echo "foo" && pnpm run bar
foo
$ echo "bar"
bar
```

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm run` e2e tests pass (18/18)
- [x] Verified `$ command` goes to stderr, script output goes to stdout
author: zkochan

- When a project's `package.json` has a script named `clean`, `setup`, or `deploy`, running `pnpm clean/setup/deploy` now executes the script instead of the built-in command

title: feat: add native view/info/show/v command
body:
## What

Added a native `pnpm view` command (with `info` alias) to fetch package information from the npm registry without using npm CLI. This is the inspection command equivalent of `npm view`.

## Why

- Provides a native pnpm command for registry queries
- Replaces npm CLI dependency for viewing package information
- Follows pnpm's architecture: inspection commands belong in `deps/inspection/commands`
- Aligns with pnpm naming conventions (`view` with `info` alias, similar to `npm view`)
- Reduces external tool dependencies

## How

- Implemented in `deps/inspection/commands/src/view/` (proper location for inspection commands)
- Uses native Node.js fetch API for registry communication
- Integrates with pnpm's registry selection and package parsing utilities
- Supports both text and JSON output formats
- Provides `view` as primary command with `info` as an alias

## Testing

The command can be tested with:

```bash
# View latest version
pnpm view lodash

# View specific version
pnpm view react@18.0.0

# Using info alias
pnpm info lodash

# JSON output
pnpm view lodash --json
```

## Related

Addresses feedback from maintainers regarding proper command positioning and naming conventions in the pnpm architecture.
author: kairosci

reviews.body:

title: fix: remove --workspace flag from version command
body:
## Summary
- Removed `--workspace` and `--workspaces` flags from the `version` command — these don't follow pnpm conventions
- Recursive versioning now only activates with the explicit `--recursive` (`-r`) flag, not when run from workspace root
- Fixed help description to reflect that the command works for single packages too

## Test plan
- [x] Existing version command tests pass
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview Removes non-standard workspace flags from the `pnpm version` command and makes workspace-wide version bumps opt-in via `--recursive`, aligning the command’s behavior and help output with pnpm’s recursive conventions. **Changes:** - Removed `--workspace` / `--workspaces` flags from `version` CLI option types and handler options. - Made recursive/workspace versioning run only when `--recursive` is explicitly set (no implicit workspace-root behavior). - Updated help description text to reflect single-package usage.
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **releasing/commands/src/version/index.ts:65** * The help text still advertises `--filter `, but in the current implementation filtering is only applied in the `opts.recursive` branch. With the new behavior (no implicit workspace mode at workspace root), `pnpm version --filter ...` will silently ignore the filter unless `--recursive` is also set. Consider either (a) updating the option description to explicitly say it requires `--recursive`, or (b) throwing a user-facing error when `--filter` is provided without `--recursive` to avoid surprising partial version bumps. ``` { description: 'Filter packages by name (glob pattern)', name: '--filter ', }, ```
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: remove --workspace flag from version command, use only --recursive

The --workspace/--workspaces flags were incorrectly added as synonyms for
--recursive in the version command. In pnpm, --recursive (-r) is the
standard convention for applying commands across workspace packages.
Recursive versioning now only activates with the explicit -r flag.

test: improve version command test coverage

Add tests for major/patch bumps, --json output, --allow-same-version,
invalid version handling, missing name/version, empty params, and
recursive mode with workspace packages including JSON output, skipping
unnamed packages, and verifying --recursive is required.

test: use expect().rejects instead of try/catch in version tests

Avoids silent passes when the handler doesn't throw.

title: fix: stop setting npm_config_ env vars during lifecycle scripts
body:
## Summary

- Update `@pnpm/npm-lifecycle` to `1100.0.0-0` which no longer dumps the entire pnpm config as `npm_config_*` environment variables during lifecycle scripts
- This fixes `npm warn Unknown env config` warnings (e.g. for `allowbuilds`, `shared-workspace-lockfile`, `resolution-mode`, `patches-dir`, `catalog`, etc.) when lifecycle scripts invoke npm internally
- Only well-known `npm_*` env vars are now set (`npm_lifecycle_event`, `npm_config_node_gyp`, `npm_config_user_agent`, `npm_package_*`, etc.), matching Yarn's behavior
- Remove the `@pnpm/npm-lifecycle` patch (the `process.argv[1]` fix is now in the upstream release)

## Test plan

- [x] Updated lifecycle test to verify `npm_config_*` vars from pnpm config are no longer set
- [x] All 8 `@pnpm/exec.lifecycle` tests pass
author: zkochan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview Updates lifecycle script execution to avoid exporting pnpm config as `npm_config_*` environment variables, reducing noise when scripts invoke npm. **Changes:** - Bump `@pnpm/npm-lifecycle` to `1100.0.0-0` and remove the local patch configuration. - Update `runLifecycleHook()` to use the new `@pnpm/npm-lifecycle` API and stop passing pnpm config into lifecycle env. - Adjust lifecycle tests + local typings, and add a changeset documenting the behavior change. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `pnpm-workspace.yaml` | Pins `@pnpm/npm-lifecycle` to `1100.0.0-0` and removes the patched dependency entry. | | `pnpm-lock.yaml` | Reflects the new resolved `@pnpm/npm-lifecycle` version and removal of the patch hash. | | `exec/lifecycle/src/runLifecycleHook.ts` | Switches to named import and passes empty `config` to avoid `npm_config_*` export. | | `exec/lifecycle/test/index.ts` | Updates the lifecycle test assertion to ensure `npm_config_*` vars aren’t set. | | `__typings__/local.d.ts` | Replaces `export = any` with explicit `lifecycle`/`makeEnv` declarations. | | `.changeset/stop-npm-config-env-vars.md` | Documents the behavior change for releases. |
Files not reviewed (1) * **pnpm-lock.yaml**: Language not supported
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: stop setting npm_config_ env vars from pnpm config during lifecycle scripts

Update @pnpm/npm-lifecycle to 1100.0.0-0 which no longer dumps the
entire pnpm config as npm_config_* environment variables. This fixes
npm warnings about unknown config when lifecycle scripts invoke npm.

Only well-known npm_* env vars are now set, matching Yarn's behavior.
fix: fix spellcheck in changeset
chore: remove obsolete @pnpm/npm-lifecycle patch file

fix: pass npm_config_user_agent via extraEnv in lifecycle scripts

The npm-lifecycle makeEnv() strips all npm_* vars from process.env,
so npm_config_user_agent must be explicitly passed via extraEnv.
chore: mark changeset as major (breaking change)

title: fix: handle ENOTSUP in clone import path during parallel I/O
body:
## Summary
- The `resilientCopyFileSync` fix (#11103) only covered the hardlink and copy import paths
- The clone path (`COPYFILE_FICLONE_FORCE`) in `createCloneFunction()` was missed — it only caught `EEXIST`, not `ENOTSUP`
- When `auto` mode locks onto clone after the first successful package, a transient `ENOTSUP` under heavy parallel I/O (e.g. parallel dlx calls) goes unhandled
- Added `ENOTSUP` fallback to `resilientCopyFileSync` in the clone function

## Test plan
- [ ] Existing `createImportPackage` unit tests pass
- [ ] CI "parallel dlx calls of the same package" test passes
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes a missing `ENOTSUP` fallback in the **clone** import path (`COPYFILE_FICLONE_FORCE`) used by the indexed package importer, addressing transient failures under heavy parallel I/O (e.g. parallel `dlx` calls) after `auto` mode has selected cloning. **Changes:** - Extend `createCloneFunction()` to catch `ENOTSUP` and fall back to `resilientCopyFileSync()` (in addition to the existing `EEXIST` handling). --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
