commits.message:
fix: handle ENOTSUP in clone import path during parallel I/O

The resilientCopyFileSync fix only covered hardlink and copy paths.
The clone path (COPYFILE_FICLONE_FORCE) was missed, so transient
ENOTSUP under heavy parallel I/O still caused failures.

fix: make ENOTSUP clone fallback atomic for package.json

The clone function is used as both importFile and importFileAtomic.
Reflinks are inherently atomic, but the ENOTSUP fallback used
resilientCopyFileSync which is not atomic. Since package.json is the
completion marker, a partial write could make a half-imported package
appear complete. Split into two wrappers: createCloneWithFallback
(regular copy) for normal files and createAtomicCloneWithFallback
(temp+rename) for package.json.
refactor: deduplicate ENOTSUP clone fallback into createCloneImporter
style: fix import sort order
docs: restore comment about reflink atomicity in createCloneImporter
style: convert createCloneImporter comment to JSDoc
test: add ENOTSUP clone fallback test with atomic package.json write
fix: spelling in test comment
refactor: create clone Importer once and reuse across packages
refactor: inline createCloneFunction into createCloneImporter
fix: remove unknown word from JSDoc comment
refactor: merge clonePkg and createCloneImporter into createClonePkg
test: add ENOTSUP clone fallback test for explicit clone mode

title: feat: `pnpm login`
body:
Closes <https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/11055>.
Closes <https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/pull/11093>.
author: KSXGitHub

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR adds a first-class `pnpm login` (and `adduser` alias) command to the pnpm CLI, and extracts reusable web-auth/OTP polling utilities into a new `@pnpm/network.web-auth` package that is also reused by `pnpm publish` OTP handling.

**Changes:**
- Introduces `@pnpm/auth.commands` with `login`/`adduser` support (web login with QR + fallback to classic username/password, plus OTP handling for classic flow).
- Introduces `@pnpm/network.web-auth` (QR generation, token polling, OTP wrapper + timeout error) and refactors publish OTP logic to use it.
- Wires the new command into the CLI, updates workspace configuration, and adds changesets.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 32 out of 34 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| releasing/commands/tsconfig.json | Adds TS project reference to the new `network/web-auth` package. |
| releasing/commands/test/publish/otp.test.ts | Updates OTP publish tests to use the extracted web-auth types and asserts info logging behavior. |
| releasing/commands/src/publish/utils/shared-context.ts | Adds `globalWarn` to shared publish context to satisfy new OTP context requirements. |
| releasing/commands/src/publish/otp.ts | Replaces inlined OTP/webauth polling logic with `withOtpHandling()` from `@pnpm/network.web-auth`. |
| releasing/commands/package.json | Depends on `@pnpm/network.web-auth` and drops direct `qrcode-terminal` deps. |
| pnpm/tsconfig.json | Adds TS project reference to `auth/commands`. |
| pnpm/src/cmd/notImplemented.ts | Removes `login` and `adduser` from the not-implemented list. |
| pnpm/src/cmd/index.ts | Registers the new `login` command definition from `@pnpm/auth.commands`. |
| pnpm/package.json | Adds `@pnpm/auth.commands` to the pnpm package’s workspace deps. |
| pnpm-workspace.yaml | Includes `auth/*` in the workspace packages list. |
| pnpm-lock

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 32 out of 34 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

commits.message:
refactor: extract web auth QR code and polling into @pnpm/network.web-auth

Extract generateQrCode() and pollForWebAuthToken() from releasing/commands
into a new shared package so that both `pnpm publish` and the upcoming
`pnpm login` can reuse the web-based authentication flow with QR code
display and doneUrl polling.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01YHYqGAAmZ1a9XMWoV7nG4S

feat: implement `pnpm login` command

Add `pnpm login` (and `pnpm adduser` alias) for authenticating with npm
registries. The command:

- Tries web-based login first (POST /-/v1/login), displaying a QR code
  and polling for the token using @pnpm/network.web-auth
- Falls back to classic username/password/email login (PUT /-/user/
  org.couchdb.user:<username>) when web login is not supported (404/405)
- Saves the received auth token to the user's global rc file

Also fixes a tsgo build issue in releasing/commands where
OtpWebAuthFetchOptions was used as a local type alias but was only
available as a re-exported name.

  any operation with EOTP challenge detection, web auth flow, and
  classic OTP prompting.
- Refactor `publishWithOtpHandling` to delegate to the shared function.
- Add OTP handling to `pnpm login`'s classic (CouchDB) login flow:
  detects 401 + `www-authenticate: otp` header and retries with the
  OTP code (or web auth token) in the `npm-otp` header.
- Remove overly strict `this: this` constraints from WebAuthFetchResponse
  interfaces to improve cross-package type compatibility.
- Add 13 unit tests for `withOtpHandling` (classic + webauth flows).

being that they shall all be discarded anyway.
refactor: remove unnecessary re-assignment
test: remove unnecessary assertion

refactor: make default globalInfo and globalWarn mocks throw on unexpected calls

Replace no-op defaults with throwing mocks in createOtpMockContext
and createMockContext. Tests that expect these to be called now
explicitly override them.

title: feat(auth): prepend 'Bearer' to auth token generated by tokenHelper
body:
**The Problem:**
Currently, pnpm uses the raw standard output of the `tokenHelper` executable as the target `Authorization` header. However, `npm` expects the helper to return only the raw token string and automatically prepends `Bearer ` internally. This inconsistency causes standard npm token helpers to fail authentication in pnpm with malformed, schemeless authorization headers.

**The Fix:**
This PR normalizes the `tokenHelper` output by natively appending `Bearer ` to the returned token string, restoring drop-in compatibility with `npm`. 

To remain entirely backwards-compatible and avoid breaking users who may have modified their scripts to explicitly output `"Bearer "` to work around this issue, the logic safely checks whether the string already begins with a known scheme prior to prefixing.
author: Karthikeya1500

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR aligns pnpm’s auth header generation with npm’s token helper behavior by ensuring token helper output is normalized to a proper `Authorization` header value, and fixes default-registry `_password` handling to match scoped-registry behavior.

**Changes:**
- Decode default-registry `_password` from base64 before forming the `Basic` auth header.
- Prefix `Bearer ` to token helper stdout when it doesn’t already contain an auth scheme.
- Add changesets for both the token helper normalization and `_password` decoding fix.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| network/auth-header/src/getAuthHeadersFromConfig.ts | Decodes `_password` for default registry; normalizes token helper output by adding `Bearer ` when missing. |
| network/auth-header/test/getAuthHeadersFromConfig.test.ts | Updates default-registry username/password test data to reflect base64 `_password`. |
| .changeset/fix-token-helper-bearer-prefix.md | Changeset entry for token helper `Bearer` prefix behavior. |
| .changeset/fix-default-registry-password-decode.md | Changeset entry for base64-decoding `_password` for the default registry. |


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (3)</summary>

**network/auth-header/src/getAuthHeadersFromConfig.ts:73**
* `loadToken()` will return `Bearer ` (a schemeless/empty credential) when the token helper prints nothing (or only whitespace). That changes prior behavior (empty string) and will lead to invalid Authorization headers that are hard to debug. Consider validating that `token.trim()` is non-empty and throwing a dedicated error when the helper output is empty.
```
    throw new PnpmError('BAD_TOKEN_HELPER_PATH', `${settingName} must be an absolute path, without arguments`)
  }
```
**network/auth-header/src/getAuthHeadersFromConfig.ts:73**
* The scheme detection here only preserves outputs

commits.message: fix(auth-header): decode _password from base64 for default registry auth
fix(auth): prepend 'Bearer ' to auth token generated by tokenHelper
chore: merge upstream main
test: skip flaky parallel dlx test on Node 25

fix(auth): improve tokenHelper Bearer prefix with validation and generic scheme detection

- Throw an error when the token helper returns an empty token instead of
  producing an invalid "Bearer " header
- Use a generic auth scheme regex instead of hardcoding only Bearer/Basic,
  so other schemes (Token, Negotiate, etc.) are preserved as-is
- Add tests for raw token prefixing, existing scheme preservation, and
  empty token error

title: fix(lockfile): handle non-semver versions in lockfile merger without crashing
body:
When merging lockfiles, if two branches have different non-semver dependency versions (e.g. `link:../path1` vs `link:../path2`), the `mergeVersions` function crashes with `TypeError: Invalid Version` because `semver.gt()` cannot parse these strings.

This wraps the `semver.gt()` call in a try-catch so non-semver versions fall through gracefully, preferring the incoming change (consistent with the existing `takeChangedValue` strategy).

Adds a regression test for `link:` protocol version merging.
author: vamsi2246

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes a lockfile merger crash caused by `semver.gt()` throwing on non-semver dependency version strings (e.g. `link:`), ensuring merges complete successfully and follow the existing “prefer incoming change” behavior when versions can’t be semver-compared. **Changes:** - Wrap `semver.gt()` in `mergeVersions` to gracefully handle non-semver version strings. - Add a regression test covering `link:` protocol dependency version merging. - Add a changeset to ship the fix as a patch release. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | lockfile/merger/src/index.ts | Prevents crashes when comparing non-semver dependency versions during merge. | | lockfile/merger/test/index.ts | Adds regression coverage to ensure `link:` version merges don’t throw and prefer incoming. | | .changeset/fix-lockfile-merger-non-semver.md | Declares patch release notes for the fix. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

title: fix(importer): handle ENOTSUP in linkOrCopy during parallel imports
body:
## Summary
- On Linux CI (GitHub Actions), `copyFileSync` can transiently fail with `ENOTSUP` when `copy_file_range`/`sendfile` hits contention during parallel imports of the same CAS store files
- Adds a manual `readFileSync` + `writeFileSync` fallback in `linkOrCopy` when `copyFileSync` throws `ENOTSUP`
- Fixes the flaky "parallel dlx calls of the same package" test

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify existing tests pass (`pnpm --filter @pnpm/fs.indexed-pkg-importer test`)
- [ ] Confirm the "parallel dlx calls of the same package" test no longer flakes on Linux CI
author: zkochan

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR hardens the indexed package importer’s hardlink fallback path by handling a transient Linux `ENOTSUP` failure from `copyFileSync` during highly-parallel imports, aiming to eliminate CI flakiness (notably the “parallel dlx calls of the same package” scenario). **Changes:** - Wraps `fs.copyFileSync()` in `linkOrCopy()` with a `try/catch`. - Adds an `ENOTSUP`-specific fallback that copies via `readFileSync` + `writeFileSync`. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix(importer): handle ENOTSUP error in linkOrCopy during parallel imports

On Linux CI, copy_file_range/sendfile can transiently fail with ENOTSUP
under heavy parallel I/O on the same CAS store files. Fall back to
manual read+write when copyFileSync hits this error.

fix(importer): preserve file mode in ENOTSUP fallback and add tests

Address review feedback:
- Preserve source file permissions (mode) when falling back to
  read+write on ENOTSUP
- Add tests for the ENOTSUP fallback path and for rethrow of
  non-ENOTSUP errors

fix(importer): handle ENOTSUP in all copyFileSync paths

The previous fix only handled ENOTSUP in linkOrCopy, but the error can
occur in any code path that calls copyFileSync: the copy import method,
atomicCopyFileSync, and the clone function.

Extract resilientCopyFileSync that falls back to read+write when
copy_file_range/sendfile fails with ENOTSUP, and use it in all paths:
- linkOrCopy (hardlink fallback)
- copyPkg (copy import method)
- atomicCopyFileSync (package.json completion marker)
- createCloneFunction (tolerate ENOTSUP alongside EEXIST)

fix(importer): don't swallow ENOTSUP in clone function

ENOTSUP from COPYFILE_FICLONE_FORCE means "reflinks not supported" and
must propagate so the auto importer falls through to hardlink. Only the
regular copyFileSync path (resilientCopyFileSync) should handle ENOTSUP
as a transient copy_file_range failure.

The previous commit incorrectly tolerated ENOTSUP in the clone function,
causing it to silently skip files and produce empty directories.

title: fix(link-bins): skip relinking bins that already point at the correct target
body:
## Summary

`linkBin()` unconditionally calls `cmdShim()` / `symlinkDir()` even when the target bin already points at the correct path. This causes redundant I/O on repeated installs and `EACCES` failures when the bin directory lives on a read-only filesystem (Docker layer caching, CI prewarm, NFS mounts).

This PR adds a check at the top of `linkBin()` that verifies the existing bin before skipping:

- **Symlinks**: `readlink` target is compared against `cmd.path`
- **Cmd-shim files**: checked via `isShimPointingAt()` from `@zkochan/cmd-shim` v9, which embeds a `# cmd-shim-target=<path>` marker in every generated sh shim
- Files larger than 4KB (binaries) are never skipped — they are not cmd-shims

Stale or incorrect bins (wrong target, missing marker, different provider) are always rewritten.

Follows up on feedback from #11020.

## Changes

- `bins/linker/src/index.ts` — add target verification check in `linkBin()`
- `bins/linker/test/index.ts` — tests for skip and rewrite behavior
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` — upgrade `@zkochan/cmd-shim` to v9

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm --filter @pnpm/bins.linker test` passes
- [x] `pnpm run lint` passes
- [x] New test verifies bin content is preserved when target matches
- [x] New test verifies bins without marker are rewritten
author: vsumner

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR aims to make bin-linking idempotent by skipping work in `linkBin()` when the target bin already exists, reducing redundant I/O and avoiding `EACCES` on read-only/warm store scenarios. **Changes:** - Add a top-of-function `fs.stat()` existence check in `linkBin()` to skip creating shims/symlinks when a bin path already exists. - Add a unit test asserting that an existing bin file is not rewritten on a second `linkBins()` call. - Add a changeset bumping `@pnpm/bins.linker` and `pnpm` with a patch release note. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `bins/linker/src/index.ts` | Adds an early-return existence check to avoid rewriting already-present bin artifacts. | | `bins/linker/test/index.ts` | Adds a regression test for “do not rewrite existing bin files”. | | `.changeset/skip-existing-bin-files.md` | Declares patch releases and documents the behavior change. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 4 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Files not reviewed (1) * **pnpm-lock.yaml**: Language not supported
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message: fix(link-bins): skip linking bins that already exist

fix(link-bins): verify target path before skipping existing bins

Address review feedback: instead of a bare existence check, verify the
existing bin actually references the correct target path. For symlinks,
compare readlink against cmd.path. For cmd-shims, check the file
content includes the expected relative path. This ensures conflict
resolution changes or provider swaps still get bins rewritten.

Add test proving stale bins referencing a wrong package are replaced.

fix(link-bins): use cmd-shim target marker for skip check

- Upgrade @zkochan/cmd-shim to v9 which embeds a `# cmd-shim-target=`
  marker in every sh shim and exports `isShimPointingAt()` to check it
- Use `isShimPointingAt()` instead of fragile `path.relative` content
  matching to verify existing shims point at the correct target
- Remove the `!IS_WINDOWS` guard — the sh shim (with marker) is created
  on all platforms, so the skip optimization now works everywhere
test: rename test to reflect marker-based skip check

fix(link-bins): exclude node from file-based skip check

The `node` bin on non-Windows must be a direct symlink, not a cmd-shim.
Skip the file content check for `node` so a stale shim is always
replaced with the correct symlink. The symlink-based skip still applies.

fix(link-bins): use file size check instead of node special case

Skip the file content check for files larger than 4KB, which cannot
be cmd-shims. This avoids reading binaries and removes the need to
special-case `node` by name.
